1.author: Ahmed Ali
1.text: TA SIN. THESE are the verses of the Qur'an, and collection of explicit laws,

1.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
1.text: Ta-Seen*; these are verses of the Qur’an and the clear Book. (Alphabets of the Arabic language – Allah, and to whomever he reveals, know their precise meanings.)

1.author: Arberry
1.text: Ta Sin. Those are the signs of the Koran and a Manifest Book,

1.author: Daryabadi
1.text: Ta. Sin. These are the verses of the Qur'an and a Book luminous.

1.author: Hilali & Khan
1.text: Ta-Sin. [These letters are one of the miracles of the Quran, and none but Allah (Alone) knows their meanings]. These are the Verses of the Quran, and (it is) a Book (that makes things) clear;

1.author: Itani
1.text: Ta, Seen. These are the Signs of the Quran—a book that makes things clear.

1.author: Maududi
1.text: Ta'. Sin. These are the verses of the Qur'an and a Clear Book;

1.author: Mubarakpuri
1.text: Ta Sin. These are the Ayat of the Qur'an, and (it is) a Book (that is) clear.

1.author: Pickthall
1.text: Ta. Sin. These are revelations of the Qur'an and a Scripture that maketh plain;

1.author: Qarai
1.text: Ta, Seen. These are the signs of the Quran and a manifest Book,

1.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
1.text: TaSeen. Those are the verses of the Koran, a clear Book,

1.author: Saheeh International
1.text: Ta, Seen. These are the verses of the Qur'an and a clear Book

1.author: Sarwar
1.text: Ta. Sin. These are the verses of the Quran and of the illustrious Book

1.author: Shakir
1.text: Ta Sin! These are the verses of the Quran and the Book that makes (things) clear

1.author: Wahiduddin Khan
1.text: Ta Sin. These are verses from the Quran, a book that makes things clear;

1.author: Yusuf Ali
1.text: These are verses of the Qur'an,-a book that makes (things) clear;

1.author: Transliteration
1.text: Taseen tilka ayatu alqurani wakitabin mubeenin

2.author: Ahmed Ali
2.text: A guidance and good tidings for the believers,

2.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
2.text: A guidance and glad tidings for the believers.

2.author: Arberry
2.text: a guidance, and good tidings unto the believers

2.author: Daryabadi
2.text: A guidance and glad tidings unto the believers.

2.author: Hilali & Khan
2.text: A guide (to the Right Path); and glad tidings for the believers [who believe in the Oneness of Allah (i.e. Islamic Monotheism)].

2.author: Itani
2.text: Guidance and good news for the believers.

2.author: Maududi
2.text: a guidance and good tidings for the believers

2.author: Mubarakpuri
2.text: A guide and glad tidings for the believers.

2.author: Pickthall
2.text: A guidance and good tidings for believers

2.author: Qarai
2.text: a guidance and good news for the faithful

2.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
2.text: a guidance and glad tidings to believers,

2.author: Saheeh International
2.text: As guidance and good tidings for the believers

2.author: Sarwar
2.text: They are glad news and guidance for the believers

2.author: Shakir
2.text: A guidance and good news for the believers,

2.author: Wahiduddin Khan
2.text: it is guidance and good news for the believers

2.author: Yusuf Ali
2.text: A guide: and glad tidings for the believers,-

3.author: Ahmed Ali
3.text: Who fulfil their devotional obligations, pay the zakat, and believe with certainty in the life to come.

3.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
3.text: Those who keep the prayer established and pay the charity and are certain of the Hereafter.

3.author: Arberry
3.text: who perform the prayer, and pay the alms, and have sure faith in the Hereafter.

3.author: Daryabadi
3.text: Who establish the prayer and give the poor-rate, and of the Hereafter they are convinced.

3.author: Hilali & Khan
3.text: Those who perform As-Salat (Iqamat-as-Salat) and give Zakat and they believe with certainty in the Hereafter (resurrection, recompense of their good and bad deeds, Paradise and Hell, etc.).

3.author: Itani
3.text: Those who observe the prayers, and give charity regularly, and are certain of the Hereafter.

3.author: Maududi
3.text: who establish Prayer and give Zakah, and have firm faith in the Hereafter.

3.author: Mubarakpuri
3.text: Those who perform the Salah and give the Zakah and they believe with certainty in the Hereafter.

3.author: Pickthall
3.text: Who establish worship and pay the poor-due and are sure of the Hereafter.

3.author: Qarai
3.text: —those who maintain the prayer and pay the zakat, and who are certain of the Hereafter.

3.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
3.text: who establish their prayers, and pay the obligatory charity, and are certain of the Everlasting Life.

3.author: Saheeh International
3.text: Who establish prayer and give zakah, and of the Hereafter they are certain [in faith].

3.author: Sarwar
3.text: who are steadfast in prayer, who pay the religious tax, and who have strong faith in the life hereafter.

3.author: Shakir
3.text: Who keep up prayer and pay the poor-rate, and of the hereafter, they are sure.

3.author: Wahiduddin Khan
3.text: who pray regularly and pay obligatory alms and have firm faith in the Hereafter.

3.author: Yusuf Ali
3.text: Those who establish regular prayers and give in regular charity, and also have (full) assurance of the hereafter.

3.author: Transliteration
3.text: Allatheena yuqeemoona alssalata wayutoona alzzakata wahum bialakhirati hum yooqinoona

4.author: Ahmed Ali
4.text: We make their deeds attractive to those who do not believe in the Hereafter, so that they may wander in perplexity.

4.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
4.text: Those who do not believe in the Hereafter – We have made their deeds seem good to them, so they are astray.

4.author: Arberry
4.text: Those who believe not in the Hereafter, We have decked out fair for them their works, and they wander blindly;

4.author: Daryabadi
4.text: Verily those who believe not in the Hereafter - fairseeming unto them We have made their works, so that they wander perplexed.

4.author: Hilali & Khan
4.text: Verily, those who believe not in the Hereafter, We have made their deeds fair-seeming to them, so they wander about blindly.

4.author: Itani
4.text: As for those who do not believe in the Hereafter: We made their deeds appear good to them, so they wander aimlessly.

4.author: Maududi
4.text: As for those who do not believe in the Hereafter, We have made their deeds seem attractive to them so they stumble around in perplexity.

4.author: Mubarakpuri
4.text: Verily, those who believe not in the Hereafter, We have made their deeds fair seeming to them, so that they wander about blindly.

4.author: Pickthall
4.text: Lo! as for those who believe not in the Hereafter, We have made their works fairseeming unto them so that they are all astray.

4.author: Qarai
4.text: As for those who do not believe in the Hereafter, We have made their deeds seem decorous to them, and so they are bewildered.

4.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
4.text: As for those who do not believe in the Everlasting Life, We have decorated for them their works, and they wander blindly.

4.author: Saheeh International
4.text: Indeed, for those who do not believe in the Hereafter, We have made pleasing to them their deeds, so they wander blindly.

4.author: Sarwar
4.text: We have made the deeds of those who do not believe in the life to come, attractive to them and they wander about blindly.

4.author: Shakir
4.text: As to those who do not believe in the hereafter, We have surely made their deeds fair-seeming to them, but they blindly wander on.

4.author: Wahiduddin Khan
4.text: We have made those who do not believe in the Hereafter feel their actions appear good to them, so they wander blindly:

4.author: Yusuf Ali
4.text: As to those who believe not in the Hereafter, We have made their deeds pleasing in their eyes; and so they wander about in distraction.

4.author: Transliteration
4.text: Inna allatheena la yuminoona bialakhirati zayyanna lahum aAAmalahum fahum yaAAmahoona

5.author: Ahmed Ali
5.text: They are those who will suffer the worst chastisement in this life, and will be the greatest losers in the life to come.

5.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
5.text: It is they for whom is the worst punishment, and they will be the greatest losers in the Hereafter.

5.author: Arberry
5.text: those are they whom an evil chastisement awaits, and they will be the greatest losers in the Hereafter.

5.author: Daryabadi
5.text: Those are they for whom shall be an evil torment. And in the Hereafter they shall be the greatest losers.

5.author: Hilali & Khan
5.text: They are those for whom there will be an evil torment (in this world). And in the Hereafter they will be the greatest losers.

5.author: Itani
5.text: It is they who will receive the grievous punishment—and in the Hereafter they will be the greatest losers.

5.author: Maududi
5.text: It is they for whom a grievous chastisement lies in store; it is they who shall be the greatest losers in the Hereafter.

5.author: Mubarakpuri
5.text: They are those for whom there will be an evil torment. And in the Hereafter they will be the greatest losers.

5.author: Pickthall
5.text: Those are they for whom is the worst of punishment, and in the Hereafter they will be the greatest losers.

5.author: Qarai
5.text: They are the ones for whom there is a terrible punishment, and they are the ones who will be the biggest losers in the Hereafter.

5.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
5.text: Such are those for whom there is an evil punishment, and in the Everlasting Life are the greatest losers.

5.author: Saheeh International
5.text: Those are the ones for whom there will be the worst of punishment, and in the Hereafter they are the greatest losers.

5.author: Sarwar
5.text: They will suffer the worst kind of torment and will be lost in the life to come.

5.author: Shakir
5.text: These are they who shall have an evil punishment, and in the hereafter they shall be the greatest losers.

5.author: Wahiduddin Khan
5.text: they are the ones who will have the worst of punishment, and in the Hereafter they will be the greatest losers.

5.author: Yusuf Ali
5.text: Such are they for whom a grievous Penalty is (waiting); and in the Hereafter theirs will be the greatest loss.

5.author: Transliteration
5.text: Olaika allatheena lahum sooo alAAathabi wahum fee alakhirati humu alakhsaroona

6.author: Ahmed Ali
6.text: You have been conveyed the Qur'an from One all-wise and all-knowing.

6.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
6.text: And indeed you (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him), are taught the Qur’an from the Wise, the All Knowing.

6.author: Arberry
6.text: Thou receivest the Koran from One All-wise, All-knowing.

6.author: Daryabadi
6.text: And verily thou art receiving the Qur'an) from before the Wise, the Knowing.

6.author: Hilali & Khan
6.text: And verily, you (O Muhammad SAW) are receiving the Quran from the One, All-Wise, All-Knowing.

6.author: Itani
6.text: You are receiving the Quran from an All-Wise, All-Knowing.

6.author: Maududi
6.text: As for you, (O Muhammad), you are receiving the Qur'an from the Most Wise, the All-Knowing.

6.author: Mubarakpuri
6.text: And verily, you are being taught the Qur'an from One, All-Wise, All-Knowing.

6.author: Pickthall
6.text: Lo! as for thee (Muhammad), thou verily receivest the Qur'an from the presence of One Wise, Aware.

6.author: Qarai
6.text: Indeed you receive the Quran from One who is all-wise, all-knowing.

6.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
6.text: You have received the Koran from the Wise, the Knower.

6.author: Saheeh International
6.text: And indeed, [O Muhammad], you receive the Qur'an from one Wise and Knowing.

6.author: Sarwar
6.text: (Muhammad), you have certainly received the Quran from the All-wise and All-knowing One.

6.author: Shakir
6.text: And most surely you are made to receive the Quran from the Wise, the Knowing Allah.

6.author: Wahiduddin Khan
6.text: You have received this Quran from One who is all-wise, all-knowing.

6.author: Yusuf Ali
6.text: As to thee, the Qur'an is bestowed upon thee from the presence of one who is wise and all-knowing.

6.author: Transliteration
6.text: Wainnaka latulaqqa alqurana min ladun hakeemin AAaleemin

7.author: Ahmed Ali
7.text: (Remember) when Moses said to his family: "I see a fire. I shall bring you news from it, or bring an ember that you may warm yourselves."

7.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
7.text: (Remember) when Moosa said to his wife, “I have sighted a fire; I will soon bring its news to you, or bring for you an ember from it so that you may warm yourselves.”

7.author: Arberry
7.text: When Moses said to his people 'I observe a fire, and will bring you news of it, or I will bring you a flaming brand, that haply you shall warm yourselves.'

7.author: Daryabadi
7.text: Recall what time Musa said unto his household: verily I perceive a fire afar; I shall forthwith bring you tidings thereof, or bring unto you a brand lighted therefrom, haply ye may warm yourselves.

7.author: Hilali & Khan
7.text: (Remember) when Musa (Moses) said to his household: "Verily! I have seen a fire, I will bring you from there some information, or I will bring you a burning brand, that you may warm yourselves."

7.author: Itani
7.text: When Moses said to his family, “I have glimpsed a fire. I will bring you some news from it; or bring you a firebrand, that you may warm yourselves.”

7.author: Maududi
7.text: (Recount to them) when Moses said to his family: "I perceive something like fire: soon will I bring to you some information from there, or I will bring you a burning brand that you may warm yourselves."

7.author: Mubarakpuri
7.text: (Remember) when Musa said to his household: "Verily, I have seen a fire; I will bring you from there some information, or I will bring you a burning ember, that you may warm yourselves."

7.author: Pickthall
7.text: (Remember) when Moses said unto his household: Lo! I spy afar off a fire; I will bring you tidings thence, or bring to you a borrowed flame that ye may warm yourselves.

7.author: Qarai
7.text: When Moses said to his family, ‘Indeed I descry a fire! I will bring you some news from it, or bring you a firebrand so that you may warm yourselves.’

7.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
7.text: When Moses said to his household: 'Indeed, I can see a fire far away. I will go and bring you news of it or I will bring you a lighted flame so that you can warm yourselves'

7.author: Saheeh International
7.text: [Mention] when Moses said to his family, "Indeed, I have perceived a fire. I will bring you from there information or will bring you a burning torch that you may warm yourselves."

7.author: Sarwar
7.text: Moses said to his family, "I have seen some fire. I shall bring you some news about it or some fire so that you can warmyourselves".

7.author: Shakir
7.text: When Musa said to his family: Surely I see fire; I will bring to you from it some news, or I will bring to you therefrom a burning firebrand so that you may warm yourselves.

7.author: Wahiduddin Khan
7.text: Tell of Moses who said to his family, "I have seen a fire. I will bring you news from there, or a burning brand for you to warm yourselves."

7.author: Yusuf Ali
7.text: Behold! Moses said to his family: "I perceive a fire; soon will I bring you from there some information, or I will bring you a burning brand to light our fuel, that ye may warm yourselves.

7.author: Transliteration
7.text: Ith qala moosa liahlihi innee anastu naran saateekum minha bikhabarin aw ateekum bishihabin qabasin laAAallakum tastaloona

8.author: Ahmed Ali
8.text: But when he reached it, a voice called out: "Blessed is He who is in the fire and all around it. Praised be God, the Lord of all the worlds.

8.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
8.text: So when he reached it, it was proclaimed, “Blessed is he who is in the location of the fire (Moosa) and those who are close to it (the angels); and Purity is to Allah, the Lord Of The Creation.”

8.author: Arberry
8.text: So, when he came to it, he was called: 'Blessed is he who is in the fire, and he who is about it. Glory be to God, the Lord of all Being!

8.author: Daryabadi
8.text: Then when he came unto it, he was cried unto Thus; Blest is whosoever is in the fire and whosoever is around it; and hallowed be Allah, the Lord of the Worlds.

8.author: Hilali & Khan
8.text: But when he came to it, he was called: "Blessed is whosoever is in the fire, and whosoever is round about it! And glorified be Allah, the Lord of the 'Alamin (mankind, jinns and all that exists).

8.author: Itani
8.text: Then, when he reached it, he was called: “Blessed is He who is within the fire, and He who is around it, and glorified be God, Lord of the Worlds.

8.author: Maududi
8.text: But when Moses came to the fire, a call was sounded: "Blessed is He Who is in the fire and whatever is around it. Glory be to Allah, the Sustainer of all in the Universe.

8.author: Mubarakpuri
8.text: But when he came to it, he was called: "Blessed is whosoever is in the fire, and whosoever is round about it! And glorified be Allah, the Lord of all that exists."

8.author: Pickthall
8.text: But when he reached it, he was called, saying: Blessed is Whosoever is in the fire and Whosoever is round about it! And Glorified be Allah, the Lord of the Worlds!

8.author: Qarai
8.text: When he came to it, he was called: ‘Blessed is He who is in the fire and who is [as well] around it, and immaculate is Allah, the Lord of all the worlds!’

8.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
8.text: And when he came near it he was called: 'Blessed be who is (Moses) in the fire and (the angels) who are around it! Exaltations to Allah, Lord of the Worlds!

8.author: Saheeh International
8.text: But when he came to it, he was called, "Blessed is whoever is at the fire and whoever is around it. And exalted is Allah, Lord of the worlds.

8.author: Sarwar
8.text: When he approached the fire, he was told, "Blessed is the one in the fire and those around it. All glory belongs to God, the Lord of the Universe.

8.author: Shakir
8.text: So when he came to it a voice was uttered saying: Blessed is Whoever is in the fire and whatever is about it; and glory be to Allah, the Lord of the worlds;

8.author: Wahiduddin Khan
8.text: When he came up to it, a voice called out, "Blessed be whoever is near this fire, and whoever is around it! Glory be to God, Lord of the Universe!

8.author: Yusuf Ali
8.text: But when he came to the (fire), a voice was heard: "Blessed are those in the fire and those around: and glory to Allah, the Lord of the worlds.

8.author: Transliteration
8.text: Falamma jaaha noodiya an boorika man fee alnnari waman hawlaha wasubhana Allahi rabbi alAAalameena

9.author: Ahmed Ali
9.text: O Moses, I am that God, the mighty and all-wise.

9.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
9.text: “O Moosa, I am indeed, in truth, Allah the Almighty, the Wise.”

9.author: Arberry
9.text: Moses, behold, it is I, God, the All-mighty, the All-wise.

9.author: Daryabadi
9.text: O Musa! verily it is I, Allah, the Mighty the Wise!

9.author: Hilali & Khan
9.text: "O Musa (Moses)! Verily! It is I, Allah, the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.

9.author: Itani
9.text: O Moses, it is I, God, the Almighty, the Wise.

9.author: Maududi
9.text: O Moses, verily this is Me, Allah, the All-Mighty, the All-Wise!

9.author: Mubarakpuri
9.text: "O Musa! Verily, it is I, Allah, the All-Mighty, the All-Wise."

9.author: Pickthall
9.text: O Moses! Lo! it is I, Allah, the Mighty, the Wise.

9.author: Qarai
9.text: ‘O Moses! Indeed I am Allah, the All-mighty, the All-wise.’

9.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
9.text: Moses, it is I, Allah, the Almighty, the Wise.

9.author: Saheeh International
9.text: O Moses, indeed it is I - Allah, the Exalted in Might, the Wise."

9.author: Sarwar
9.text: Moses, I am God, the Majestic and All-wise.

9.author: Shakir
9.text: O Musa! surely I am Allah, the Mighty, the Wise;

9.author: Wahiduddin Khan
9.text: O Moses, I am God, the Powerful, the Wise.

9.author: Yusuf Ali
9.text: "O Moses! verily, I am Allah, the exalted in might, the wise!....

9.author: Transliteration
9.text: Ya moosa innahu ana Allahu alAAazeezu alhakeemu

10.author: Ahmed Ali
10.text: Throw down your staff." When he saw it wriggling like a serpent he turned his back and fled without turning (to look). "O Moses," (said the voice), "be not fearful. Surely those sent as messengers do not fear in My presence,

10.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
10.text: “And put down your staff”; so when he saw it writhing like a serpent, he turned moving away without looking back; We said, “O Moosa, do not fear; indeed the Noble Messengers do not fear in My presence.”

10.author: Arberry
10.text: Cast down thy staff.' And when he saw it quivering like a serpent he turned about, retreating, and turned not back. 'Moses, fear not; surely the Envoys do not fear in My presence,

10.author: Daryabadi
10.text: And cast thou down thy staff. Then when he saw it wriggling as though it were a serpent, he turned in flight and looked not back. O Musa! fear thou not; verily in My presence the sent ones fear not.

10.author: Hilali & Khan
10.text: "And throw down your stick!" But when he saw it moving as if it were a snake, he turned in flight, and did not look back. (It was said): "O Musa (Moses)! Fear not, verily! The Messengers fear not in front of Me.

10.author: Itani
10.text: Throw down your staff.” But when he saw it quivering, as though it were a demon, he turned around not looking back. “O Moses, do not fear; the messengers do not fear in My presence.

10.author: Maududi
10.text: Now cast your rod!" But when he saw the rod writhing as though it were a serpent, he turned his back in retreat and did not even look behind. "O Moses, have no fear. Messengers have no fear in My presence,

10.author: Mubarakpuri
10.text: "And throw down your stick!" But when he saw it moving as if it were a snake, he turned in flight, and did not look back. (It was said:) "O Musa! Fear not: verily, the Messengers fear not in front of Me."

10.author: Pickthall
10.text: And throw down thy staff! But when he saw it writhing as it were a demon, he turned to flee headlong; (but it was said unto him): O Moses! Fear not! the emissaries fear not in My presence,

10.author: Qarai
10.text: ‘Throw down your staff!’ When he saw it wriggling, as if it were a snake, he turned his back [to flee], without looking back. ‘O Moses! ‘Do not be afraid. Indeed the apostles are not afraid before Me,

10.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
10.text: Cast down your staff' And when he saw it writhing like a serpent, he turned about retreating, and did not turn back. 'Moses, do not fear, indeed theMessengers do not fear (when they are) with Me,

10.author: Saheeh International
10.text: And [he was told], "Throw down your staff." But when he saw it writhing as if it were a snake, he turned in flight and did not return. [Allah said], "O Moses, fear not. Indeed, in My presence the messengers do not fear.

10.author: Sarwar
10.text: Throw down your staff." When Moses saw his staff on the ground moving like a living creature, he stepped back and did not come forward again. The Lord said,"Moses, do not be afraid. Messengers do not become afraid in My presence".

10.author: Shakir
10.text: And cast down your staff. So when he saw it in motion as if it were a serpent, he turned back retreating and did not return: O Musa! fear not; surely the apostles shall not fear in My presence;

10.author: Wahiduddin Khan
10.text: Throw down your staff." But when he saw it moving like a snake, he turned and fled. "Moses, do not be afraid! The messengers need have no fear in My presence;

10.author: Yusuf Ali
10.text: "Now do thou throw thy rod!" But when he saw it moving (of its own accord) as if it had been a snake, he turned back in retreat, and retraced not his steps: "O Moses!" (it was said), "Fear not: truly, in My presence, those called as messengers have no fear,-

10.author: Transliteration
10.text: Waalqi AAasaka falamma raaha tahtazzu kaannaha jannun walla mudbiran walam yuAAaqqib ya moosa la takhaf innee la yakhafu ladayya almursaloona

11.author: Ahmed Ali
11.text: Except those who do some wrong but afterwards do good to make up for the wrong. And I am forgiving and merciful.

11.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
11.text: “Except the one* who does injustice and then after evil changes it for virtue – then indeed I am Oft Forgiving, Most Merciful.” (Other than the Prophets.)

11.author: Arberry
11.text: save him who has done evil, then; after evil, has changed into good; All-forgiving am I, All-compassionate.

11.author: Daryabadi
11.text: Excepting any who may have done wrong and thereafter changeth evil for good then verily I am Forgiving, Merciful.

11.author: Hilali & Khan
11.text: "Except him who has done wrong and afterwards has changed evil for good, then surely, I am Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

11.author: Itani
11.text: But whoever has done wrong, and then substituted goodness in place of evil. I am Forgiving and Merciful.

11.author: Maududi
11.text: except he who has committed some wrong. But if he substitutes good in place of evil, I am Most Forgiving, Most Compassionate.

11.author: Mubarakpuri
11.text: "Except him who has done wrong and afterwards has changed evil for good; then surely, I am Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful."

11.author: Pickthall
11.text: Save him who hath done wrong and afterward hath changed evil for good. And lo! I am Forgiving, Merciful.

11.author: Qarai
11.text: nor those who do wrong and then make up for [their] fault with goodness, for indeed I am all-forgiving, all-merciful.’

11.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
11.text: except he who has done evil, then, after evil, has changed into good. I am the Forgiving, Most Merciful.

11.author: Saheeh International
11.text: Otherwise, he who wrongs, then substitutes good after evil - indeed, I am Forgiving and Merciful.

11.author: Sarwar
11.text: Only the unjust become afraid in My presence. Even to these people who replace their bad deeds by good ones, I am All-forgiving and All-merciful.

11.author: Shakir
11.text: Neither he who has been unjust, then he does good instead after evil, for surely I am the Forgiving, the Merciful:

11.author: Wahiduddin Khan
11.text: as for those who do wrong and then do good after evil, I am most forgiving, most merciful.

11.author: Yusuf Ali
11.text: "But if any have done wrong and have thereafter substituted good to take the place of evil, truly, I am Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

11.author: Transliteration
11.text: Illa man thalama thumma baddala husnan baAAda sooin fainnee ghafoorun raheemun

12.author: Ahmed Ali
12.text: Put your hand in the bosom of your shirt; it will come out white without any blemish. This will be one of nine tokens for the Pharaoh and his people, who are a wicked lot indeed."

12.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
12.text: “And put your hand inside your armpit – it will come out shining white, not due to any illness; a sign among the nine signs towards Firaun and his people; they are indeed a lawless nation.”

12.author: Arberry
12.text: Thrust thy hand in thy bosom and it will come forth white without evil-among nine signs to Pharaoh and his people; they are an ungodly people.'

12.author: Daryabadi
12.text: And put thy hand into thy bosom, it Shall come forth white, with out hurt: amongst nine signs unto Fir'awn and his people. Verily they have been a people transgressing.

12.author: Hilali & Khan
12.text: "And put your hand into your bosom, it will come forth white without hurt. (These are) among the nine signs (you will take) to Fir'aun (Pharaoh) and his people, they are a people who are the Fasiqun (rebellious, disobedient to Allah).

12.author: Itani
12.text: Put your hand inside your pocket, and it will come out white, without blemish—among nine miracles to Pharaoh and his people, for they are immoral people.”

12.author: Maududi
12.text: Now put your hand into your bosom, and it will come forth shining without any blemish. (These are two of the) nine Signs to be carried to Pharaoh and his people. They are surely a wicked people."

12.author: Mubarakpuri
12.text: "And put your hand into opening of your garment, it will come forth white without hurt. (These are) among the nine signs (you will take) to Fir`awn and his people. Verily, they are a people who are rebellious."

12.author: Pickthall
12.text: And put thy hand into the bosom of thy robe, it will come forth white but unhurt. (This will be one) among nine tokens unto Pharaoh and his people Lo! they were ever evil-living folk.

12.author: Qarai
12.text: ‘Insert your hand into your shirt. It will emerge white and bright, without any fault—among nine signs meant for Pharaoh and his people. Indeed they are a transgressing lot.’

12.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
12.text: Put your hand inside your collar and it will come out luminous without evil? among the nine signs to Pharaoh and his nation, indeed they were an impious nation'

12.author: Saheeh International
12.text: And put your hand into the opening of your garment [at the breast]; it will come out white without disease. [These are] among the nine signs [you will take] to Pharaoh and his people. Indeed, they have been a people defiantly disobedient."

12.author: Sarwar
12.text: Put your hand into your pocket. It will come out sheer white but unharmed. This is one of the nine miracles which shall be showing to the Pharaoh and his people; they are truly wicked men."

12.author: Shakir
12.text: And enter your hand into the opening of your bosom, it shall come forth white without evil; among nine signs to Firon and his people, surely they are a transgressing people.

12.author: Wahiduddin Khan
12.text: Now put your hand inside your cloak next to your bosom and it will come out [shining] white, without any blemish. This will be one of the nine signs for Pharaoh and his people: for truly they are a rebellious people."

12.author: Yusuf Ali
12.text: "Now put thy hand into thy bosom, and it will come forth white without stain (or harm): (these are) among the nine Signs (thou wilt take) to Pharaoh and his people: for they are a people rebellious in transgression."

12.author: Transliteration
12.text: Waadkhil yadaka fee jaybika takhruj baydaa min ghayri sooin fee tisAAi ayatin ila firAAawna waqawmihi innahum kanoo qawman fasiqeena

13.author: Ahmed Ali
13.text: And when Our signs came as distinct proofs, they said: "This is only magic."

13.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
13.text: Then when Our enlightening signs came to them, they said, “This is clear magic.”

13.author: Arberry
13.text: But when Our signs came to them visibly, they said, 'This is a manifest sorcery';

13.author: Daryabadi
13.text: Then when Our signs came unto them illuminating, they said: this is a magic manifest.

13.author: Hilali & Khan
13.text: But when Our Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.) came to them, clear to see, they said: "This is a manifest magic."

13.author: Itani
13.text: Yet when Our enlightening signs came to them, they said, “This is obvious witchcraft.”

13.author: Maududi
13.text: But when Our Clear Signs came to them, they said: "This is plain magic."

13.author: Mubarakpuri
13.text: But when Our Ayat came to them, clear to see, they said: "This is a manifest magic."

13.author: Pickthall
13.text: But when Our tokens came unto them, plain to see, they said: This is mere magic,

13.author: Qarai
13.text: But when Our signs came to them, as eye-openers, they said, ‘This is plain magic.’

13.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
13.text: But when Our signs came to them visibly, they said: 'This is plain sorcery.'

13.author: Saheeh International
13.text: But when there came to them Our visible signs, they said, "This is obvious magic."

13.author: Sarwar
13.text: When Our miracles were visibly shown to them, they said, "It is plain magic".

13.author: Shakir
13.text: So when Our clear signs came to them, they said: This is clear enchantment.

13.author: Wahiduddin Khan
13.text: But when Our signs came to them in all their clarity they said, "This is clearly sorcery!"

13.author: Yusuf Ali
13.text: But when Our Signs came to them, that should have opened their eyes, they said: "This is sorcery manifest!"

13.author: Transliteration
13.text: Falamma jaathum ayatuna mubsiratan qaloo hatha sihrun mubeenun

14.author: Ahmed Ali
14.text: And they denied them out of malice and pride, though in their hearts they believed that they were true. So see how was the end of evil-doers!

14.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
14.text: And they denied them – whereas in their hearts they were certain of them – due to injustice and pride; therefore see what sort of fate befell the mischievous!

14.author: Arberry
14.text: and they denied them, though their souls acknowledged them, wrongfully and out of pride. Behold, how was the end of the workers of corruption!

14.author: Daryabadi
14.text: And they gainsaid them, out of spite and arrogance, although their souls were convinced thereof. So behold! what hath been the end of the corruptors.

14.author: Hilali & Khan
14.text: And they belied them (those Ayat) wrongfully and arrogantly, though their ownselves were convinced thereof [i.e. those (Ayat) are from Allah, and Musa (Moses) is the Messenger of Allah in truth, but they disliked to obey Musa (Moses), and hated to believe in his Message of Monotheism]. So see what was the end of the Mufsidun (disbelievers, disobedient to Allah, evil-doers, liars.).

14.author: Itani
14.text: And they rejected them, although their souls were certain of them, out of wickedness and pride. So see how the outcome was for the mischief-makers.

14.author: Maududi
14.text: They denied those Signs out of iniquity and arrogance although their hearts were convinced of their truth. So see how evil was the end of those mischief-makers!

14.author: Mubarakpuri
14.text: And they belied them wrongfully and arrogantly, though they themselves were convinced thereof. So, see what was the end of the mischief-makers.

14.author: Pickthall
14.text: And they denied them, though their souls acknowledged them, for spite and arrogance. Then see the nature of the consequence for the wrong-doers!

14.author: Qarai
14.text: They impugned them, wrongfully and out of arrogance, though they were convinced in their hearts [of their veracity]. So observe how the fate of the agents of corruption was!

14.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
14.text: They denied them unjustly out of pride, though their souls acknowledged them. See, how was the end of the corrupt workers!

14.author: Saheeh International
14.text: And they rejected them, while their [inner] selves were convinced thereof, out of injustice and haughtiness. So see how was the end of the corrupters.

14.author: Sarwar
14.text: They rejected the evidence because of their arrogance and injustice, although their souls knew it to be true. Think how terrible the end of the sinful ones was!

14.author: Shakir
14.text: And they denied them unjustly and proudly while their soul had been convinced of them; consider, then how was the end of the mischief-makers.

14.author: Wahiduddin Khan
14.text: And they persisted in rejecting them wrongfully and arrogantly, while in their hearts they were convinced of their truth. Observe, then, how evil was the fate of the evil-doers.

14.author: Yusuf Ali
14.text: And they rejected those Signs in iniquity and arrogance, though their souls were convinced thereof: so see what was the end of those who acted corruptly!

14.author: Transliteration
14.text: Wajahadoo biha waistayqanatha anfusuhum thulman waAAuluwwan faonthur kayfa kana AAaqibatu almufsideena

15.author: Ahmed Ali
15.text: We gave David and Solomon knowledge; and they said: "All praise be to God who has favoured us over many of His creatures who believe."

15.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
15.text: And We indeed bestowed great knowledge to Dawud and Sulaiman; and they both said, “All praise is to Allah, Who bestowed us superiority over many of His believing bondmen.”

15.author: Arberry
15.text: And We gave David and Solomon knowledge and they said, 'Praise belongs to God who has preferred us over many of His believing servants.'

15.author: Daryabadi
15.text: And assuredly We vouchsafed unto Daud and Sulaiman a knowledge, and the twain said: praise unto Allah Who hath preferred us above many of His believing bondmen!

15.author: Hilali & Khan
15.text: And indeed We gave knowledge to Dawud (David) and Sulaiman (Solomon), and they both said: "All the praises and thanks be to Allah, Who has preferred us above many of His believing slaves!"

15.author: Itani
15.text: And We gave David and Solomon knowledge. They said, “Praise God, who has favored us over many of His believing servants.”

15.author: Maududi
15.text: (On the other hand), We granted knowledge to David and Solomon and they said: "All praise be to Allah Who has exalted us above many of His believing servants!"

15.author: Mubarakpuri
15.text: And indeed We gave knowledge to Dawud and Sulayman, and they both said: "All praise be to Allah, Who has preferred us above many of His believing servants!"

15.author: Pickthall
15.text: And We verily gave knowledge unto David and Solomon, and they said: Praise be to Allah, Who hath preferred us above many of His believing slaves!

15.author: Qarai
15.text: Certainly, We gave knowledge to David and Solomon, and they said, ‘All praise belongs to Allah, who granted us an advantage over many of His faithful servants.’

15.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
15.text: We gave knowledge to David and Solomon. They said: 'Praise be to Allah who has preferred us above many of His believing worshipers'

15.author: Saheeh International
15.text: And We had certainly given to David and Solomon knowledge, and they said, "Praise [is due] to Allah, who has favored us over many of His believing servants."

15.author: Sarwar
15.text: We gave knowledge to David and Soloman. They said, "It is only God who deserves all praise. He has exalted us above many of His believing servants."

15.author: Shakir
15.text: And certainly We gave knowledge to Dawood and Sulaiman, and they both said: Praise be to Allah, Who has made us to excel many of His believing servants.

15.author: Wahiduddin Khan
15.text: We bestowed knowledge on David and Solomon and they both said, "Praise be to God who has exalted us above many of His believing servants."

15.author: Yusuf Ali
15.text: We gave (in the past) knowledge to David and Solomon: And they both said: "Praise be to Allah, Who has favoured us above many of his servants who believe!"

15.author: Transliteration
15.text: Walaqad atayna dawooda wasulaymana AAilman waqala alhamdu lillahi allathee faddalana AAala katheerin min AAibadihi almumineena

16.author: Ahmed Ali
16.text: Solomon was heir to David, and he said: "O people, we have been taught the language of Tair, and have been given of everything. This is a clear favour indeed."

16.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
16.text: And Sulaiman became Dawud’s heir; and he said, “O people, we have indeed been taught the language of birds, and have been given from all things; this surely is a manifest favour.”

16.author: Arberry
16.text: And Solomon was David's heir, and he said, 'Men, we have been taught the speech of the birds, and we have been given of everything; surely this is indeed the manifest bounty.'

16.author: Daryabadi
16.text: And Sulaiman inherited from Daud. And he said: O mankind! verily have been taught the diction of we birds, and we have been vcuchsafed of every thing; and verily this is grace manifest.

16.author: Hilali & Khan
16.text: And Sulaiman (Solomon) inherited (the knowledge of) Dawud (David). He said: "O mankind! We have been taught the language of birds, and on us have been bestowed all things. This, verily, is an evident grace (from Allah)."

16.author: Itani
16.text: And Solomon succeeded David. He said, “O people, we were taught the language of birds, and we were given from everything. This is indeed a real blessing.”

16.author: Maududi
16.text: And Solomon succeeded David and said: "O people, we have been taught the speech of birds and we have been endowed with all kinds of things. Surely this is a conspicuous favour (from Allah)."

16.author: Mubarakpuri
16.text: And Sulayman inherited from Dawud. He said: "O mankind! We have been taught the language of birds, and we have been given from everything. This, verily, is an evident grace."

16.author: Pickthall
16.text: And Solomon was David's heir. And he said: O mankind! Lo! we have been taught the language of birds, and have been given (abundance) of all things. This surely is evident favour.

16.author: Qarai
16.text: Solomon inherited from David, and he said, ‘O people! We have been taught the speech of the birds, and we have been given out of everything. Indeed this is a manifest advantage.’

16.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
16.text: Solomon inherited David. He said: 'Know, my people, we have been taught the speech of birds and given everything. Surely, this is a clear bounty'

16.author: Saheeh International
16.text: And Solomon inherited David. He said, "O people, we have been taught the language of birds, and we have been given from all things. Indeed, this is evident bounty."

16.author: Sarwar
16.text: Solomon became the heir to David. He said, "People, we have been taught the language of the birds and have been granted a share of everything. This indeed is a manifest favor (from God)".

16.author: Shakir
16.text: And Sulaiman was Dawood's heir, and he said: O men! we have been taught the language of birds, and we have been given all things; most surely this is manifest grace.

16.author: Wahiduddin Khan
16.text: Solomon succeeded David. He said, "Know, my people, that we have been taught the speech of birds and endowed with all good things. Surely this is God's manifest grace."

16.author: Yusuf Ali
16.text: And Solomon was David's heir. He said: "O ye people! We have been taught the speech of birds, and on us has been bestowed (a little) of all things: this is indeed Grace manifest (from Allah.)"

16.author: Transliteration
16.text: Wawaritha sulaymanu dawooda waqala ya ayyuha alnnasu AAullimna mantiqa alttayri waooteena min kulli shayin inna hatha lahuwa alfadlu almubeenu

17.author: Ahmed Ali
17.text: His armies of jinns and men and Tair assembled, formed into ranks, (and marched)

17.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
17.text: And assembled together for Sulaiman were his armies of jinns and men, and of birds – so they had to be restricted.

17.author: Arberry
17.text: And his hosts were mustered to Solomon, jinn, men and birds, duly disposed;

17.author: Daryabadi
17.text: And there were gathered unto Sulaiman his hosts of jinns and mankind and birds, and they were set in bands.

17.author: Hilali & Khan
17.text: And there were gathered before Sulaiman (Solomon) his hosts of jinns and men, and birds, and they all were set in battle order (marching forwards).

17.author: Itani
17.text: To the service of Solomon were mobilized his troops of sprites, and men, and birds—all held in strict order.

17.author: Maududi
17.text: Hosts of jinn and humans and birds were marshalled for Solomon and were kept under full control.

17.author: Mubarakpuri
17.text: And there were gathered before Sulayman his hosts of Jinn and men, and birds, and they all were set in battle order (marching forward).

17.author: Pickthall
17.text: And there were gathered together unto Solomon his armies of the jinn and humankind, and of the birds, and they were set in battle order;

17.author: Qarai
17.text: [Once] Solomon’s hosts were marched out for him, comprising jinn, humans and birds, and they were held in check.

17.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
17.text: We gathered to Solomon his army of jinn, humans and birds; gathered and dispersed,

17.author: Saheeh International
17.text: And gathered for Solomon were his soldiers of the jinn and men and birds, and they were [marching] in rows.

17.author: Sarwar
17.text: Soloman's army, consisting of human beings, jinn, and birds were gathered together in his presence in ranks.

17.author: Shakir
17.text: And his hosts of the jinn and the men and the birds were gathered to him, and they were formed into groups.

17.author: Wahiduddin Khan
17.text: Solomon's hosts of jinn and men and birds, were all gathered together in his presence and were ranged in battle order,

17.author: Yusuf Ali
17.text: And before Solomon were marshalled his hosts,- of Jinns and men and birds, and they were all kept in order and ranks.

17.author: Transliteration
17.text: Wahushira lisulaymana junooduhu mina aljinni waalinsi waalttayri fahum yoozaAAoona

18.author: Ahmed Ali
18.text: Till they reached the Valley of Naml. Said the lady of Naml: "O Naml, go into your dwellings lest Solomon and his hordes should crush you unawares."

18.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
18.text: Until when they came to the valley of the ants, a she ant exclaimed, “O ants, enter your houses – may not Sulaiman and his armies crush you, unknowingly.”

18.author: Arberry
18.text: till, when they came on the Valley of Ants, an ant said, 'Ants, enter your dwelling-places, lest Solomon and his hosts crush you, being unaware!'

18.author: Daryabadi
18.text: Until when they came unto the valley of the ants, an ant said: O ants! enter Your habitations lest Sulaiman and his hosts crush you while they perceive not.

18.author: Hilali & Khan
18.text: Till, when they came to the valley of the ants, one of the ants said: "O ants! Enter your dwellings, lest Sulaiman (Solomon) and his hosts crush you, while they perceive not."

18.author: Itani
18.text: Until, when they came upon the Valley of Ants, an ant said, “O ants! Go into your nests, lest Solomon and his troops crush you without noticing.”

18.author: Maududi
18.text: (Solomon was once on the move with them) until when they reached a valley of ants one of the ants said: "O ants, get into your holes, lest Solomon and his hosts crush you (under their feet) without even knowing."

18.author: Mubarakpuri
18.text: Till, when they came to the valley of the ants, one of the ants said: "O ants! Enter your dwellings, lest Sulayman and his armies should crush you, while they perceive not."

18.author: Pickthall
18.text: Till, when they reached the Valley of the Ants, an ant exclaimed: O ants! Enter your dwellings lest Solomon and his armies crush you, unperceiving.

18.author: Qarai
18.text: When they came to the Valley of Ants, an ant said, ‘O ants! Enter your dwellings, lest Solomon and his hosts should trample on you while they are unaware.’

18.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
18.text: and when they came to the Valley of the Ants, an ant said: 'Ants, go into your dwellings lest Solomon and his army should, unknowingly, crush you'

18.author: Saheeh International
18.text: Until, when they came upon the valley of the ants, an ant said, "O ants, enter your dwellings that you not be crushed by Solomon and his soldiers while they perceive not."

18.author: Sarwar
18.text: When they arrived in the valley of the ants, one ant said to the others, "Enter your dwellings lest you be carelessly crushed by Soloman and his army."

18.author: Shakir
18.text: Until when they came to the valley of the Naml, a Namlite said: O Naml! enter your houses, (that) Sulaiman and his hosts may not crush you while they do not know.

18.author: Wahiduddin Khan
18.text: and when they came to the Valley of the Ants, one ant said, "Ants! Go into your dwellings, in case Solomon and his hosts inadvertently crush you."

18.author: Yusuf Ali
18.text: At length, when they came to a (lowly) valley of ants, one of the ants said: "O ye ants, get into your habitations, lest Solomon and his hosts crush you (under foot) without knowing it."

18.author: Transliteration
18.text: Hatta itha ataw AAala wadi alnnamli qalat namlatun ya ayyuha alnnamlu odkhuloo masakinakum la yahtimannakum sulaymanu wajunooduhu wahum la yashAAuroona

19.author: Ahmed Ali
19.text: (Solomon) smiled, amused at her speech, and said: "O Lord grant me that I should be grateful for the favours You have bestowed on me and my parents, and do good things of Your pleasing; and admit me among Your righteous devotees by Your grace."

19.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
19.text: He therefore smiled beamingly at her speech*, and submitted, “My Lord, bestow me guidance so that I thank you for the favour which You bestowed upon me and my parents, and so that I may perform the good deeds which please You, and by Your mercy include me among Your bondmen who are worthy of Your proximity.” (Prophet Sulaiman heard the voice of the she ant from far away.)

19.author: Arberry
19.text: But he smiled, laughing at its words, and he said, 'My Lord, dispose me that I may be thankful for Thy blessing wherewith Thou hast blessed me and my father and mother, and that I may do righteousness well-pleasing to Thee; and do Thou admit me, by Thy mercy, amongst Thy righteous servants.'

19.author: Daryabadi
19.text: So he smiled, amused at her speech, and said: my Lord! arouse me that I should be thankful for Thy favour wherewith Thou hast favoured me and my parents, and that I should work righteously pleasing Thee, and out of Thy mercy enter me among Thine righteous bondmen.

19.author: Hilali & Khan
19.text: So he [Sulaiman (Solomon)] smiled, amused at her speech and said: "My Lord! Inspire and bestow upon me the power and ability that I may be grateful for Your Favours which You have bestowed on me and on my parents, and that I may do righteous good deeds that will please You, and admit me by Your Mercy among Your righteous slaves."

19.author: Itani
19.text: He smiled and laughed at her words, and said, “My Lord, direct me to be thankful for the blessings you have bestowed upon me and upon my parents, and to do good works that please You. And admit me, by Your grace, into the company of Your virtuous servants.”

19.author: Maududi
19.text: Smiling at the ant's utterance, Solomon burst into laughter and said: "My Lord! Hold me under (Your) control that I may render thanks for the favour which You have bestowed on me and on my parents, and that I may act righteously in a manner that would please You. Include me, out of Your Mercy, among Your righteous servants."

19.author: Mubarakpuri
19.text: So he (Sulayman) smiled, amused at her speech and said: "My Lord! Grant me the power and ability that I may be grateful for Your favors which You have bestowed on me and on my parents, and that I may do righteous good deeds that will please You, and admit me by Your mercy among Your righteous servants."

19.author: Pickthall
19.text: And (Solomon) smiled, laughing at her speech, and said: My Lord, arouse me to be thankful for Thy favour wherewith Thou hast favoured me and my parents, and to do good that shall be pleasing unto Thee, and include me in (the number of) Thy righteous slaves.

19.author: Qarai
19.text: Whereat he smiled, amused at its words, and he said, ‘My Lord! Inspire me to give thanks for Your blessing with which You have blessed me and my parents, and that I may do righteous deeds which please You, and admit me, by Your mercy, among Your righteous servants.’

19.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
19.text: He smiled, and laughed at its words, and said: 'My Lord, inspire me that I should be thankful for Your blessing with which You have blessed me and my parents, and that I may do good works that will please You. Admit me, by Your Mercy, among Your righteous worshipers'

19.author: Saheeh International
19.text: So [Solomon] smiled, amused at her speech, and said, "My Lord, enable me to be grateful for Your favor which You have bestowed upon me and upon my parents and to do righteousness of which You approve. And admit me by Your mercy into [the ranks of] Your righteous servants."

19.author: Sarwar
19.text: (Solomon) smiled at the ant's remarks and said, "Lord, inspire me to thank you for Your favors to me and my parents and to act righteously so as to please you. Admit me, by your mercy into the company of Your righteous servants".

19.author: Shakir
19.text: So he smiled, wondering at her word, and said: My Lord! grant me that I should be grateful for Thy favor which Thou hast bestowed on me and on my parents, and that I should do good such as Thou art pleased with, and make me enter, by Thy mercy, into Thy servants, the good ones.

19.author: Wahiduddin Khan
19.text: Solomon smiled broadly at its words and said, "Lord, inspire me to be thankful for the blessings You have granted me and my parents, and to do good deeds that please You; and include me, by Your grace, among Your righteous servants!"

19.author: Yusuf Ali
19.text: So he smiled, amused at her speech; and he said: "O my Lord! so order me that I may be grateful for Thy favours, which thou hast bestowed on me and on my parents, and that I may work the righteousness that will please Thee: And admit me, by Thy Grace, to the ranks of Thy righteous Servants."

19.author: Transliteration
19.text: Fatabassama dahikan min qawliha waqala rabbi awziAAnee an ashkura niAAmataka allatee anAAamta AAalayya waAAala walidayya waan aAAmala salihan tardahu waadkhilnee birahmatika fee AAibadika alssaliheena

20.author: Ahmed Ali
20.text: When he reviewed the Tair, he said: "How is it I do not see Hud-hud? Is he absent?

20.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
20.text: And he surveyed the birds – he therefore said, “What is to me that I do not see the Hudhud (hoopoe), or is he really absent?”

20.author: Arberry
20.text: And he reviewed the birds; then he said, 'How is it with me, that I do not see the hoopoe? Or is he among the absent?

20.author: Daryabadi
20.text: And he sought after the birds and said: what aileth me that I see not the hoopoe; is he among the absentees?

20.author: Hilali & Khan
20.text: He inspected the birds, and said: "What is the matter that I see not the hoopoe? Or is he among the absentees?

20.author: Itani
20.text: Then he inspected the birds, and said, “Why do I not see the hoopoe? Or is he among the absentees?

20.author: Maududi
20.text: (On another occasion) Solomon inspected the birds and said: "Why is it that I do not see the hoopoe? Is he among the absentees?

20.author: Mubarakpuri
20.text: He inspected the birds, and said: "What is the matter that I see not the hoopoe Or is he among the absentees"

20.author: Pickthall
20.text: And he sought among the birds and said: How is it that I see not the hoopoe, or is he among the absent?

20.author: Qarai
20.text: [One day] he reviewed the birds, and said, ‘Why do I not see the hoopoe? Or is he absent?’

20.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
20.text: He reviewed the birds and said: 'Why is it that I do not see the hoopoe here? Or is he among the absent?

20.author: Saheeh International
20.text: And he took attendance of the birds and said, "Why do I not see the hoopoe - or is he among the absent?

20.author: Sarwar
20.text: (Solomon) inspected the birds and said, "How is it that I cannot see the hoopoe. Is he absent?

20.author: Shakir
20.text: And he reviewed the birds, then said: How is it I see not the hoopoe or is it that he is of the absentees?

20.author: Wahiduddin Khan
20.text: Then Solomon inspected the birds, and said, "How is it that I do not see the hoopoe? Is he absent then?

20.author: Yusuf Ali
20.text: And he took a muster of the Birds; and he said: "Why is it I see not the Hoopoe? Or is he among the absentees?

20.author: Transliteration
20.text: Watafaqqada alttayra faqala ma liya la ara alhudhuda am kana mina alghaibeena

21.author: Ahmed Ali
21.text: I will punish him severely, or cut his throat, unless he bring a valid excuse."

21.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
21.text: “I will indeed punish him severely or slay him, or he must bring to me some clear evidence.”

21.author: Arberry
21.text: Assuredly I will chastise him with a terrible chastisement, or I will slaughter him, or he bring me a clear authority.'

21.author: Daryabadi
21.text: Surely I shall torment him with a severe torment, or I shall slaughter him, unless he bringeth unto me a warranty manifest.

21.author: Hilali & Khan
21.text: "I will surely punish him with a severe torment, or slaughter him, unless he brings me a clear reason."

21.author: Itani
21.text: I will punish him most severely, or slay him, unless he gives me a valid excuse.”

21.author: Maududi
21.text: I will inflict a severe punishment on him or maybe even slaughter him unless he comes forth with a convincing reason (for his absence)."

21.author: Mubarakpuri
21.text: "I will surely punish him with a severe torment or slaughter him, unless he brings me a clear reason."

21.author: Pickthall
21.text: I verily will punish him with hard punishment or I verily will slay him, or he verily shall bring me a plain excuse.

21.author: Qarai
21.text: ‘I will punish him with a severe punishment, or I will behead him, unless he brings me a credible excuse.’

21.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
21.text: Surely, I will punish him with a terrible punishment, or I will slaughter him or he gives me a good reason'

21.author: Saheeh International
21.text: I will surely punish him with a severe punishment or slaughter him unless he brings me clear authorization."

21.author: Sarwar
21.text: I shall certainly punish him severely or slaughter him unless he has a good reason for his absence."

21.author: Shakir
21.text: I will most certainly punish him with a severe punishment, or kill him, or he shall bring to me a clear plea.

21.author: Wahiduddin Khan
21.text: I shall surely punish him severely or order him to be executed, unless he gives me a good reason for his absence."

21.author: Yusuf Ali
21.text: "I will certainly punish him with a severe penalty, or execute him, unless he bring me a clear reason (for absence)."

21.author: Transliteration
21.text: LaoAAaththibannahu AAathaban shadeedan aw laathbahannahu aw layatiyannee bisultanin mubeenin

22.author: Ahmed Ali
22.text: But he was not long in coming, and reported: "I have been around where you have not been. I come from Saba with positive news.

22.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
22.text: So Hudhud did not stay absent for long, and presenting himself submitted, “I have witnessed a matter that your majesty has not seen, and I have brought definite information to you from the city of Saba.”

22.author: Arberry
22.text: But he tarried not long, and said, 'I have comprehended that which thou hast not comprehended, and I have come from Sheba to thee with a sure tiding.

22.author: Daryabadi
22.text: Buthe tarried not far, and he said: I have encompassed that which thou hast- not encompassed, and I come unto thee from Saba with a tidings sure.

22.author: Hilali & Khan
22.text: But the hoopoe stayed not long, he (came up and) said: "I have grasped (the knowledge of a thing) which you have not grasped and I have come to you from Saba' (Sheba) with true news.

22.author: Itani
22.text: But he did not stay for long. He said, “I have learnt something you did not know. I have come to you from Sheba, with reliable information.

22.author: Maududi
22.text: Not before long the hoopoe came up and said: "I have obtained a knowledge which you could not. I have brought for you sure news about Sheba.

22.author: Mubarakpuri
22.text: But (the hoopoe) stayed not long, he said: "I have grasped which you have not grasped and I have come to you from Saba' with true news."

22.author: Pickthall
22.text: But he was not long in coming, and he said: I have found out (a thing) that thou apprehendest not, and I come unto thee from Sheba with sure tidings.

22.author: Qarai
22.text: He did not stay for long [before he turned up] and said, ‘I have alighted on something which you have not alighted on, and I have brought you from Sheba a definite report.

22.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
22.text: He was not long in coming, and said: 'I know what you do not know. I come to you from Sheba with certain news.

22.author: Saheeh International
22.text: But the hoopoe stayed not long and said, "I have encompassed [in knowledge] that which you have not encompassed, and I have come to you from Sheba with certain news.

22.author: Sarwar
22.text: Not long after the hoopoe came forward and said, "I have information which you do not have. I have come from the land of Sheba with a true report.

22.author: Shakir
22.text: And he tarried not long, then said: I comprehend that which you do not comprehend and I have brought to you a sure information from Sheba.

22.author: Wahiduddin Khan
22.text: But he was not long in coming, and said, "I have learnt something you did not know. I have come to you from Sheba with reliable news.

22.author: Yusuf Ali
22.text: But the Hoopoe tarried not far: he (came up and) said: "I have compassed (territory) which thou hast not compassed, and I have come to thee from Saba with tidings true.

22.author: Transliteration
22.text: Famakatha ghayra baAAeedin faqala ahattu bima lam tuhit bihi wajituka min sabain binabain yaqeenin

23.author: Ahmed Ali
23.text: I found a woman reigning over them, and she has been favoured with everything; and she has a throne that is magnificent.

23.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
23.text: “I have seen a woman who rules over them, and she has been given from all things, and she has a mighty throne.”

23.author: Arberry
23.text: I found a woman ruling over them, and she has been given of everything, and she possesses a mighty throne.

23.author: Daryabadi
23.text: Verily have found a woman ruling over them and she hath been vouchsafed somewhat of everything, and hers is a mighty throne.

23.author: Hilali & Khan
23.text: "I found a woman ruling over them, and she has been given all things that could be possessed by any ruler of the earth, and she has a great throne.

23.author: Itani
23.text: I found a woman ruling over them, and she was given of everything, and she has a magnificent throne.

23.author: Maududi
23.text: I found there a woman ruling over them, one who has been endowed with all things and has a mighty throne.

23.author: Mubarakpuri
23.text: "I found a woman ruling over them, she has been given all things, and she has a great throne."

23.author: Pickthall
23.text: Lo! I found a woman ruling over them, and she hath been given (abundance) of all things, and hers is a mighty throne.

23.author: Qarai
23.text: I found a woman ruling over them, and she has been given everything, and she has a great throne.

23.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
23.text: There I found a woman ruling over them. She possess everything and has a great throne.

23.author: Saheeh International
23.text: Indeed, I found [there] a woman ruling them, and she has been given of all things, and she has a great throne.

23.author: Sarwar
23.text: I found a woman ruling the people there and she possessed something of (almost) everything and a great throne.

23.author: Shakir
23.text: Surely I found a woman ruling over them, and she has been given abundance and she has a mighty throne:

23.author: Wahiduddin Khan
23.text: I found a woman ruling over them, who has been given everything and she has a mighty throne.

23.author: Yusuf Ali
23.text: "I found (there) a woman ruling over them and provided with every requisite; and she has a magnificent throne.

23.author: Transliteration
23.text: Innee wajadtu imraatan tamlikuhum waootiyat min kulli shayin walaha AAarshun AAatheemun

24.author: Ahmed Ali
24.text: I found that she and her people worship the sun in place of God, for Satan has made their deeds look attractive to them and has turned them away from the Path, so they do not find the way

24.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
24.text: “I found her and her nation prostrating before the sun instead of Allah, and Satan has made their deeds seem good to them thereby preventing them from the Straight Path – so they do not attain guidance.”

24.author: Arberry
24.text: I found her and her people prostrating to the sun, apart from God; Satan has decked out fair their deeds to them and he has barred them from the way, and therefore they are not guided,

24.author: Daryabadi
24.text: I have found her and her people adoring the sun instead of Allah, and the Satan hath made their works fairseeming unto them, and hath barred them from the way, so they are not guided.

24.author: Hilali & Khan
24.text: "I found her and her people worshipping the sun instead of Allah, and Shaitan (Satan) has made their deeds fair-seeming to them, and has barred them from (Allah's) Way, so they have no guidance,"

24.author: Itani
24.text: I found her and her people worshiping the sun, instead of God. Satan made their conduct appear good to them, and diverted them from the path, so they are not guided.

24.author: Maududi
24.text: I found that she and her people prostrate themselves before the sun rather than Allah. " Satan has made their deeds appear attractive to them and has, thus, debarred them from the Right Path so they do not find true guidance

24.author: Mubarakpuri
24.text: "I found her and her people worshipping the sun instead of Allah, and Shaytan has made their deeds fair seeming to them to prevent them from the way, so they have no guidance."

24.author: Pickthall
24.text: I found her and her people worshipping the sun instead of Allah; and Satan maketh their works fairseeming unto them, and debarreth them from the way (of Truth), so that they go not aright;

24.author: Qarai
24.text: I found her and her people prostrating to the sun instead of Allah, and Satan has made their deeds seem decorous to them—thus he has barred them from the way [of Allah], so they are not guided—

24.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
24.text: But she and her people prostrate to the sun instead of Allah. And satan has made their deeds seem pleasing to them and barred them from the Path, and therefore they are not guided.

24.author: Saheeh International
24.text: I found her and her people prostrating to the sun instead of Allah, and Satan has made their deeds pleasing to them and averted them from [His] way, so they are not guided,

24.author: Sarwar
24.text: I found her and her people prostrating before the sun instead of God. Satan has made their deeds attractive to them. He has kept them away from the right path and they have no guidance.

24.author: Shakir
24.text: I found her and her people adoring the sun instead of Allah, and the Shaitan has made their deeds fair-seeming to them and thus turned them from the way, so they do not go aright

24.author: Wahiduddin Khan
24.text: I found her and her people worshipping the sun, instead of God. Satan has made their conduct appear good to them, and has thus diverted them from the right path, so that they might not be guided.

24.author: Yusuf Ali
24.text: "I found her and her people worshipping the sun besides Allah: Satan has made their deeds seem pleasing in their eyes, and has kept them away from the Path,- so they receive no guidance,-

24.author: Transliteration
24.text: Wajadtuha waqawmaha yasjudoona lilshshamsi min dooni Allahi wazayyana lahumu alshshaytanu aAAmalahum fasaddahum AAani alssabeeli fahum la yahtadoona

25.author: Ahmed Ali
25.text: To worship God who brings to light what is hidden in the heavens and the earth, and is cognisant of what you hide and what you disclose.

25.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
25.text: “Why do they not prostrate to Allah, Who brings forth the things hidden in the heavens and the earth, and knows all what you hide and all what you disclose?”

25.author: Arberry
25.text: so that they prostrate not themselves to God, who brings forth what is hidden in the heavens and earth; and He knows what you conceal and what you publish.

25.author: Daryabadi
25.text: So that they adore not Allah who bringeth forth the hidden in the heavens and the earth, and knoweth that which conceal and that which ye make known.

25.author: Hilali & Khan
25.text: Al-La (this word has two interpretations) (A) [As Shaitan (Satan) has barred them from Allah's Way] so that they do not worship (prostrate before) Allah, or (B) So that they may worship (prostrate before) Allah, Who brings to light what is hidden in the heavens and the earth, and knows what you conceal and what you reveal. [Tafsir At-Tabari, Vol. 19, Page 149]

25.author: Itani
25.text: If only they would worship God, who brings to light the mysteries of the heavens and the earth, and knows what you conceal and what you reveal.

25.author: Maududi
25.text: that they would prostrate themselves before Allah Who brings to light all that is hidden in the heavens and the earth and knows all that you conceal and all that you reveal.

25.author: Mubarakpuri
25.text: So they do not prostrate themselves before Allah, Who brings to light what is hidden in the heavens and the earth, and knows what you conceal and what you reveal.

25.author: Pickthall
25.text: So that they worship not Allah, Who bringeth forth the hidden in the heavens and the earth, and knoweth what ye hide and what ye proclaim,

25.author: Qarai
25.text: so that they do not prostrate themselves to Allah, who brings forth the hidden in the heavens and the earth, and He knows whatever you hide and whatever you disclose.

25.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
25.text: Do they not prostrate themselves to Allah who brings forth all that is concealed in the heavens and earth and He knows what they hide and what they reveal?

25.author: Saheeh International
25.text: [And] so they do not prostrate to Allah, who brings forth what is hidden within the heavens and the earth and knows what you conceal and what you declare -

25.author: Sarwar
25.text: (Satan has done this) so that they will not worship God who brings forth whatever is hidden in the heavens and the earth and knows whatever you conceal or reveal.

25.author: Shakir
25.text: That they do not make obeisance to Allah, Who brings forth what is hidden in the heavens and the earth and knows what you hide and what you make manifest:

25.author: Wahiduddin Khan
25.text: Should they not worship God who brings forth what is hidden in the heavens and earth and knows both what you conceal and what you make known?

25.author: Yusuf Ali
25.text: "(Kept them away from the Path), that they should not worship Allah, Who brings to light what is hidden in the heavens and the earth, and knows what ye hide and what ye reveal.

25.author: Transliteration
25.text: Alla yasjudoo lillahi allathee yukhriju alkhabaa fee alssamawati waalardi wayaAAlamu ma tukhfoona wama tuAAlinoona

26.author: Ahmed Ali
26.text: God: There is no god but He, the Lord of the glorious throne."

26.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
26.text: “Allah – there is no True God except Him, the Owner of the Great Throne.” (Command of Prostration # 8)

26.author: Arberry
26.text: God: there is no god but He, the Lord of the Mighty Throne.'

26.author: Daryabadi
26.text: Allah! there is no God but he, the Lord of the Magnificent Throne.

26.author: Hilali & Khan
26.text: Allah, La ilaha illa Huwa (none has the right to be worshipped but He), the Lord of the Supreme Throne!

26.author: Itani
26.text: God—There is no god but He, the Lord of the Sublime Throne.”

26.author: Maududi
26.text: Allah - none is worthy of worship save He; He is the Lord of the Mighty Throne.

26.author: Mubarakpuri
26.text: Allah, La ilaha illa Huwa, the Lord of the Supreme Throne!"

26.author: Pickthall
26.text: Allah; there is no Allah save Him, the Lord of the Tremendous Throne.

26.author: Qarai
26.text: Allah—there is no god except Him—is the Lord of the Great Throne.’

26.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
26.text: Allah, there is no god except He, the Lord of the Mighty Throne'

26.author: Saheeh International
26.text: Allah - there is no deity except Him, Lord of the Great Throne."

26.author: Sarwar
26.text: God is the only Lord and master of the Great Throne."

26.author: Shakir
26.text: Allah, there is no god but He: He is the Lord of mighty power.

26.author: Wahiduddin Khan
26.text: He is God: there is no deity but He, the Lord of the mighty throne."

26.author: Yusuf Ali
26.text: "Allah!- there is no god but He!- Lord of the Throne Supreme!"

26.author: Transliteration
26.text: Allahu la ilaha illa huwa rabbu alAAarshi alAAatheemi

27.author: Ahmed Ali
27.text: (Solomon) said: 'We shall see if you speak the truth or you are a liar.

27.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
27.text: Said Sulaiman, “We shall now see whether you spoke the truth or are among the liars.”

27.author: Arberry
27.text: Said he, 'Now We will see whether thou hast spoken truly, or whether thou art amongst those that lie.

27.author: Daryabadi
27.text: Sulaiman said: we shall see now whether thou hast spoken the truth or whether thou art of the liars.

27.author: Hilali & Khan
27.text: [Sulaiman (Solomon)] said: "We shall see whether you speak the truth or you are (one) of the liars.

27.author: Itani
27.text: He said, “We will see, whether you have spoken the truth, or whether you are a liar.

27.author: Maududi
27.text: Solomon said: "Soon shall we see whether you have spoken the truth or are one of those that lie.

27.author: Mubarakpuri
27.text: (Sulayman) said: "We shall see whether you speak the truth or you are (one) of the liars."

27.author: Pickthall
27.text: (Solomon) said: We shall see whether thou speakest truth or whether thou art of the liars.

27.author: Qarai
27.text: He said, ‘We shall see whether you are truthful, or if you are one of the liars.

27.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
27.text: He replied: 'We shall see if what you have said is true or whether you are among those who lie.

27.author: Saheeh International
27.text: [Solomon] said, "We will see whether you were truthful or were of the liars.

27.author: Sarwar
27.text: Solomon said, "We shall see whether you are truthful or a liar.

27.author: Shakir
27.text: He said: We will see whether you have told the truth or whether you are of the liars:

27.author: Wahiduddin Khan
27.text: Solomon said, "We shall soon see whether you have spoken the truth, or whether you are a liar.

27.author: Yusuf Ali
27.text: (Solomon) said: "Soon shall we see whether thou hast told the truth or lied!

27.author: Transliteration
27.text: Qala sananthuru asadaqta am kunta mina alkathibeena

28.author: Ahmed Ali
28.text: Take this letter from me, and deliver it to them and withdraw, then see what reply they give in return."

28.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
28.text: “Go with this letter of mine and drop it upon them – then move aside from them and see what they answer in return.”

28.author: Arberry
28.text: Take this letter of mine, and cast it unto them, then turn back from them and see what they shall return '

28.author: Daryabadi
28.text: Go thou with this epistle of mine, and cast it down unto them, and turn aside from them, and see how they return.

28.author: Hilali & Khan
28.text: "Go you with this letter of mine, and deliver it to them, then draw back from them, and see what (answer) they return."

28.author: Itani
28.text: Go with this letter of mine, and deliver it to them; then withdraw from them, and see how they respond.”

28.author: Maududi
28.text: Take this letter of mine, deliver it to them, and then draw back from them, and observe what they do."

28.author: Mubarakpuri
28.text: "Go you with this letter of mine and deliver it to them, then draw back from them and see what they return."

28.author: Pickthall
28.text: Go with this my letter and throw it down unto them; then turn away and see what (answer) they return,

28.author: Qarai
28.text: Take this letter of mine and deliver it to them. Then draw away from them and observe what [response] they return.’

28.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
28.text: Take my letter, and drop it to them. Then turn aside and see what they shall return'

28.author: Saheeh International
28.text: Take this letter of mine and deliver it to them. Then leave them and see what [answer] they will return."

28.author: Sarwar
28.text: Take this letter of mine and deliver it to them, then return and see what their reply will be."

28.author: Shakir
28.text: Take this my letter and hand it over to them, then turn away from them and see what (answer) they return.

28.author: Wahiduddin Khan
28.text: Go with this letter of mine and lay it before them, then withdraw from them and see how they respond."

28.author: Yusuf Ali
28.text: "Go thou, with this letter of mine, and deliver it to them: then draw back from them, and (wait to) see what answer they return"...

28.author: Transliteration
28.text: Ithhab bikitabee hatha faalqih ilayhim thumma tawalla AAanhum faonthur matha yarjiAAoona

29.author: Ahmed Ali
29.text: (The Queen said): "O nobles, a venerable letter has been delivered to me.

29.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
29.text: The woman said, “O chieftains, indeed a noble letter has been dropped upon me.”

29.author: Arberry
29.text: She said, 'O Council, see, a letter honourable has been cast unto me.

29.author: Daryabadi
29.text: She said: O chiefs! verily there hath been cast unto me an honourable epistle.

29.author: Hilali & Khan
29.text: She said: "O chiefs! Verily! Here is delivered to me a noble letter,

29.author: Itani
29.text: She said, “O Counselors, a gracious letter was delivered to me.

29.author: Maududi
29.text: The Queen said:" Know my nobles that a gracious letter has been delivered to me.

29.author: Mubarakpuri
29.text: She said: "O chiefs! Verily, here is delivered to me a noble letter, "

29.author: Pickthall
29.text: (The Queen of Sheba) said (when she received the letter): O chieftains! Lo! there hath been thrown unto me a noble letter.

29.author: Qarai
29.text: She said, ‘O [members of the] elite! Indeed a noble letter has been delivered to me.

29.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
29.text: She (the Queen of Sheba) said: 'O Council, look, an honorable letter has been dropped to me.

29.author: Saheeh International
29.text: She said, "O eminent ones, indeed, to me has been delivered a noble letter.

29.author: Sarwar
29.text: (The Queen of Sheba) said to her officials, "A gracious letter has been dropped before me.

29.author: Shakir
29.text: She said: O chief! surely an honorable letter has been delivered to me

29.author: Wahiduddin Khan
29.text: The Queen of Sheba said, "O Counsellors, an honourable letter has been delivered to me.

29.author: Yusuf Ali
29.text: (The queen) said: "Ye chiefs! here is delivered to me - a letter worthy of respect.

29.author: Transliteration
29.text: Qalat ya ayyuha almalao innee olqiya ilayya kitabun kareemun

30.author: Ahmed Ali
30.text: It is from Solomon, and (says): 'In the name of Allah, Ar-Rahman, Ar-Rahim.

30.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
30.text: “Indeed it is from Sulaiman, and it is (begins) with ‘Allah – beginning with the name of – the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful.’”

30.author: Arberry
30.text: It is from Solomon, and it is "In the Name of God, the Merciful, the Compassionate.

30.author: Daryabadi
30.text: Verily it is from Sulaiman, and verily it is: in the name of Allah, the Compassionate, the Merciful.

30.author: Hilali & Khan
30.text: "Verily! It is from Sulaiman (Solomon), and verily! It (reads): In the Name of Allah, the Most Beneficent, the Most Merciful;

30.author: Itani
30.text: It is from Solomon, and it is, ‘In the Name of God, the Gracious, the Merciful.

30.author: Maududi
30.text: It is from Solomon, and it is: "In the name of Allah, the Most Merciful, the Most Compassionate."

30.author: Mubarakpuri
30.text: "Verily, it is from Sulayman, and it (reads): `In the Name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful;'

30.author: Pickthall
30.text: Lo! it is from Solomon, and lo! it is: In the name of Allah, the Beneficent, the Merciful;

30.author: Qarai
30.text: It is from Solomon, and it begins in the name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful.

30.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
30.text: It is from Solomon and it is "In the Name of Allah, the Merciful, the Most Merciful.

30.author: Saheeh International
