30.text: Indeed, it is from Solomon, and indeed, it reads: 'In the name of Allah, the Entirely Merciful, the Especially Merciful,

30.author: Sarwar
30.text: It reads, 'From Soloman. In the Name of God, the Beneficent and the Merciful.

30.author: Shakir
30.text: Surely it is from Sulaiman, and surely it is in the name of Allah, the Beneficent, the Merciful;

30.author: Wahiduddin Khan
30.text: It is from Solomon. It reads, "In the name of God, Most Gracious, Most Merciful,

30.author: Yusuf Ali
30.text: "It is from Solomon, and is (as follows): 'In the name of Allah, Most Gracious, Most Merciful:

30.author: Transliteration
30.text: Innahu min sulaymana wainnahu bismi Allahi alrrahmani alrraheemi

31.author: Ahmed Ali
31.text: Do not rise against me, but come to me in submission.'

31.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
31.text: “That ‘Do not wish eminence above me, and present yourselves humbly to me, with submission.’”

31.author: Arberry
31.text: Rise not up against me, but come to me in surrender." '

31.author: Daryabadi
31.text: Saying: exalt not yourselves against me, and come unto me submissive.

31.author: Hilali & Khan
31.text: "Be you not exalted against me, but come to me as Muslims (true believers who submit to Allah with full submission)' "

31.author: Itani
31.text: Do not defy me, and come to me submissively.’”

31.author: Maududi
31.text: (It says): "Do not act towards me with defiance, but come to me in submission."

31.author: Mubarakpuri
31.text: `Be you not exalted against me, but come to me submitting (as Muslims).' "

31.author: Pickthall
31.text: Exalt not yourselves against me, but come unto me as those who surrender.

31.author: Qarai
31.text: [It states,] ‘‘Do not defy me, and come to me in submission.’’ ’

31.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
31.text: Do not rise up against me, but come to me in surrender (Muslims)."

31.author: Saheeh International
31.text: Be not haughty with me but come to me in submission [as Muslims].' "

31.author: Sarwar
31.text: Do not consider yourselves superior to me but come to me as Muslims (in submission)".

31.author: Shakir
31.text: Saying: exalt not yourselves against me and come to me in submission.

31.author: Wahiduddin Khan
31.text: do not exalt yourselves above me, but come to me in all submission."

31.author: Yusuf Ali
31.text: "'Be ye not arrogant against me, but come to me in submission (to the true Religion).'"

32.author: Ahmed Ali
32.text: ." Advise me, O nobles," she said "in this matter. I do not decide any thing until you concur."

32.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
32.text: She said, “O chieftains, advise me in this matter of mine; I do not give a final decision until you are present with me.”

32.author: Arberry
32.text: She said, 'O Council, pronounce to me concerning my affair; I am not used to decide an affair until you bear me witness.'

32.author: Daryabadi
32.text: She said: O chiefs! counsel me in my affair. I am wont not to resolve on any affair until ye are present with me.

32.author: Hilali & Khan
32.text: She said: "O chiefs! Advise me in (this) case of mine. I decide no case till you are present with me."

32.author: Itani
32.text: She said, “O counselors, advise me in this matter of mine. I never make a decision unless you are present.”

32.author: Maududi
32.text: (After reading the letter) the Queen said: "Nobles, let me have your counsel in this matter for I make no firm decision without you."

32.author: Mubarakpuri
32.text: She said: "O chiefs! Advise me in case of mine. I decide no case till you are present with me."

32.author: Pickthall
32.text: She said: O chieftains! Pronounce for me in my case. I decide no case till ye are present with me.

32.author: Qarai
32.text: She said, ‘O [members of the] elite! Give me your opinion concerning my matter. I do not decide any matter until you are present.’

32.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
32.text: She said: 'O Council, let me hear your counsel, concerning my affairs, for I am not used to deciding an affair until you bear me witness'

32.author: Saheeh International
32.text: She said, "O eminent ones, advise me in my affair. I would not decide a matter until you witness [for] me."

32.author: Sarwar
32.text: She said, "My officials, what are your views on this matter? I will not decide until I have your views.

32.author: Shakir
32.text: She said: O chiefs! give me advice respecting my affair: I never decide an affair until you are in my presence.

32.author: Wahiduddin Khan
32.text: Now advise me in this, Counsellors. I never decide any affair till I have conferred with you."

32.author: Yusuf Ali
32.text: She said: "Ye chiefs! advise me in (this) my affair: no affair have I decided except in your presence."

32.author: Transliteration
32.text: Qalat ya ayyuha almalao aftoonee fee amree ma kuntu qatiAAatan amran hatta tashhadoona

33.author: Ahmed Ali
33.text: They said: "We are men of valour, and brave fighters. It is for you to decide. So consider what you should command."

33.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
33.text: They said, “We possess great strength and are great warriors, and the decision is yours, therefore consider what you will command.”

33.author: Arberry
33.text: They said, 'We possess force and we possess great might. The affair rests with thee; so consider what thou wilt command.'

33.author: Daryabadi
33.text: They said: we are owners of power and owners of great violence, but the command is with thee; see then whatsoever thou shalt command.

33.author: Hilali & Khan
33.text: They said: "We have great strength, and great ability for war, but it is for you to command; so think over what you will command."

33.author: Itani
33.text: They said, “We are a people of might and great courage, but the decision is yours, so consider what you wish to command.”

33.author: Maududi
33.text: They said: "We are strong and are given to vehement fighting. But the decision is yours. Therefore, consider what you would like to command."

33.author: Mubarakpuri
33.text: They said: "We have great strength, and great ability for war, but it is for you to command; so think over what you will command."

33.author: Pickthall
33.text: They said: We are lords of might and lords of great prowess, but it is for thee to command; so consider what thou wilt command.

33.author: Qarai
33.text: They said, ‘We are powerful and possess a great might. But it is up to you to command. So consider what orders you will give.’

33.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
33.text: They replied: 'We are possessors of force and great might. It is for you to command, so consider what you will'

33.author: Saheeh International
33.text: They said, "We are men of strength and of great military might, but the command is yours, so see what you will command."

33.author: Sarwar
33.text: They replied, "We have great power and valor. You are the commander, so decide as you like".

33.author: Shakir
33.text: They said: We are possessors of strength and possessors of mighty prowess, and the command is yours, therefore see what you will command.

33.author: Wahiduddin Khan
33.text: They said, "We are strong and our prowess in battle is great, but the decision is in your hands, so consider what you will command."

33.author: Yusuf Ali
33.text: They said: "We are endued with strength, and given to vehement war: but the command is with thee; so consider what thou wilt command."

33.author: Transliteration
33.text: Qaloo nahnu oloo quwwatin waoloo basin shadeedin waalamru ilayki faonthuree matha tamureena

34.author: Ahmed Ali
34.text: She said: "Surely when kings enter a city they destroy it and despoil the honour of its nobility. So will they do (to us).

34.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
34.text: She said, “Indeed the kings, when they enter a township, destroy it and disgrace its honourable people; and this is what they do.”

34.author: Arberry
34.text: She said, 'Kings, when they enter a city, disorder it and make the mighty ones of its inhabitants abased. Even so they too will do.

34.author: Daryabadi
34.text: She said: verily the kings, when they enter a City, despoil it, and make the most powerful inhabitants thereof the most abased; so will they do.

34.author: Hilali & Khan
34.text: She said: "Verily! Kings, when they enter a town (country), they despoil it, and make the most honourable amongst its people low. And thus they do.

34.author: Itani
34.text: She said, “When kings enter a city, they devastate it, and subjugate its dignified people. Thus they always do.

34.author: Maududi
34.text: The Queen said: "When the kings enter a country they cause corruption in it and abase those of its people who are held in honour. This is what they are wont to do.

34.author: Mubarakpuri
34.text: She said: "Verily, kings, when they enter a town, they destroy it and make the most honorable among its people the lowest. And thus they do."

34.author: Pickthall
34.text: She said: Lo! kings, when they enter a township, ruin it and make the honour of its people shame. Thus will they do.

34.author: Qarai
34.text: She said, ‘Indeed when kings enter a town, they devastate it, and make the mightiest of its people the weakest. That is how they act.

34.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
34.text: She said: 'When kings enter a village, they ruin it and humiliate its nobles. And this they will do.

34.author: Saheeh International
34.text: She said, "Indeed kings - when they enter a city, they ruin it and render the honored of its people humbled. And thus do they do.

34.author: Sarwar
34.text: She said, "When Kings enter a town they destroy it and disrespect its honorable people. That is what they will do, too.

34.author: Shakir
34.text: She said: Surely the kings, when they enter a town, ruin it and make the noblest of its people to be low, and thus they (always) do;

34.author: Wahiduddin Khan
34.text: She said, "Surely, when mighty kings invade a country, they despoil it and humiliate its noblest inhabitants, these men will do the same,

34.author: Yusuf Ali
34.text: She said: "Kings, when they enter a country, despoil it, and make the noblest of its people its meanest thus do they behave.

34.author: Transliteration
34.text: Qalat inna almulooka itha dakhaloo qaryatan afsadooha wajaAAaloo aAAizzata ahliha athillatan wakathalika yafAAaloona

35.author: Ahmed Ali
35.text: But I will send them a gift and see what the envoys bring back (in return)."

35.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
35.text: “And I shall send a present to them, then see what reply the envoys bring.”

35.author: Arberry
35.text: Now I will send them a present, and see what the envoys bring back.'

35.author: Daryabadi
35.text: So verily I am going to send a present unto them, and see with what answer the envoys come back.

35.author: Hilali & Khan
35.text: "But verily! I am going to send him a present, and see with what (answer) the messengers return."

35.author: Itani
35.text: I am sending them a gift, and will see what the envoys bring back.”

35.author: Maududi
35.text: I will send them a gift and then see with what answer my envoys return."

35.author: Mubarakpuri
35.text: "But verily, I am going to send him a present, and see with what (answer) the messengers return."

35.author: Pickthall
35.text: But lo! I am going to send a present unto them, and to see with what (answer) the messengers return.

35.author: Qarai
35.text: I will send them a gift, and see what the envoys bring back.’

35.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
35.text: But I shall send them a gift and see what the messengers bring back'

35.author: Saheeh International
35.text: But indeed, I will send to them a gift and see with what [reply] the messengers will return."

35.author: Sarwar
35.text: I will send a gift and we shall see what response the Messengers will bring."

35.author: Shakir
35.text: And surely I am going to send a present to them, and shall wait to see what (answer) do the apostles bring back.

35.author: Wahiduddin Khan
35.text: but I shall send them a present and see with what reply my envoys will return."

35.author: Yusuf Ali
35.text: "But I am going to send him a present, and (wait) to see with what (answer) return (my) ambassadors."

35.author: Transliteration
35.text: Wainnee mursilatun ilayhim bihadiyyatin fanathiratun bima yarjiAAu almursaloona

36.author: Ahmed Ali
36.text: So, when the envoys came to Solomon he said: "Do you wish to increase my wealth? Yet what God has given me is better than what He has given you. No. Be gratified in your present.

36.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
36.text: So when the envoy came to Sulaiman, he said, “Are you helping me with wealth? What Allah has bestowed upon me is better than what He has given you; rather it is you who are delighted at your gift.”

36.author: Arberry
36.text: But when he came to Solomon he said, 'What, would you succour me with wealth, and what God gave me is better than what He has given you? Nay, but instead you rejoice in your gift!

36.author: Daryabadi
36.text: Then when he came unto Sulaiman, he said: are ye going to add riches to me. Then that which Allah hath vouchsafed unto me is better than that which He hath vouchsafed unto you. Aye! it is ye who exult in your present.

36.author: Hilali & Khan
36.text: So when (the messengers with the present) came to Sulaiman (Solomon), he said: "Will you help me in wealth? What Allah has given me is better than that which He has given you! Nay, you rejoice in your gift!"

36.author: Itani
36.text: When he came to Solomon, he said, “Are you supplying me with money? What God has given me is better than what He has given you. It is you who delight in your gift.

36.author: Maududi
36.text: Now, when (the envoy of the Queen) came to Solomon, he said: "Do you want to aid me with wealth? Whatever Allah has granted me is much more than what He has given you. (Keep for yourselves) your gift in which you are exulting.

36.author: Mubarakpuri
36.text: So, when (the messengers with the gift) came to Sulayman, he said: "Will you help me in wealth What Allah has given me is better than that which He has given you! Nay, you rejoice in your gift!"

36.author: Pickthall
36.text: So when (the envoy) came unto Solomon, (the King) said: What! Would ye help me with wealth? But that which Allah hath given me is better than that which He hath given you. Nay it is ye (and not I) who exult in your gift.

36.author: Qarai
36.text: So when he came to Solomon, he said, ‘Are you aiding me with wealth? What Allah has given me is better than what He has given you. Indeed, you are proud of your gift!

36.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
36.text: But when he came to Solomon, he said: 'Is it wealth that you would give me, when Allah has given me that which is better than He has given to you? No, but instead you rejoice in your gift!

36.author: Saheeh International
36.text: So when they came to Solomon, he said, "Do you provide me with wealth? But what Allah has given me is better than what He has given you. Rather, it is you who rejoice in your gift.

36.author: Sarwar
36.text: When her Messengers came to Soloman, he said, "Have you brought me wealth? What God has given to me is far better than what He has given to you, but you are happy with your gifts.

36.author: Shakir
36.text: So when he came to Sulaiman, he said: What! will you help me with wealth? But what Allah has given me is better than what He has given you. Nay, you are exultant because of your present;

36.author: Wahiduddin Khan
36.text: So when [the envoy] came to Solomon he said, "What! Are you offering me wealth? But that which God has given me is better than that which He has given you. Yet you rejoice in your gift.

36.author: Yusuf Ali
36.text: Now when (the embassy) came to Solomon, he said: "Will ye give me abundance in wealth? But that which Allah has given me is better than that which He has given you! Nay it is ye who rejoice in your gift!

36.author: Transliteration
36.text: Falamma jaa sulaymana qala atumiddoonani bimalin fama ataniya Allahu khayrun mimma atakum bal antum bihadiyyatikum tafrahoona

37.author: Ahmed Ali
37.text: Go back to them. We shall soon come with our armies which they will not be able to face. We shall drive them out of (the land) with ignominy, and they will be humbled."

37.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
37.text: “Go back to them – so we shall indeed come upon them with an army they cannot fight, and degrading them shall certainly drive them out from that city, so they will be humiliated.”

37.author: Arberry
37.text: Return thou to them; we shall assuredly come against them with hosts they have not power to resist, and we shall expel them from there, abased and utterly humbled.'

37.author: Daryabadi
37.text: Go back unto them. Then surely we shall come unto them with hosts which they cannot withstand, and we shall drive them forth therefrom abased and they shall be humbled.

37.author: Hilali & Khan
37.text: [Then Sulaiman (Solomon) said to the chief of her messengers who brought the present]: "Go back to them. We verily shall come to them with hosts that they cannot resist, and we shall drive them out from there in disgrace, and they will be abased."

37.author: Itani
37.text: Go back to them. We will come upon them with troops they cannot resist; and we will expel them from there, disgraced and humiliated.”

37.author: Maududi
37.text: Envoy, go back to those who sent you and we shall certainly come upon them with hosts whom they will be unable to resist. We shall drive them out from there, and they will suffer humiliation and disgrace."

37.author: Mubarakpuri
37.text: "Go back to them. We verily, shall come to them with armies that they cannot resist, and we shall drive them out from there in disgrace, and they will be abased."

37.author: Pickthall
37.text: Return unto them. We verily shall come unto them with hosts that they cannot resist, and we shall drive them out from thence with shame, and they will be abased.

37.author: Qarai
37.text: Go back to them, for we will come at them with hosts which they cannot face, and we will expel them from it, abased and degraded.’

37.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
37.text: Go back then, we shall surely come against them with soldiers of which they have no power to oppose, and we shall drive them from there abased and humiliated'

37.author: Saheeh International
37.text: Return to them, for we will surely come to them with soldiers that they will be powerless to encounter, and we will surely expel them therefrom in humiliation, and they will be debased."

37.author: Sarwar
37.text: Go back to your people and we shall soon come there with an army which they will not be able to face. We shall drive them from their town, humble, and disgraced."

37.author: Shakir
37.text: Go back to them, so we will most certainly come to them with hosts which they shall have no power to oppose, and we will most certainly expel them therefrom in abasement, and they shall be in a state of ignominy.

37.author: Wahiduddin Khan
37.text: Go back to them: we shall most certainly come upon them with forces which they will never be able to withstand, and shall most certainly cause them to be driven from their lands, disgraced and humbled!"

37.author: Yusuf Ali
37.text: "Go back to them, and be sure we shall come to them with such hosts as they will never be able to meet: We shall expel them from there in disgrace, and they will feel humbled (indeed)."

37.author: Transliteration
37.text: IrjiAA ilayhim falanatiyannahum bijunoodin la qibala lahum biha walanukhrijannahum minha athillatan wahum saghiroona

38.author: Ahmed Ali
38.text: He (then) said (to his courtiers): "O you nobles, is there any one who can bring me her throne before they come to me in submission?"

38.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
38.text: Said Sulaiman, “O court members, which one of you can bring me her throne before they come humbled in my presence?”

38.author: Arberry
38.text: He said, 'O Council, which one of you will bring me her throne, before they come to me in surrender?'

38.author: Daryabadi
38.text: He said: O chiefs! which of you will bring unto me her throne, ere they come unto me submitting themselves?

38.author: Hilali & Khan
38.text: He said: "O chiefs! Which of you can bring me her throne before they come to me surrendering themselves in obedience?"

38.author: Itani
38.text: He said, “O notables, which one of you will bring me her throne before they come to me in submission?”

38.author: Maududi
38.text: Solomon said: "My nobles, which of you can bring me her throne before they come to me in submission?"

38.author: Mubarakpuri
38.text: He said: "O chiefs! Which of you can bring me her throne before they come to me surrendering themselves in obedience (as Muslims)"

38.author: Pickthall
38.text: He said: O chiefs! Which of you will bring me her throne before they come unto me, surrendering?

38.author: Qarai
38.text: He said, ‘O [members of the] elite! Which of you will bring me her throne before they come to me in submission?’

38.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
38.text: And he said: 'O Council, which of you will bring me her throne, before they come to me, Muslims (submissive to Allah)'

38.author: Saheeh International
38.text: [Solomon] said, "O assembly [of jinn], which of you will bring me her throne before they come to me in submission?"

38.author: Sarwar
38.text: Solomon asked his people, "Who among you can bring her throne before (she, the queen of Sheba) comes to me submissively?"

38.author: Shakir
38.text: He said: O chiefs! which of you can bring to me her throne before they come to me in submission?

38.author: Wahiduddin Khan
38.text: Solomon then said, "O Counsellors,  which of you can bring me her throne before they come to me in submission?

38.author: Yusuf Ali
38.text: He said (to his own men): "Ye chiefs! which of you can bring me her throne before they come to me in submission?"

38.author: Transliteration
38.text: Qala ya ayyuha almalao ayyukum yateenee biAAarshiha qabla an yatoonee muslimeena

39.author: Ahmed Ali
39.text: A crafty jinn said: "I will bring it before you rise from your seat, for I am strong and trustworthy."

39.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
39.text: An extremely evil jinn said, “I will bring it in your presence before you disperse the assembly; and I am indeed strong and trustworthy upon it.”

39.author: Arberry
39.text: An efreet of the jinns said, 'I will bring it to thee, before thou risest from thy place; I have strength for it

39.author: Daryabadi
39.text: A giant from the jinns said: I shall bring it unto thee ere thou arisest from thy place; verily I am strong for it and trusty.

39.author: Hilali & Khan
39.text: An Ifrit (strong) from the jinns said: "I will bring it to you before you rise from your place (council). And verily, I am indeed strong, and trustworthy for such work."

39.author: Itani
39.text: An imp of the sprites said, “I will bring it to you before you rise from your seat. I am strong and reliable enough to do it.”

39.author: Maududi
39.text: A stalwart of the jinn said: "I will bring it to you before you rise from your council. Surely I have the power to do so and I am trustworthy."

39.author: Mubarakpuri
39.text: An `Ifrit from the Jinn said: "I will bring it to you before you rise from your place. And verily, I am indeed strong and trustworthy for such work."

39.author: Pickthall
39.text: A stalwart of the jinn said: I will bring it thee before thou canst rise from thy place. Lo! I verily am strong and trusty for such work.

39.author: Qarai
39.text: An afreet from among the jinn said, ‘I will bring it to you before you rise from your place. I have the power to do it and am trustworthy.’

39.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
39.text: An efreet (an extremely strong jinn) among the jinn replied: 'I will bring it to you before you rise from your place; I have the strength and am trustworthy'

39.author: Saheeh International
39.text: A powerful one from among the jinn said, "I will bring it to you before you rise from your place, and indeed, I am for this [task] strong and trustworthy."

39.author: Sarwar
39.text: A monstrous jinn said, "I can bring it before you even stand up. I am powerful and trustworthy".

39.author: Shakir
39.text: One audacious among the jinn said: I will bring it to you before you rise up from your place; and most surely I am strong (and) trusty for it.

39.author: Wahiduddin Khan
39.text: A demon from among the jinn said, "I will bring it to you before you get up from your seat. I am strong and trustworthy enough to do it."

39.author: Yusuf Ali
39.text: Said an 'Ifrit, of the Jinns: "I will bring it to thee before thou rise from thy council: indeed I have full strength for the purpose, and may be trusted."

39.author: Transliteration
39.text: Qala AAifreetun mina aljinni ana ateeka bihi qabla an taqooma min maqamika wainne AAalayhi laqawiyyun ameenun

40.author: Ahmed Ali
40.text: But one who had knowledge of the letter, said: "I will bring it to you in the twinkling of an eye." When Solomon saw it before him, (he said): "This is by the grace of my Lord that He may test me whether I am grateful or I am thankless. Yet if one is grateful, he is grateful for himself, and if one is thankless, then surely my Lord is unconcerned and magnanimous."

40.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
40.text: Said one who had knowledge of the Book, “I will bring it in your majesty’s presence before you bat your eyelid”; then when he saw it set in his presence*, Sulaiman said, “This is of the favours of my Lord; so that He may test me whether I give thanks or am ungrateful; and whoever gives thanks only gives thanks for his own good; and whoever is ungrateful – then indeed my Lord is the Independent (Not Needing Anything), the Owner Of All Praise.” (A miracle which occurred through one of Allah’s friends.)

40.author: Arberry
40.text: and I am trusty.' Said he who possessed knowledge of the Book, 'I will bring it to thee, before ever thy glance returns to thee.' Then, when he saw it settled before him, he said, 'This is of my Lord's bounty that He may try me, whether I am thankful or ungrateful. Whosoever gives thanks gives thanks only for his own soul's good, and whosoever is ungrateful -- my Lord is surely All-sufficient, All-generous.'

40.author: Daryabadi
40.text: The one who had some knowledge of the Book said: I shall bring it unto thee ere thy eye twinkleth. Then when he saw it placed before him, he said: this is of the grace of my Lord that he may prove me whether I give thanks or am ungrateful. Whosoever giveth thanks he only giveth thanks for his own soul; and whosoever is ungrateful then verily my Lord is Self-sufficient, Munificent.

40.author: Hilali & Khan
40.text: One with whom was knowledge of the Scripture said: "I will bring it to you within the twinkling of an eye!" then when [Sulaiman (Solomon)] saw it placed before him, he said: "This is by the Grace of my Lord to test me whether I am grateful or ungrateful! And whoever is grateful, truly, his gratitude is for (the good of) his ownself, and whoever is ungrateful, (he is ungrateful only for the loss of his ownself). Certainly! My Lord is Rich (Free of all wants), Bountiful."

40.author: Itani
40.text: He who had knowledge from the Book said, “I will bring it to you before your glance returns to you.” And when he saw it settled before him, he said, “This is from the grace of my Lord, to test me, whether I am grateful or ungrateful. He who is grateful, his gratitude is to his own credit; but he who is ungrateful—my Lord is Independent and Generous.”

40.author: Maududi
40.text: And he who had some knowledge of the Book said: "I will bring it before the twinkling of your eye." When Solomon saw the throne placed firmly beside him, he cried out: "This is by the grace of my Lord so that He may test me whether I give thanks for (His Bounty) or act with ingratitude. Whoever is grateful is so to his own good; and whoever is ungrateful, let him know that my Lord is Immensely Resourceful, Most Bountiful."

40.author: Mubarakpuri
40.text: One with whom was knowledge of the Scripture, said: "I will bring it to you within the twinkling of an eye!" Then when he saw it placed before him, he said: "This is by the grace of my Lord -- to test me whether I am grateful or ungrateful! And whoever is grateful, truly, his gratitude is for himself; and whoever is ungrateful, certainly my Lord is Rich, Bountiful."

40.author: Pickthall
40.text: One with whom was knowledge of the Scripture said: I will bring it thee before thy gaze returneth unto thee. And when he saw it set in his presence, (Solomon) said: This is of the bounty of my Lord, that He may try me whether I give thanks or am ungrateful. Whosoever giveth thanks he only giveth thanks for (the good of) his own soul; and whosoever is ungrateful (is ungrateful only to his own soul's hurt). For lo! my Lord is Absolute in independence, Bountiful.

40.author: Qarai
40.text: The one who had knowledge of the Book said, ‘I will bring it to you in the twinkling of an eye.’ So when he saw it set near him, he said, ‘This is by the grace of my Lord, to test me if I will give thanks or be ungrateful. Whoever gives thanks, gives thanks only for his own sake. And whoever is ungrateful [should know that] my Lord is indeed all-sufficient, all-generous.’

40.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
40.text: But he who had knowledge of the Book, said: 'I will bring it to you before your glance comes back to you' And when he saw it set before him, he (Solomon) said: 'This is a favor from my Lord that He might test me whether I am thankful or ungrateful. Whosoever gives thanks gives thanks only for his (own soul's) good, but he who is ungrateful, truly my Lord is Rich and Generous'

40.author: Saheeh International
40.text: Said one who had knowledge from the Scripture, "I will bring it to you before your glance returns to you." And when [Solomon] saw it placed before him, he said, "This is from the favor of my Lord to test me whether I will be grateful or ungrateful. And whoever is grateful - his gratitude is only for [the benefit of] himself. And whoever is ungrateful - then indeed, my Lord is Free of need and Generous."

40.author: Sarwar
40.text: The one who had knowledge from the Book said, "I can bring it to you before you even blink your eye." When Solomon saw the throne placed before him, he said, "This is a favor from my Lord by which He wants to test whether I am grateful or ungrateful. Whoever thanks God does so for his own good. Whoever is ungrateful to God should know that my Lord is Self-Sufficient and Benevolent."

40.author: Shakir
40.text: One who had the knowledge of the Book said: I will bring it to you in the twinkling of an eye. Then when he saw it settled beside him, he said: This is of the grace of my Lord that He may try me whether I am grateful or ungrateful; and whoever is grateful, he is grateful only for his own soul, and whoever is ungrateful, then surely my Lord is Self-sufficient, Honored.

40.author: Wahiduddin Khan
40.text: But one of them who had some knowledge of the Book said, "I will bring it to you in the twinkling of an eye." When Solomon saw it placed before him, he exclaimed, "This is by the grace of my Lord, to test whether I am grateful or ungrateful. Whosoever is grateful, it is for the good of his own self; and whosoever is ungrateful, then surely my Lord is self-sufficient and generous."

40.author: Yusuf Ali
40.text: Said one who had knowledge of the Book: "I will bring it to thee within the twinkling of an eye!" Then when (Solomon) saw it placed firmly before him, he said: "This is by the Grace of my Lord!- to test me whether I am grateful or ungrateful! and if any is grateful, truly his gratitude is (a gain) for his own soul; but if any is ungrateful, truly my Lord is Free of all Needs, Supreme in Honour!"

40.author: Transliteration
40.text: Qala allathee AAindahu AAilmun mina alkitabi ana ateeka bihi qabla an yartadda ilayka tarfuka falamma raahu mustaqirran AAindahu qala hatha min fadli rabbee liyabluwanee aashkuru am akfuru waman shakara fainnama yashkuru linafsihi waman kafara fainna rabbee ghaniyyun kareemun

41.author: Ahmed Ali
41.text: (Turning to his nobles) he said: "Change the appearance of her throne. Let us see if she is rightly guided, or is not guided at all."

41.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
41.text: Said Sulaiman, “Disguise her throne in front of her so that we may see whether she finds the way* or becomes of those who remain unknowing.” (*Recognises her throne.)

41.author: Arberry
41.text: He said, 'Disguise her throne for her, and we shall behold whether she is guided or if she is of those that are not guided.'

41.author: Daryabadi
41.text: He said: disguise for her her throne, that we may see whether she be guided or be of those who are not guided.

41.author: Hilali & Khan
41.text: He said: "Disguise her throne for her that we may see whether she will be guided (to recognise her throne), or she will be one of those not guided."

41.author: Itani
41.text: He said, “Disguise her throne for her, and we shall see whether she will be guided, or remains one of the misguided.”

41.author: Maududi
41.text: Solomon said: " Set the throne before her casually, and let us see whether she gets to the Truth or is one of those who are not guided to what is right."

41.author: Mubarakpuri
41.text: He said: "Disguise her throne for her that we may see whether she will be guided, or she will be one of those not guided."

41.author: Pickthall
41.text: He said: Disguise her throne for her that we may see whether she will go aright or be of those not rightly guided.

41.author: Qarai
41.text: He said, ‘Disguise her throne for her, so that we may see whether she is discerning or if she is one of the undiscerning ones.’

41.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
41.text: (Then) he said: 'Let her throne be disguised, so that we can see whether she is guided or if she is among those who are not guided'

41.author: Saheeh International
41.text: He said, "Disguise for her her throne; we will see whether she will be guided [to truth] or will be of those who is not guided."

41.author: Sarwar
41.text: Then he said, "Make a few changes to her throne and let us see whether she will recognize it or not."

41.author: Shakir
41.text: He said: Alter her throne for her, we will see whether she follows the right way or is of those who do not go aright.

41.author: Wahiduddin Khan
41.text: He said, "Disguise her throne. We shall see whether or not she will recognize it."

41.author: Yusuf Ali
41.text: He said: "Transform her throne out of all recognition by her: let us see whether she is guided (to the truth) or is one of those who receive no guidance."

41.author: Transliteration
41.text: Qala nakkiroo laha AAarshaha nanthur atahtadee am takoonu mina allatheena la yahtadoona

42.author: Ahmed Ali
42.text: When she arrived, they asked her: "Is your throne like this?" She said: "As though this is it. We had come to have knowledge and already submitted."

42.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
42.text: Then when she came, it was said to her, “Is your throne like this? She said, “As if this is it! And we came to know about this incident beforehand and submit (to you).”

42.author: Arberry
42.text: So, when she came, it was said, 'Is thy throne like this?' She said, 'It seems the same.' 'And we were given the knowledge before her, and we were in surrender,

42.author: Daryabadi
42.text: Then when she arrived, it was said: is thy throne like unto? She said: it is as though it were it; and we have been vouchsafed the knowledge before this, and we have been Muslims.

42.author: Hilali & Khan
42.text: So when she came, it was said (to her): "Is your throne like this?" She said: "(It is) as though it were the very same." And [Sulaiman (Solomon) said]: "Knowledge was bestowed on us before her, and we were submitted to Allah (in Islam as Muslims before her)."

42.author: Itani
42.text: When she arrived, it was said, “Is your throne like this?” She said, “As if this is it.” “We were given knowledge before her, and we were submissive.”

42.author: Maududi
42.text: When the Queen arrived, she was asked: "Is your throne like this one?" She said: "It seems as if it is the same. We had already come to know this and we had submitted ourselves."

42.author: Mubarakpuri
42.text: So when she came, it was said, "Is your throne like this" She said: "As though it were the very same." And he said, "Knowledge was bestowed on us before her, and we had submitted to Allah (as Muslims)."

42.author: Pickthall
42.text: So, when she came, it was said (unto her): Is thy throne like this? She said: (It is) as though it were the very one. And (Solomon said): We were given the knowledge before her and we had surrendered (to Allah).

42.author: Qarai
42.text: So when she came, it was said [to her], ‘Is your throne like this one?’ She said, ‘It seems to be the same, and we were informed before it, and we had submitted.’

42.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
42.text: And when she came she was asked: 'Is your throne like this' And she replied: 'It looks like it' And we were given the knowledge before her, and wereMuslims.

42.author: Saheeh International
42.text: So when she arrived, it was said [to her], "Is your throne like this?" She said, "[It is] as though it was it." [Solomon said], "And we were given knowledge before her, and we have been Muslims [in submission to Allah].

42.author: Sarwar
42.text: When she came she was asked, "Is your throne like this?" She replied, "It seems that this is it. We had received the knowledge before this and were submissive (to Solomon's power)".

42.author: Shakir
42.text: So when she came, it was said: Is your throne like this? She said: It is as it were the same, and we were given the knowledge before it, and we were submissive.

42.author: Wahiduddin Khan
42.text: When she came to Solomon, she was asked, "Is your throne like this?" She replied, "It looks as though it were the same, and we had been given knowledge [of your power] before this, and we have already submitted."

42.author: Yusuf Ali
42.text: So when she arrived, she was asked, "Is this thy throne?" She said, "It was just like this; and knowledge was bestowed on us in advance of this, and we have submitted to Allah (in Islam)."

42.author: Transliteration
42.text: Falamma jaat qeela ahakatha AAarshuki qalat kaannahu huwa waooteena alAAilma min qabliha wakunna muslimeena

43.author: Ahmed Ali
43.text: She was (in fact) turned away by what she worshipped other than God, for she came of an unbelieving people.

43.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
43.text: And the thing she used to worship instead of Allah prevented her; she was indeed from the disbelieving people.

43.author: Arberry
43.text: but that she served, apart from God, barred her, for she was of a people of unbelievers.'

43.author: Daryabadi
43.text: And that which she was wont to worship instead of Allah hindered her; verily she was of an infidel people.

43.author: Hilali & Khan
43.text: And that which she used to worship besides Allah has prevented her (from Islam), for she was of a disbelieving people.

43.author: Itani
43.text: But she was prevented by what she worshiped besides God; she belonged to a disbelieving people.

43.author: Maududi
43.text: What prevented her (from accepting the True Faith) was her worshipping deities other than Allah, for she belonged to an unbelieving people.

43.author: Mubarakpuri
43.text: And Saddaha that which she used to worship besides Allah has prevented her (from Islam), for she was of a disbelieving people.

43.author: Pickthall
43.text: And (all) that she was wont to worship instead of Allah hindered her, for she came of disbelieving folk.

43.author: Qarai
43.text: She had been barred [from the way of Allah] by what she used to worship besides Allah, for she belonged to a faithless people.

43.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
43.text: That which she worshipped, other than Allah, had prevented her, for she came from an unbelieving nation.

43.author: Saheeh International
43.text: And that which she was worshipping other than Allah had averted her [from submission to Him]. Indeed, she was from a disbelieving people."

43.author: Sarwar
43.text: Her idols prevented her from believing in God and she was an infidel.

43.author: Shakir
43.text: And what she worshipped besides Allah prevented her, surely she was of an unbelieving people.

43.author: Wahiduddin Khan
43.text: And that which she used to worship beside God had stopped her [from believing]; for she came of a disbelieving people.

43.author: Yusuf Ali
43.text: And he diverted her from the worship of others besides Allah: for she was (sprung) of a people that had no faith.

43.author: Transliteration
43.text: Wasaddaha ma kanat taAAbudu min dooni Allahi innaha kanat min qawmin kafireena

44.author: Ahmed Ali
44.text: She was invited to enter the court. When she saw it, she took it for a sheet of water, and (pulling up her skirts) uncovered her legs. (Solomon) told her: "This is paved with tiles of glass." "O Lord," she said, "I have wronged myself, and I submit to the Lord of all the worlds with Solomon."

44.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
44.text: It was said to her, “Enter the hall”; and when she saw it she thought it was a pool and bared her shins*; said Sulaiman, “This is only a smooth hall, affixed with glass”; she said, “My Lord, I have indeed wronged myself, and I now submit myself along with Sulaiman to Allah, the Lord Of The Creation.” (* In order to cross it)

44.author: Arberry
44.text: It was said to her, 'Enter the pavilion.' But when she saw it, she supposed it was a spreading water, and she bared her legs. He said, 'It is a pavilion smoothed of crystal. She said, 'My Lord, indeed I have wronged myself, and I surrender with Solomon to God, the Lord of all Being.'

44.author: Daryabadi
44.text: It was said unto her: enter the palace. Then when she saw it,, he deemed it a pool and bared her shanks. He said: verily it is a palace evenly floored with glass. She said: my Lord! verily I wronged my soul, and now submit myself together with Sulaiman unto Allah, the Lord of the Worlds.

44.author: Hilali & Khan
44.text: It was said to her: "Enter As-Sarh" [(a glass surface with water underneath it) or a palace], but when she saw it, she thought it was a pool, and she (tucked up her clothes) uncovering her legs, Sulaiman (Solomon) said: "Verily, it is Sarh [(a glass surface with water underneath it) or a palace] paved smooth with slab of glass." She said: "My Lord! Verily, I have wronged myself, and I submit (in Islam, together with Sulaiman (Solomon), to Allah, the Lord of the 'Alamin (mankind, jinns and all that exists)."

44.author: Itani
44.text: It was said to her, “Go inside the palace.” And when she saw it, she thought it was a deep pond, and she bared her legs. He said, “It is a palace paved with glass.” She said, “My Lord, I have done wrong to myself, and I have submitted with Solomon, to God, Lord of the Worlds.”

44.author: Maududi
44.text: She was told: "Enter the palace." But when she saw it, she thought it was a pool of water and she bared both her calves (to enter into it). Solomon said: "This is a slippery floor of crystal." Thereupon she cried out: "My Lord, I have been inflicting much wrong on myself. Now I submit myself with Solomon to Allah, the Lord of the whole Universe."

44.author: Mubarakpuri
44.text: It was said to her: "Enter As-Sarh," but when she saw it, she thought it was a pool, and she (tucked up her clothes) uncovering her legs. Sulayman said: "Verily, it is a Sarh Mumarrad of Qawarir." She said: "My Lord! Verily, I have wronged myself, and I submit, together with Sulayman to Allah, the Lord of all that exits."

44.author: Pickthall
44.text: It was said unto her: Enter the hall. And when she saw it she deemed it a pool and bared her legs. (Solomon) said: Lo! it is a hall, made smooth, of glass. She said: My Lord! Lo! I have wronged myself, and I surrender with Solomon unto Allah, the Lord of the Worlds.

44.author: Qarai
44.text: It was said to her, ‘Enter the palace.’ So when she saw it, she supposed it to be a pool of water, and she bared her shanks. He said, ‘It is a palace paved with crystal.’ She said, ‘My Lord! Indeed I have wronged myself, and I submit with Solomon to Allah, the Lord of all the worlds.’

44.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
44.text: It was said to her: 'Enter the pavilion' And when she saw it, she thought it was a pool of water, and bared her legs. But he said: 'It is a pavilion smoothed with crystal' She said: 'My Lord, I have wronged myself, and I become a Muslim (submissive) with Solomon to Allah, Lord of the Worlds'

44.author: Saheeh International
44.text: She was told, "Enter the palace." But when she saw it, she thought it was a body of water and uncovered her shins [to wade through]. He said, "Indeed, it is a palace [whose floor is] made smooth with glass." She said, "My Lord, indeed I have wronged myself, and I submit with Solomon to Allah, Lord of the worlds."

44.author: Sarwar
44.text: She was told to enter the palace. When she saw it, she thought that it was a pool and raised her clothe up to her legs. Solomon said, "This is a palace constructed with glass." She said, "My Lord, indeed I have wronged myself and I submit myself with Solomon to the will of God, the Lord of the Universe."

44.author: Shakir
44.text: It was said to her: Enter the palace; but when she saw it she deemed it to be a great expanse of water, and bared her legs. He said: Surely it is a palace made smooth with glass. She said: My Lord! surely I have been unjust to myself, and I submit with Sulaiman to Allah, the Lord of the worlds.

44.author: Wahiduddin Khan
44.text: Then she was bidden to enter the palace; but when she saw it, she thought it was a deep pool of water, and bared her legs. But Solomon explained, "It is just a palace paved with glass," and she said, "My Lord, I have wronged myself: now I submit myself along with Solomon, to God, the Lord of the Universe."

44.author: Yusuf Ali
44.text: She was asked to enter the lofty Palace: but when she saw it, she thought it was a lake of water, and she (tucked up her skirts), uncovering her legs. He said: "This is but a palace paved smooth with slabs of glass." She said: "O my Lord! I have indeed wronged my soul: I do (now) submit (in Islam), with Solomon, to the Lord of the Worlds."

44.author: Transliteration
44.text: Qeela laha odkhulee alssarha falamma raathu hasibathu lujjatan wakashafat AAan saqayha qala innahu sarhun mumarradun min qawareera qalat rabbi innee thalamtu nafsee waaslamtu maAAa sulaymana lillahi rabbi alAAalameena

45.author: Ahmed Ali
45.text: We sent to Thamud their brother Saleh (who said): "Worship God." But they were divided into two groups disputing with one another.

45.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
45.text: And indeed We sent to the Thamud, their fellowman Saleh that, “Worship Allah”, thereupon they became two parties quarrelling.

45.author: Arberry
45.text: And We sent to Thamood their brother Salih: 'Serve you God!' And behold, they were two parties, that were disputing one with another.

45.author: Daryabadi
45.text: And assuredly We sent unto the Thamud their brother Saleh saying: worship Allah. Then lo! they became two parties contending.

45.author: Hilali & Khan
45.text: And indeed We sent to Thamud their brother Salih (Saleh), saying: "Worship Allah (Alone and none else). Then look! They became two parties (believers and disbelievers) quarreling with each other."

45.author: Itani
45.text: And We sent to Thamood their brother Saleh: “Worship God.” But they became two disputing factions.

45.author: Maududi
45.text: And We sent to Thamud their brother Salih (with the Message): "Serve Allah," but all of a sudden they became split into two quarrelling factions.

45.author: Mubarakpuri
45.text: And indeed We sent to Thamud their brother Salih, (saying): "Worship Allah." Then look! They became two parties quarreling with each other.

45.author: Pickthall
45.text: And We verily sent unto Thamud their brother Salih, saying: Worship Allah. And lo! they (then became two parties quarrelling.

45.author: Qarai
45.text: Certainly We sent to Thamud Salih, their brother, [with the summons:] ‘Worship Allah!’ But thereat they became two groups contending with each other.

45.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
45.text: To Thamood We sent their brother Salih saying: 'Worship Allah' But they were two parties in dispute with one another.

45.author: Saheeh International
45.text: And We had certainly sent to Thamud their brother Salih, [saying], "Worship Allah," and at once they were two parties conflicting.

45.author: Sarwar
45.text: We sent to the tribe of Thamud their brother Salih so that they would worship God, but they became two quarrelling groups.

45.author: Shakir
45.text: And certainly We sent to Samood their brother Salih, saying: Serve Allah; and lo! they became two sects quarrelling with each other.

45.author: Wahiduddin Khan
45.text: To Thamud We sent their brother Salih. He said, "Serve none but God." But they divided themselves into two factions contending with one another.

45.author: Yusuf Ali
45.text: We sent (aforetime), to the Thamud, their brother Salih, saying, "Serve Allah": But behold, they became two factions quarrelling with each other.

45.author: Transliteration
45.text: Walaqad arsalna ila thamooda akhahum salihan ani oAAbudoo Allaha faitha hum fareeqani yakhtasimoona

46.author: Ahmed Ali
46.text: (Saleh) said: "O people, why do you wish to hasten evil rather than good? Why not ask for God's forgiveness? You may well be forgiven."

46.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
46.text: He said, “O my people, why do you hasten upon the evil before the good? Why do you not seek forgiveness from Allah? Perhaps there may be mercy upon you.”

46.author: Arberry
46.text: He said, 'O my people, why do you seek to hasten evil before good? Why do you not ask forgiveness of God? Haply so you will find mercy.'

46.author: Daryabadi
46.text: He said: O my people! wherefore seek ye to hasten the evil before the good? Wherefore ask ye not forgiveness of Allah, that haply ye may be shewn mercy.

46.author: Hilali & Khan
46.text: He said: "O my people! Why do you seek to hasten the evil (torment) before the good (Allah's Mercy)? Why seek you not the Forgiveness of Allah, that you may receive mercy?"

46.author: Itani
46.text: He said, “O my people, why are you quick to do evil rather than good? If only you would seek God’s forgiveness, so that you may be shown mercy.”

46.author: Maududi
46.text: Salih said: "My people, why do you wish to hasten that evil rather than good should come upon you? Why should you not seek pardon from Allah so that mercy be shown to you?"

46.author: Mubarakpuri
46.text: He said: "O my people! Why do you seek to hasten the evil before the good Why seek you not the forgiveness of Allah, that you may receive mercy"

46.author: Pickthall
46.text: He said: O my people! Why will ye hasten on the evil rather than the good? Why will ye not ask pardon of Allah, that ye may receive mercy.

46.author: Qarai
46.text: He said, ‘O My people! Why do you press for evil sooner than for good? Why do you not plead to Allah for forgiveness so that you may receive His mercy?’

46.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
46.text: 'My nation' he said, 'why do you wish to hasten evil rather than good? Why do you not ask the forgiveness of Allah, in order that you find mercy'

46.author: Saheeh International
46.text: He said, "O my people, why are you impatient for evil instead of good? Why do you not seek forgiveness of Allah that you may receive mercy?"

46.author: Sarwar
46.text: Salih said, "My people, why do you commit sins so quickly before doing good? Would that you ask forgiveness from God so that perhaps He will have mercy upon you."

46.author: Shakir
46.text: He said: O my people! why do you seek to hasten on the evil before the good? Why do you not ask forgiveness of Allah so that you may be dealt with mercifully?

46.author: Wahiduddin Khan
46.text: He urged them, "O my people, why do you wish to hasten on the evil rather than the good? Why do you not ask forgiveness of God, so that you may be shown mercy?"

46.author: Yusuf Ali
46.text: He said: "O my people! why ask ye to hasten on the evil in preference to the good? If only ye ask Allah for forgiveness, ye may hope to receive mercy.

46.author: Transliteration
46.text: Qala ya qawmi lima tastaAAjiloona bialssayyiati qabla alhasanati lawla tastaghfiroona Allaha laAAallakum turhamoona

47.author: Ahmed Ali
47.text: They said: "You betoken evil, and those with you." (Saleh) replied: "The evil you presage can only come from God. In reality you are a people under trial (for your own inauspiciousness)."

47.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
47.text: They said, “We consider you an evil omen, and your companions”; he said, “Your evil omen is with Allah – in fact you people have fallen into trial.”

47.author: Arberry
47.text: They said, 'We augur ill of thee and of those that are with thee.' He said, 'Your augury is with God; nay, but you are a people being proved.'

47.author: Daryabadi
47.text: They said: we augur ill of thee and of those who are with thee. He said: your augury is with Allah. Aye! ye are a people being tested.

47.author: Hilali & Khan
47.text: They said: "We augur ill omen from you and those with you." He said: "Your ill omen is with Allah; nay, but you are a people that are being tested."

47.author: Itani
47.text: They said, “We consider you an ill omen, and those with you.” He said, “Your omen is with God. In fact, you are a people being tested.”

47.author: Maududi
47.text: They said: "We augur ill of you and those who are with you." Salih replied: "Your augury is with Allah. The truth is that you are a people who are being tried."

47.author: Mubarakpuri
47.text: They said: "We augur an omen from you and those with you." He said: "Your omen is of Allah; nay, but you are a people that are being tested."

47.author: Pickthall
47.text: They said: We augur evil of thee and those with thee. He said: Your evil augury is with Allah. Nay, but ye are folk that are being tested.

47.author: Qarai
47.text: They said, ‘We take you and those who are with you for a bad omen.’ He said, ‘Your bad omens are from Allah. Indeed, you are a people being tested.’

47.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
47.text: They said: 'We predict an evil omen from you and those who are with you' He replied: 'Your prediction is with Allah, you are a nation being tested'

47.author: Saheeh International
47.text: They said, "We consider you a bad omen, you and those with you." He said, "Your omen is with Allah. Rather, you are a people being tested."

47.author: Sarwar
47.text: They said, "We have an ill omen about you and your followers." Salih replied, God has made your ill fortune await you. You are a people on trial."

47.author: Shakir
47.text: They said: We have met with ill luck on account of you and on account of those with you. He said: The cause of your evil fortune is with Allah; nay, you are a people who are tried.

47.author: Wahiduddin Khan
47.text: They said, "We see you and your followers as an evil omen." He replied, "No, your evil omen is with God; the truth is that you are a people being put to the test."

47.author: Yusuf Ali
47.text: They said: "Ill omen do we augur from thee and those that are with thee". He said: "Your ill omen is with Allah; yea, ye are a people under trial."

47.author: Transliteration
47.text: Qaloo ittayyarna bika wabiman maAAaka qala tairukum AAinda Allahi bal antum qawmun tuftanoona

48.author: Ahmed Ali
48.text: There were in that city nine persons who spread disorder in the land, and did not reform.

48.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
48.text: And there were nine persons in the city who used to cause turmoil in the land and did not wish reform.

48.author: Arberry
48.text: Now in the city there were nine persons who did corruption in the land, and put not things right;

48.author: Daryabadi
48.text: And there were nine of a group in the City, who spread corruption in the land and rectified not.

48.author: Hilali & Khan
48.text: And there were in the city nine men (from the sons of their chiefs), who made mischief in the land, and would not reform.

48.author: Itani
48.text: In the city was a gang of nine who made mischief in the land and did no good.

48.author: Maududi
48.text: Now there were nine ring-leaders in the city who created corruption in the land and never worked to set things right.

48.author: Mubarakpuri
48.text: And there were in the city nine Rahtin, who made mischief in the land, and would not reform.

48.author: Pickthall
48.text: And there were in the city nine persons who made mischief in the land and reformed not.

48.author: Qarai
48.text: There were nine persons in the city who caused corruption in the land and did not set things right.

48.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
48.text: In the city there were nine people who were corrupting the land and did not reform.

48.author: Saheeh International
48.text: And there were in the city nine family heads causing corruption in the land and not amending [its affairs].

48.author: Sarwar
48.text: There were nine tribes in the city spreading evil without any reform in the land.

48.author: Shakir
48.text: And there were in the city nine persons who made mischief in the land and did not act aright.

48.author: Wahiduddin Khan
48.text: There were in the city nine men who spread corruption in the land, and would not reform.

48.author: Yusuf Ali
48.text: There were in the city nine men of a family, who made mischief in the land, and would not reform.

48.author: Transliteration
48.text: Wakana fee almadeenati tisAAatu rahtin yufsidoona fee alardi wala yuslihoona

49.author: Ahmed Ali
49.text: They said: "Let us swear by God that we will attack Saleh and his family at night, and later tell his heirs: 'We did not see his family destroyed, and we speak the truth.'"

49.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
49.text: Swearing by Allah they said to one another, “We will indeed attack him and his family at night, and then say to his heir, ‘We were not present at the time of slaying of this household, and indeed we are truthful.’”

49.author: Arberry
49.text: they said, 'Swear you, one to another, by God, "We will attack him and his family by night, then we will tell his protector, We were not witnesses of the destruction of his family; and assuredly we are truthful men."'

49.author: Daryabadi
49.text: They said: swear one to anot her by God that we shall surely fall upon him and his household by night and thereafter we shall surely say unto his heir; We witnessed not the destruction of his household, and verily we are truthtellers.

49.author: Hilali & Khan
49.text: They said: "Swear one to another by Allah that we shall make a secret night attack on him and his household, and afterwards we will surely say to his near relatives: 'We witnessed not the destruction of his household, and verily! We are telling the truth.'"

49.author: Itani
49.text: They said, “Swear by God to one another that we will attack him and his family by night, and then tell his guardian, 'We did not witness the murder of his family, and we are being truthful.'“

49.author: Maududi
49.text: They said: "Swear to one another in the name of Allah that we shall make a sudden night swoop on Salih and his family and will then tell their heirs that we did not witness the destruction of his family. We are indeed truthful."

49.author: Mubarakpuri
49.text: They said: "Swear one to another by Allah that we shall make a secret night attack on him and his household, and thereafter we will surely say to his near relatives: `We witnessed not the destruction of his household, and verily, we are telling the truth."'

49.author: Pickthall
49.text: They said: Swear one to another by Allah that we verily will attack him and his household by night, and afterward we will surely say unto his friend: We witnessed not the destruction of his household. And lo! we are truthtellers.

49.author: Qarai
49.text: They said, ‘Swear by Allah that we will attack him and his family by night. Then we will tell his heir that we were not present at the murder of his family and that we speak the truth.’

49.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
49.text: They said: 'Let us swear by Allah to attack him and his family at night, then we will tell his guardian we were not witnesses of the destruction of his family; and surely we are truthful'

49.author: Saheeh International
49.text: They said, "Take a mutual oath by Allah that we will kill him by night, he and his family. Then we will say to his executor, 'We did not witness the destruction of his family, and indeed, we are truthful.' "

49.author: Sarwar
49.text: They said, "Let us swear by God to do away with him and his family during the night then tell his guardian that we did not see how he and his family had been destroyed, and we shall be telling the truth."

49.author: Shakir
49.text: They said: Swear to each other by Allah that we will certainly make a sudden attack on him and his family by night, then we will say to his heir: We did not witness the destruction of his family, and we are most surely truthful.

49.author: Wahiduddin Khan
49.text: They said, "Let us bind ourselves by an oath sworn in the name of God that we shall attack Salih and his family by night, and to his protector [who demands retribution], we shall say, "We were not present when they were slain. We are telling the truth."

49.author: Yusuf Ali
49.text: They said: "Swear a mutual oath by Allah that we shall make a secret night attack on him and his people, and that we shall then say to his heir (when he seeks vengeance): 'We were not present at the slaughter of his people, and we are positively telling the truth.'"

49.author: Transliteration
49.text: Qaloo taqasamoo biAllahi lanubayyitannahu waahlahu thumma lanaqoolanna liwaliyyihi ma shahidna mahlika ahlihi wainna lasadiqoona

50.author: Ahmed Ali
50.text: They hatched up the plot; We also planned without their knowledge.

50.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
50.text: So they devised a scheme, and We made our secret plan, and they remained neglectful.

50.author: Arberry
50.text: And they devised a device, and We likewise devised a device, while they were not aware;

50.author: Daryabadi
50.text: And they plotted a plot, and We plotted a plot, while they perceived it not.

50.author: Hilali & Khan
50.text: So they plotted a plot, and We planned a plan, while they perceived not.

50.author: Itani
50.text: They planned a plan, and We planned a plan, but they did not notice.

50.author: Maududi
50.text: Thus they planned and We too planned, the while they did not know.

50.author: Mubarakpuri
50.text: So, they plotted a plot, and We planned a plan, while they perceived not.

50.author: Pickthall
50.text: So they plotted a plot: and We plotted a plot, while they perceived not.

50.author: Qarai
50.text: They devised a plot, and We [too] devised a plan, but they were not aware.

50.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
50.text: And they devised a scheme and, without their knowledge, We devised a scheme.

50.author: Saheeh International
50.text: And they planned a plan, and We planned a plan, while they perceived not.

50.author: Sarwar
50.text: They plotted and We planned without their knowledge. Consider the result of their plot.

50.author: Shakir
50.text: And they planned a plan, and We planned a plan while they perceived not.

50.author: Wahiduddin Khan
50.text: Thus they devised a plan, and We also devised a plan, but they were not aware of it.

50.author: Yusuf Ali
50.text: They plotted and planned, but We too planned, even while they perceived it not.

50.author: Transliteration
50.text: Wamakaroo makran wamakarna makran wahum la yashAAuroona

51.author: Ahmed Ali
51.text: Now see the end of their machinations: We destroyed them and their entire people.

51.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
51.text: Therefore see what was the result of their scheming – We destroyed them and their entire nation.

51.author: Arberry
51.text: and behold, how was the end of their device! For We destroyed them and their people all together.

51.author: Daryabadi
51.text: So behold thou how was the end of their plotting: verily We annihilated them and their nation all together.

51.author: Hilali & Khan
51.text: Then see how was the end of their plot! Verily! We destroyed them and their nation, all together.

51.author: Itani
51.text: So note the outcome of their planning; We destroyed them and their people, altogether.

51.author: Maududi
51.text: So see what was the outcome of the plan they made: We utterly destroyed them and their people, all of them.

51.author: Mubarakpuri
51.text: Then see how was the end of their plot! Verily, We destroyed them and their nation all together.

51.author: Pickthall
51.text: Then see the nature of the consequence of their plotting, for lo! We destroyed them and their people, every one.

51.author: Qarai
51.text: So observe how was the outcome of their plotting, as We destroyed them and all their people.

51.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
51.text: And behold the consequences of their devising! We destroyed them, and their nation altogether.

51.author: Saheeh International
51.text: Then look how was the outcome of their plan - that We destroyed them and their people, all.

51.author: Sarwar
51.text: We destroyed them and their people altogether.

51.author: Shakir
51.text: See, then, how was the end of their plan that We destroyed them and their people, all (of them).

51.author: Wahiduddin Khan
51.text: See, then, what the consequences of their plan were. We destroyed them and their people utterly, all together.

51.author: Yusuf Ali
51.text: Then see what was the end of their plot!- this, that We destroyed them and their people, all (of them).

51.author: Transliteration
51.text: Faonthur kayfa kana AAaqibatu makrihim anna dammarnahum waqawmahum ajmaAAeena

52.author: Ahmed Ali
52.text: So these their habitations lie deserted now because of their iniquities. Verily there is a sign in this for those who understand.

52.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
52.text: So these are their houses fallen flat, the recompense of their injustice; indeed in this is a sign for people who know.

52.author: Arberry
52.text: Those are their houses, all fallen down because of the evil they committed; surely in that is a sign for a people who have knowledge.

52.author: Daryabadi
52.text: So yonder are their houses overturned, for they did wrong. Verily, herein is a sign unto a people who know.

52.author: Hilali & Khan
52.text: These are their houses in utter ruin, for they did wrong. Verily, in this is indeed an Ayah (a lesson or a sign) for people who know.

52.author: Itani
52.text: Here are their homes, in ruins, on account of their iniquities. Surely in this is a sign for people who know.

52.author: Maududi
52.text: Now behold their houses that lie in utter ruins because of their wrong-doing. Verily there is a Sign in this for the people who know.

52.author: Mubarakpuri
52.text: These are their houses in utter ruin, for they did wrong. Verily, in this is indeed an Ayah for people who know.

52.author: Pickthall
52.text: See, yonder are their dwellings empty and in ruins because they did wrong. Lo! herein is indeed a portent for a people who have knowledge.

52.author: Qarai
52.text: So there lay their houses, fallen in ruin because of their wrongdoing. There is indeed a sign in that for a people who have knowledge.

52.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
52.text: Those are their houses, all are in ruins because of the evil they committed; surely in this there is a sign for nation who know.

52.author: Saheeh International
52.text: So those are their houses, desolate because of the wrong they had done. Indeed in that is a sign for people who know.

52.author: Sarwar
52.text: Those are their empty houses which We ruined because of their injustice. In this there is evidence (of the truth) for the people of knowledge.

52.author: Shakir
52.text: So those are their houses fallen down because they were unjust, most surely there is a sign in this for a people who know.

52.author: Wahiduddin Khan
52.text: Because of their wrongdoing, their houses are in ruins, in that surely there is a sign for people who have knowledge,

52.author: Yusuf Ali
52.text: Now such were their houses, - in utter ruin, - because they practised wrong-doing. Verily in this is a Sign for people of knowledge.

52.author: Transliteration
52.text: Fatilka buyootuhum khawiyatan bima thalamoo inna fee thalika laayatan liqawmin yaAAlamoona

53.author: Ahmed Ali
53.text: We deliver those who believe and take heed for themselves.

53.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
53.text: And We rescued those who accepted faith and used to fear.

53.author: Arberry
53.text: And We delivered those who believed and were godfearing.

53.author: Daryabadi
53.text: And We delivered those who believed and were wont to fear.

53.author: Hilali & Khan
53.text: And We saved those who believed, and used to fear Allah, and keep their duty to Him.

53.author: Itani
53.text: And We saved those who believed and were pious.

53.author: Maududi
53.text: And We delivered those who believed and were wont to avoid disobeying (Allah).

53.author: Mubarakpuri
53.text: And We saved those who believed, and had Taqwa of Allah.

53.author: Pickthall
53.text: And we saved those who believed and used to ward off (evil).

53.author: Qarai
53.text: And We delivered those who had faith and were Godwary.

53.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
53.text: And We saved those who believed and were cautious.

53.author: Saheeh International
53.text: And We saved those who believed and used to fear Allah.

53.author: Sarwar
53.text: We saved the faithful God-fearing believers.

53.author: Shakir
53.text: And We delivered those who believed and who guarded (against evil).

53.author: Wahiduddin Khan
53.text: and We saved those who believed in and feared God.

53.author: Yusuf Ali
53.text: And We saved those who believed and practised righteousness.

54.author: Ahmed Ali
54.text: (Remember) Lot, when he said to his people: "Why do you indulge in obscenities when you know (it is evil)?

54.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
54.text: And (remember) Lut when he said to his people, “What! You stoop to the shameful whereas you can see?”

54.author: Arberry
54.text: And Lot, when he said to his people, 'What, do you commit indecency with your eyes open?

54.author: Daryabadi
54.text: And Lut! what time he said unto his people: commit ye indecency while ye see?

54.author: Hilali & Khan
54.text: And (remember) Lout (Lot)! When he said to his people. Do you commit Al-Fahishah (evil, great sin, every kind of unlawful sexual intercourse, sodomy, etc.) while you see (one another doing evil without any screen, etc.)?"

54.author: Itani
54.text: And Lot, when he said to his people, “Do you commit lewdness with open eyes?

54.author: Maududi
54.text: We also sent Lot, and recall when he told his people: "Do you commit shameless acts with your eyes open?

54.author: Mubarakpuri
54.text: And (remember) Lut! When he said to his people: "Do you commit immoral sins while you see"

54.author: Pickthall
54.text: And Lot! when he said unto his folk: Will ye commit abomination knowingly?

54.author: Qarai
54.text: [We also sent] Lot, when he said to his people, ‘What! Do you commit this indecency while you look on?

54.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
54.text: And Lot, he said to his nation: 'Do you commit indecencies with your eyes open!

54.author: Saheeh International
54.text: And [mention] Lot, when he said to his people, "Do you commit immorality while you are seeing?

54.author: Sarwar
54.text: Lot asked his people, "Do you understandably commit indecency?

54.author: Shakir
54.text: And (We sent) Lut, when he said to his people: What! do you commit indecency while you see?

54.author: Wahiduddin Khan
54.text: And tell of Lot. He said to his people, "Will you commit evil knowingly?

54.author: Yusuf Ali
54.text: (We also sent) Lut (as a messenger): behold, He said to his people, "Do ye do what is shameful though ye see (its iniquity)?

54.author: Transliteration
54.text: Walootan ith qala liqawmihi atatoona alfahishata waantum tubsiroona

55.author: Ahmed Ali
55.text: You lust after men in place of women. You are indeed a stolid people."

55.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
55.text: “What! You lustfully go towards men, leaving the women?! In fact, you are an ignorant people.”

55.author: Arberry
55.text: What, do you approach men lustfully instead of women? No, you are a people that are ignorant.'

55.author: Daryabadi
55.text: Would ye go in lustfully unto men instead of women? Aye! ye are a people addicted to ignorant ways.

55.author: Hilali & Khan
55.text: "Do you approach men in your lusts rather than women? Nay, but you are a people who behave senselessly."

55.author: Itani
55.text: Do you lust after men instead of women? You are truly ignorant people.”

55.author: Maududi
55.text: Do you lustfully approach men instead of women? Nay, you engage in acts of sheer ignorance."

55.author: Mubarakpuri
55.text: "Do you practice your lusts on men instead of women Nay, but you are a people who behave senselessly."

55.author: Pickthall
55.text: Must ye needs lust after men instead of women? Nay, but ye are folk who act senselessly.

55.author: Qarai
55.text: Do you approach men with [sexual] desire instead of women?! Indeed, you are a senseless lot!’

55.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
55.text: Do you approach men lustfully instead of women! No, you are an ignorant nation'

55.author: Saheeh International
55.text: Do you indeed approach men with desire instead of women? Rather, you are a people behaving ignorantly."

55.author: Sarwar
55.text: Do you have carnal relations with men rather than women? You are ignorant people".

55.author: Shakir
55.text: What! do you indeed approach men lustfully rather than women? Nay, you are a people who act ignorantly.

55.author: Wahiduddin Khan
55.text: Must you go lustfully to men instead of women? Indeed, you are a people who are deeply ignorant."

55.author: Yusuf Ali
55.text: Would ye really approach men in your lusts rather than women? Nay, ye are a people (grossly) ignorant!

55.author: Transliteration
55.text: Ainnakum latatoona alrrijala shahwatan min dooni alnnisai bal antum qawmun tajhaloona

56.author: Ahmed Ali
56.text: His people had no answer except saying: "Expel the family of Lot from your city. They are a people who would (rather) be pure!"

56.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
56.text: Therefore the answer of his people was nothing except that they said, “Expel the family of Lut from your township; these people wish purity!”

56.author: Arberry
56.text: And the only answer of his people was that they said, 'Expel the folk of Lot from your city; they are men that keep themselves clean!'

56.author: Daryabadi
56.text: Then there was no answer of his people save that they said: drive forth the family of Lut from your City, verily they are a people who would be pure!

56.author: Hilali & Khan
56.text: There was no other answer given by his people except that they said: "Drive out the family of Lout (Lot) from your city. Verily, these are men who want to be clean and pure!"

56.author: Itani
56.text: But the only response of his people was to say, “Expel the family of Lot from your town. They are purist people.”

56.author: Maududi
56.text: But this had only one answer from his people. They said: "Expel Lot's folk from your city. They pretend to be absolutely clean."

56.author: Mubarakpuri
56.text: There was no other answer given by his people except that they said: "Drive out the family of Lut from your city. Verily, these are men who want to be clean and pure!"

56.author: Pickthall
56.text: But the answer of his folk was naught save that they said: Expel the household of Lot from your township, for they (forsooth) are folk who would keep clean!

56.author: Qarai
56.text: But the only answer of his people was that they said, ‘Expel Lot’s family from your town! They are indeed a puritanical lot.’

56.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
56.text: Yet the only answer of his nation was that they said: 'Expel the family of Lot from your village, they are people who purified themselves'

56.author: Saheeh International
56.text: But the answer of his people was not except that they said, "Expel the family of Lot from your city. Indeed, they are people who keep themselves pure."

56.author: Sarwar
56.text: His people had no answer but to say, "Expel Lot and his family from the town for they want to be pure."

56.author: Shakir
56.text: But the answer of his people was no other except that they ~s said: Turn out Lut's followers from your town; surely they are a people who would keep pure!

56.author: Wahiduddin Khan
56.text: The only answer of his people was, "Drive out Lot and his family from the city. They are a people who make themselves out to be pure."

56.author: Yusuf Ali
56.text: But his people gave no other answer but this: they said, "Drive out the followers of Lut from your city: these are indeed men who want to be clean and pure!"

56.author: Transliteration
56.text: Fama kana jawaba qawmihi illa an qaloo akhrijoo ala lootin min qaryatikum innahum onasun yatatahharoona

57.author: Ahmed Ali
57.text: So We saved him and his family except his wife who was destined to stay behind.

57.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
57.text: We therefore rescued him and his family, except his wife; We had decreed that she is of those who will remain behind.

57.author: Arberry
57.text: So We delivered him and his family, except his wife; We decreed she should be of those that tarried.

57.author: Daryabadi
57.text: Then We delivered him and his household save his wife: We destined hers to be of the lingerers.

57.author: Hilali & Khan
57.text: So We saved him and his family, except his wife. We destined her to be of those who remained behind.

57.author: Itani
57.text: So We saved him and his family, except for his wife, whom We destined to be among the laggards.

57.author: Maududi
57.text: Eventually We saved (Lot) and his family, except his wife. We had decreed that she should be among those who would remain behind.

57.author: Mubarakpuri
57.text: So, We saved him and his family, except his wife. We destined her to be of those who remained behind.

57.author: Pickthall
57.text: Then We saved him and his household save his wife; We destined her to be of those who stayed behind.

57.author: Qarai
57.text: So We delivered him and his family, except his wife. We ordained her to be among those who remained behind.

57.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
57.text: So We saved him and his family, except his wife, whom We decreed should stay behind.

57.author: Saheeh International
57.text: So We saved him and his family, except for his wife; We destined her to be of those who remained behind.

57.author: Sarwar
57.text: We saved (Lot) and his family except his wife who was destined to remain behind.

57.author: Shakir
57.text: But We delivered him and his followers except his wife; We ordained her to be of those who remained behind.

57.author: Wahiduddin Khan
57.text: So We delivered him and his family, except his wife: We ordained her to be one of those who stayed behind,

57.author: Yusuf Ali
57.text: But We saved him and his family, except his wife; her We destined to be of those who lagged behind.

57.author: Transliteration
57.text: Faanjaynahu waahlahu illa imraatahu qaddarnaha mina alghabireena

58.author: Ahmed Ali
58.text: And We rained down on them a shower (of stones). How ruinous was the rain that fell on those who had been warned (but warned in vain)!

58.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
58.text: And We showered a rain upon them; so what a wretched rain for those who were warned!
