58.author: Arberry
58.text: And We rained on them a rain; and evil indeed is the rain of them that are warned.

58.author: Daryabadi
58.text: And We rained upon them a rain; ill was the rain upon the warned.

58.author: Hilali & Khan
58.text: And We rained down on them a rain (of stones). So evil was the rain of those who were warned.

58.author: Itani
58.text: And We rained upon them a rain. Miserable was the rain of those forewarned.

58.author: Maududi
58.text: And We rained down upon them a rain. It was an evil rain for those who had already been warned.

58.author: Mubarakpuri
58.text: And We rained down on them a rain. So, evil was the rain of those who were warned.

58.author: Pickthall
58.text: And We rained a rain upon them. Dreadful is the rain of those who have been warned.

58.author: Qarai
58.text: Then We poured down upon them a rain [of stones]. Evil was that rain for those who had been warned!

58.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
58.text: And we rained on them a rain (of stones); indeed it is an evil rain that rains on those that are warned.

58.author: Saheeh International
58.text: And We rained upon them a rain [of stones], and evil was the rain of those who were warned.

58.author: Sarwar
58.text: We sent to them a terrible rainstorm. How horrible was the rain for the people who had already received warning.

58.author: Shakir
58.text: And We rained on them a rain, and evil was the rain of those who had been warned.

58.author: Wahiduddin Khan
58.text: and We pelted them with torrential rain. How dreadful that rain was for those who had been warned!

58.author: Yusuf Ali
58.text: And We rained down on them a shower (of brimstone): and evil was the shower on those who were admonished (but heeded not)!

58.author: Transliteration
58.text: Waamtarna AAalayhim mataran fasaa mataru almunthareena

59.author: Ahmed Ali
59.text: Say: "All praise be to God, and peace on those of His creatures whom He has chosen." Is God better or those they associate with Him --

59.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
59.text: Say (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him), “All praise is to Allah, and peace upon His chosen bondmen; is Allah better or what you ascribe as His partners?”

59.author: Arberry
59.text: Say: 'Praise belongs to God, and peace be on His servants whom He has chosen.' What, is God better, or that they associate?

59.author: Daryabadi
59.text: Say thou: All praise unto Allah, and peace upon His bondmen whom He hath Chosen! Is Allah best, or that which they associate?

59.author: Hilali & Khan
59.text: Say (O Muhammad SAW): "Praise and thanks be to Allah, and peace be on His slaves whom He has chosen (for His Message)! Is Allah better, or (all) that you ascribe as partners (to Him)?" (Of course, Allah is Better).

59.author: Itani
59.text: Say, “Praise God, and peace be upon His servants whom He has selected. Is God better, or what they associate?”

59.author: Maududi
59.text: Say, (O Muhammad): "All praise be to Allah, and peace be on those of His servants whom He has chosen." (Ask them): "Who is better: Allah or the false gods that they associate with Him as His partners?

59.author: Mubarakpuri
59.text: Say: "Praise and thanks be to Allah, and peace be on His servants whom He has chosen! Is Allah better, or what they ascribe as partners"

59.author: Pickthall
59.text: Say (O Muhammad): Praise be to Allah, and peace be on His slaves whom He hath chosen! Is Allah best, or (all) that ye ascribe as partners (unto Him)?

59.author: Qarai
59.text: Say, ‘All praise belongs to Allah, and Peace be to the servants whom He has chosen.’ Is Allah better, or the partners they ascribe [to Him]?

59.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
59.text: Say: 'Praise belongs to Allah and peace be upon His worshipers whom He has chosen' Who is more worthier, Allah or that which they associate!

59.author: Saheeh International
59.text: Say, [O Muhammad], "Praise be to Allah, and peace upon His servants whom He has chosen. Is Allah better or what they associate with Him?"

59.author: Sarwar
59.text: (Muhammad), say, "It is only God who deserves all praise. Peace be upon His chosen servants. Which is better God or the idols?

59.author: Shakir
59.text: Say: Praise be to Allah and peace on His servants whom He has chosen: is Allah better, or what they associate (with Him)?

59.author: Wahiduddin Khan
59.text: Say, "All praise be to God, and peace be upon those servants of His whom He has chosen. Is God better, or what they associate with Him?

59.author: Yusuf Ali
59.text: Say: Praise be to Allah, and Peace on his servants whom He has chosen (for his Message). (Who) is better?- Allah or the false gods they associate (with Him)?

59.author: Transliteration
59.text: Quli alhamdu lillahi wasalamun AAala AAibadihi allatheena istafa allahu khayrun amma yushrikoona

60.author: Ahmed Ali
60.text: Who created the heavens and the earth, who sends down water from the sky for you, with which He causes graceful gardens to grow? It was not in your power to make trees germinate. Is there any other god along with God. In fact they are a people who turn away (from the truth).

60.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
60.text: Or He Who created the heavens and the earth, and sent down water from the sky for you; so We grew delightful gardens with it; you had no strength to grow its trees; is there a God along with Allah?! In fact they are those who shun the right path.

60.author: Arberry
60.text: He who created the heavens and earth, and sent down for you out of heaven water; and We caused to grow therewith gardens full of loveliness whose trees you could never grow. Is there a god with God? Nay, but they are a people who assign to Him equals!

60.author: Daryabadi
60.text: Is not he best Who hath created the heavens and the earth, and Who sendeth down water for you from the heaven wherewith We cause beauteous orchards to grow up, whereof it was not possible for you to cause the trees to grow up! Is there any god along with Allah! Nay! but they are a people who equalise.

60.author: Hilali & Khan
60.text: Is not He (better than your gods) Who created the heavens and the earth, and sends down for you water (rain) from the sky, whereby We cause to grow wonderful gardens full of beauty and delight? It is not in your ability to cause the growth of their trees. Is there any ilah (god) with Allah? Nay, but they are a people who ascribe equals (to Him)!

60.author: Itani
60.text: Or, who created the heavens and the earth, and rains down water from the sky for you? With it We produce gardens full of beauty, whose trees you could not have produced. Is there another god with God? But they are a people who equate.

60.author: Maududi
60.text: Who is it that has created the heavens and the earth and sent down for you water from the sky and then We caused to grow therewith orchards full of beauty whose trees you could never grow. Is there any god associated with Allah (in these tasks)?" Nay, they are a people who are veering away from the Right Path.

60.author: Mubarakpuri
60.text: Is not He Who created the heavens and the earth, and sends down for you water from the sky, whereby We cause to grow wonderful gardens full of beauty and delight It is not in your ability to cause the growth of their trees. Is there any god with Allah Nay, but they are a people who ascribe equals!

60.author: Pickthall
60.text: Is not He (best) Who created the heavens and the earth, and sendeth down for you water from the sky wherewith We cause to spring forth joyous orchards, whose trees it never hath been yours to cause to grow. Is there any Allah beside Allah? Nay, but they are folk who ascribe equals (unto Him)!

60.author: Qarai
60.text: Is He who created the heavens and the earth, and sends down for you water from the sky, whereby We grow delightful gardens, whose trees you could never cause to grow...? What! Is there a god besides Allah? Indeed, they are a lot who equate [others with Allah].

60.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
60.text: Is He who created the heavens and the earth, and sent water from the sky for you and caused gardens to grow full of beauty of which its tree you could never grow, is there a god with Allah? No, but they are a nation who set up equals with Him!

60.author: Saheeh International
60.text: [More precisely], is He [not best] who created the heavens and the earth and sent down for you rain from the sky, causing to grow thereby gardens of joyful beauty which you could not [otherwise] have grown the trees thereof? Is there a deity with Allah? [No], but they are a people who ascribe equals [to Him].

60.author: Sarwar
60.text: "(Are the idols worthier or) the One who has created the heavens and the earth, who has sent water from the sky for you, who has established delightful gardens and you could not even plant one tree? Is there any Lord besides God? In fact, the unbelievers are the ones who deviate from the right path.

60.author: Shakir
60.text: Nay, He Who created the heavens and the earth, and sent down for you water from the cloud; then We cause to grow thereby beautiful gardens; it is not possible for you that you should make the trees thereof to grow. Is there a god with Allah? Nay! they are people who deviate.

60.author: Wahiduddin Khan
60.text: Who created the heavens and the earth and sends down water for you from the sky, by which We make luxuriant gardens grow, you could never make such trees grow in them, is it another deity besides God? No indeed, but they are a people who equate others with Him.

60.author: Yusuf Ali
60.text: Or, Who has created the heavens and the earth, and Who sends you down rain from the sky? Yea, with it We cause to grow well-planted orchards full of beauty of delight: it is not in your power to cause the growth of the trees in them. (Can there be another) god besides Allah? Nay, they are a people who swerve from justice.

60.author: Transliteration
60.text: Amman khalaqa alssamawati waalarda waanzala lakum mina alssamai maan faanbatna bihi hadaiqa thata bahjatin ma kana lakum an tunbitoo shajaraha ailahun maAAa Allahi bal hum qawmun yaAAdiloona

61.author: Ahmed Ali
61.text: Who then made the earth a habitable place, and made the rivers (flow) in its valleys and dales, and placed upon it firm stabilisers, and kept a barrier between two bodies of water? Is there any other god along with God? In reality most of them do not know.

61.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
61.text: Or He Who made the earth for habitation, and placed rivers within it, and created mountains as anchors for it, and kept a barrier between the two seas? Is there a God along with Allah?! In fact, most of them are ignorant.

61.author: Arberry
61.text: He who made the earth a fixed place and set amidst it rivers and appointed for it firm mountains and placed a partition between the two seas. Is there a god with God? Nay, but the most of them have no knowledge.

61.author: Daryabadi
61.text: Is not he best Who hath made the earth a fixed abode and placed rivers in the midst thereof and placed Firm mountains thereon, and hath set a barrier between the two seas? IS there any god along with Allah? Nay! but most of them know not.

61.author: Hilali & Khan
61.text: Is not He (better than your gods) Who has made the earth as a fixed abode, and has placed rivers in its midst, and has placed firm mountains therein, and has set a barrier between the two seas (of salt and sweet water). Is there any ilah (god) with Allah? Nay, but most of them know not.

61.author: Itani
61.text: Or, who made the earth habitable, and made rivers flow through it, and set mountains on it, and placed a partition between the two seas? Is there another god with God? But most of them do not know.

61.author: Maududi
61.text: Who is it Who has made the earth a place of resort, and has caused rivers to flow in its midst, and has placed upon it firm mountains, and has placed a barrier between two masses of water? Is there any god associated with Allah (in these tasks)? Nay; but most of them do not know.

61.author: Mubarakpuri
61.text: Is not He Who has made the earth as a fixed abode, and has placed rivers in its midst, and has placed firm mountains therein, and has set a barrier between the two seas (of salt and sweet water) Is there any ilah (god) with Allah Nay, but most of them know not!

61.author: Pickthall
61.text: Is not He (best) Who made the earth a fixed abode, and placed rivers in the folds thereof, and placed firm hills therein, and hath set a barrier between the two seas? Is there any Allah beside Allah? Nay, but most of them know not!

61.author: Qarai
61.text: Is He who made the earth an abode [for you], and made rivers [flowing] through it, and set firm mountains for it [’s stability], and set a barrier between the two seas...? What! Is there a god besides Allah? Indeed, most of them do not know.

61.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
61.text: Who has established the earth a fixed place and set therein rivers; and set for it firm mountains and placed a barrier between the two seas, is there a god with Allah? No, most of them are without knowledge.

61.author: Saheeh International
61.text: Is He [not best] who made the earth a stable ground and placed within it rivers and made for it firmly set mountains and placed between the two seas a barrier? Is there a deity with Allah? [No], but most of them do not know.

61.author: Sarwar
61.text: "(Are the idols worthier or) the One who has made the earth a resting place, the rivers flow from its valleys, the mountains as anchors and a barrier between the two seas? Is there any lord besides God? In fact, most people do not know.

61.author: Shakir
61.text: Or, Who made the earth a restingplace, and made in it rivers, and raised on it mountains and placed between the two seas a barrier. Is there a god with Allah? Nay! most of them do not know!

61.author: Wahiduddin Khan
61.text: Who is it that made the earth a stable place to live in? Who made rivers flow through it? Who set mountains upon it and placed a barrier between the two seas? Is there another deity besides God? Indeed, most of them have no knowledge.

61.author: Yusuf Ali
61.text: Or, Who has made the earth firm to live in; made rivers in its midst; set thereon mountains immovable; and made a separating bar between the two bodies of flowing water? (can there be another) god besides Allah? Nay, most of them know not.

61.author: Transliteration
61.text: Amman jaAAala alarda qararan wajaAAala khilalaha anharan wajaAAala laha rawasiya wajaAAala bayna albahrayni hajizan ailahun maAAa Allahi bal aktharuhum la yaAAlamoona

62.author: Ahmed Ali
62.text: Who hears the cry of the anguished (soul) when it calls to Him, and relieves its suffering? And who made you trustees on the earth? Is there any other god along with God? How little it is that you reflect!

62.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
62.text: Or He Who answers the prayer of the helpless when he invokes Him and removes the evil, and makes you inheritors of the earth? Is there a God along with Allah?! Very little do they ponder!

62.author: Arberry
62.text: He who answers the constrained, when he calls unto Him, and removes the evil and appoints you to be successors in the earth. Is there a god with God? Little indeed do you remember.

62.author: Daryabadi
62.text: Is not he best Who answereth the distressed when he calleth unto Him and removeth the evil, and hath made you the successors in the earth? Is there any god along with Allah? Little ye reflect!

62.author: Hilali & Khan
62.text: Is not He (better than your gods) Who responds to the distressed one, when he calls Him, and Who removes the evil, and makes you inheritors of the earth, generations after generations. Is there any ilah (god) with Allah? Little is that you remember!

62.author: Itani
62.text: Or, who answers the one in need when he prays to Him, and relieves adversity, and makes you successors on earth? Is there another god with God? How hardly you pay attention.

62.author: Maududi
62.text: Who is it Who heeds the prayers of the distressed when he calls out to Him and Who removes his affliction? And who is it Who makes you vicegerents of the earth? Is there any god associated with Allah (in this task)? How little do you reflect!

62.author: Mubarakpuri
62.text: Is not He Who responds to the distressed one, when he calls on Him, and Who removes the evil, and makes you inheritors of the earth, generations after generations Is there any god with Allah Little is that you remember!

62.author: Pickthall
62.text: Is not He (best) Who answereth the wronged one when he crieth unto Him and removeth the evil, and hath made you viceroys of the earth? Is there any Allah beside Allah? Little do they reflect!

62.author: Qarai
62.text: Is He who answers the call of the distressed [person] when he invokes Him and removes his distress, and makes you successors on the earth...? What! Is there a god besides Allah? Little is the admonition that you take.

62.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
62.text: Who answers the oppressed when he supplicates to Him and removes evil and appoints you as inheritors of the earth, is there a god with Allah? How little you remember!

62.author: Saheeh International
62.text: Is He [not best] who responds to the desperate one when he calls upon Him and removes evil and makes you inheritors of the earth? Is there a deity with Allah? Little do you remember.

62.author: Sarwar
62.text: "(Are the idols worthier or) the One who answers the prayers of the distressed ones, removes their hardship, and makes you the successors in the land? Is there any lord besides God? In fact, you take very little heed.

62.author: Shakir
62.text: Or, Who answers the distressed one when he calls upon Him and removes the evil, and He will make you successors in the earth. Is there a god with Allah? Little is it that you mind!

62.author: Wahiduddin Khan
62.text: Who responds to the oppressed when he calls out to Him, and relieves his suffering and who will make you inheritors of the earth? Then, is there a god besides God? How little you pay heed!

62.author: Yusuf Ali
62.text: Or, Who listens to the (soul) distressed when it calls on Him, and Who relieves its suffering, and makes you (mankind) inheritors of the earth? (Can there be another) god besides Allah? Little it is that ye heed!

62.author: Transliteration
62.text: Amman yujeebu almudtarra itha daAAahu wayakshifu alssooa wayajAAalukum khulafaa alardi ailahun maAAa Allahi qaleelan ma tathakkaroona

63.author: Ahmed Ali
63.text: Who then really shows you the way in the darkness of the desert and the sea? And who sends breezes bringing news of His benevolence? Is there any other god along with God? He is far too exalted for what they associate with Him!

63.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
63.text: Or He Who shows you the path in the darkness of the land and the sea, and He Who sends the winds before His mercy, heralding glad tidings? Is there a God along with Allah?! Supremacy is to Allah above all that they ascribe as partners!

63.author: Arberry
63.text: He who guides you in the shadows of the land and the sea and looses the winds, bearing good tidings before His mercy. Is there a god with God? High exalted be God, above that which they associate!

63.author: Daryabadi
63.text: Is not he best Who guideth you in the darknesses of the land and the sea, and Who sendeth the winds as heralds before His mercy Is there any god along with Allah? Exalted be Allah from all that they associate!

63.author: Hilali & Khan
63.text: Is not He (better than your gods) Who guides you in the darkness of the land and the sea, and Who sends the winds as heralds of glad tidings, going before His Mercy (rain)? Is there any ilah (god) with Allah? High Exalted be Allah above all that they associate as partners (to Him)!

63.author: Itani
63.text: Or, who guides you through the darkness of land and sea, and who sends the winds as heralds of His mercy? Is there another god with God? Most exalted is God, above what they associate.

63.author: Maududi
63.text: Who is it Who guides you through the darkness on land and sea? And Who sends winds as heralds of good tidings ahead of His Mercy? Is there any god associated with Allah (in this task)? Exalted be Allah above whatever they associate with Him in His Divinity!

63.author: Mubarakpuri
63.text: Is not He Who guides you in the darkness of the land and the sea, and Who sends the winds as heralds of glad tidings, going before His mercy Is there any god with Allah Exalted be Allah above all that they associate as partners!

63.author: Pickthall
63.text: Is not He (best) Who guideth you in the darkness of the land and the sea, He Who sendeth the winds as heralds of His mercy? Is there any Allah beside Allah? High Exalted be Allah from all that they ascribe as partner (unto Him)!

63.author: Qarai
63.text: Is He who guides you in the darkness of land and sea and who sends the winds as harbingers of His mercy...? What! Is there a god besides Allah? Exalted is Allah above [having] any partners that they ascribe [to Him].

63.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
63.text: Who guides you in the darkness of the land and sea, and sends the winds bearing glad tidings of His Mercy, is there a god with Allah? Exalted is Allah above what they associate.

63.author: Saheeh International
63.text: Is He [not best] who guides you through the darknesses of the land and sea and who sends the winds as good tidings before His mercy? Is there a deity with Allah? High is Allah above whatever they associate with Him.

63.author: Sarwar
63.text: "(Are the idols worthier or) the One who guides you in the darkness of the land and sea and sends the winds bearing the glad news of His mercy? Is there any lord besides God? God is too exalted to be considered equal to anything else.

63.author: Shakir
63.text: Or, Who guides you in utter darkness of the land and the sea, and Who sends the winds as good news before His mercy. Is there a god with Allah? Exalted by Allah above what they associate (with Him).

63.author: Wahiduddin Khan
63.text: Who guides you in the darkness of the land and sea? Who sends the breezes as heralds of His mercy? Then, is there a deity besides God? Exalted is God above what they associate with Him.

63.author: Yusuf Ali
63.text: Or, Who guides you through the depths of darkness on land and sea, and Who sends the winds as heralds of glad tidings, going before His Mercy? (Can there be another) god besides Allah?- High is Allah above what they associate with Him!

63.author: Transliteration
63.text: Amman yahdeekum fee thulumati albarri waalbahri waman yursilu alrriyaha bushran bayna yaday rahmatihi ailahun maAAa Allahi taAAala Allahu AAamma yushrikoona

64.author: Ahmed Ali
64.text: Who creates first then reverts it? And who gives you provision from the heavens and the earth? Is there any other god along with God? Tell them: "Bring your proof, if you are truthful."

64.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
64.text: Or He Who initiates the creation, then will create it again, and He Who provides you sustenance from the skies and the earth? Is there a God along with Allah?! Proclaim, “Bring your proof, if you are truthful!”

64.author: Arberry
64.text: Who originates creation, then brings it back again, and provides you out of heaven and earth. Is there a god with God? Say: 'Produce your proof, if you speak truly.'

64.author: Daryabadi
64.text: Is not he best Who originateth creation, and shall thereafter restoreth it, and Who provideth for you from the heaven and the earth? IS there any godl along with Allah? Say thou: bring your proof, if ye are truth-tellers.

64.author: Hilali & Khan
64.text: Is not He (better than your so-called gods) Who originates creation, and shall thereafter repeat it, and Who provides for you from heaven and earth? Is there any ilah (god) with Allah? Say, "Bring forth your proofs, if you are truthful."

64.author: Itani
64.text: Or, who originates the creation and then repeats it, and who gives you livelihood from the sky and the earth? Is there another god with God? Say, “Produce your evidence, if you are truthful.”

64.author: Maududi
64.text: Who is it Who creates in the first instance and then repeats it? Who is it Who provides you with sustenance from the heavens and the earth? Is there any god associated with Allah (in these tasks)? Say: "Bring forth your evidence, if you are truthful."

64.author: Mubarakpuri
64.text: Is not He Who originates creation, and shall thereafter repeat it, and Who provides for you from heaven and earth Is there any god with Allah Say: "Bring forth your proofs, if you are truthful."

64.author: Pickthall
64.text: Is not He (best) Who produceth creation, then reproduceth it, and Who provideth for you from the heaven and the earth? Is there any Allah beside Allah? Say: Bring your proof, if ye are truthful!

64.author: Qarai
64.text: Is He who originates the creation, then He will bring it back, and who provides for you from the sky and the earth...? What! Is there a god besides Allah? Say, ‘Produce your evidence, if you are truthful.’

64.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
64.text: Who originates creation, then brings it back again, who gives you sustenance from the heaven and the earth, is there a god with Allah? Say: 'Bring us your proof if you are among the truthful'

64.author: Saheeh International
64.text: Is He [not best] who begins creation and then repeats it and who provides for you from the heaven and earth? Is there a deity with Allah? Say, "Produce your proof, if you should be truthful."

64.author: Sarwar
64.text: "(Are the idols worthier or) the One who began the creation and who will turn it back, who gives you sustenance from the heavens and the earth? Is there any lord besides God? Say, "Bring your proof if what you say is true."

64.author: Shakir
64.text: Or, Who originates the creation, then reproduces it and Who gives you sustenance from the heaven and the earth. Is there a god With Allah? Say: Bring your proof if you are truthful.

64.author: Wahiduddin Khan
64.text: Who originates creation, then regenerates it, and who gives you sustenance from heaven and earth? Then, is there a deity besides God?" Say, "Bring forward your proofs, if you are telling the truth."

64.author: Yusuf Ali
64.text: Or, Who originates creation, then repeats it, and who gives you sustenance from heaven and earth? (Can there be another) god besides Allah? Say, "Bring forth your argument, if ye are telling the truth!"

64.author: Transliteration
64.text: Amman yabdao alkhalqa thumma yuAAeeduhu waman yarzuqukum mina alssamai waalardi ailahun maAAa Allahi qul hatoo burhanakum in kuntum sadiqeena

65.author: Ahmed Ali
65.text: Say: "No one in the heavens and the earth has knowledge of the unknown except God, nor can they know when they will be raised again.

65.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
65.text: Say (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him), “None in the heavens and the earth know the hidden by themselves, except Allah; and they do not know when they will be raised.”

65.author: Arberry
65.text: Say: 'None knows the Unseen in the heavens and earth except God. And they are not aware when they shall be raised;

65.author: Daryabadi
65.text: Say thou: none in the heavens and the earth knoweth the Unseen save Allah; nor can they perceive when they will be raised.

65.author: Hilali & Khan
65.text: Say: "None in the heavens and the earth knows the Ghaib (unseen) except Allah, nor can they perceive when they shall be resurrected."

65.author: Itani
65.text: Say, “No one in the heavens or on earth knows the future except God; and they do not perceive when they will be resurrected.”

65.author: Maududi
65.text: Say: "None in the heavens or on the earth has knowledge of the Unseen save Allah. They do not know when they will be raised to life."

65.author: Mubarakpuri
65.text: Say: "None in the heavens and the earth knows the Unseen except Allah, nor can they perceive when they shall be resurrected."

65.author: Pickthall
65.text: Say (O Muhammad): None in the heavens and the earth knoweth the Unseen save Allah; and they know not when they will be raised (again).

65.author: Qarai
65.text: Say, ‘No one in the heavens or the earth knows the Unseen except Allah, and they are not aware when they will be resurrected.’

65.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
65.text: Say: 'None in the heavens or the earth knows the Unseen except Allah, and they are not aware when they shall be resurrected'

65.author: Saheeh International
65.text: Say, "None in the heavens and earth knows the unseen except Allah, and they do not perceive when they will be resurrected."

65.author: Sarwar
65.text: (Muhammad) say, "No one in the heavens or the earth knows the unseen except God, and no one knows when they will be resurrected.

65.author: Shakir
65.text: Say: No one in the heavens and the earth knows the unseen but Allah; and they do not know when they shall be raised.

65.author: Wahiduddin Khan
65.text: Say, "No one in the heavens and the earth has knowledge of the unseen except God." They do not know when they will be raised up again.

65.author: Yusuf Ali
65.text: Say: None in the heavens or on earth, except Allah, knows what is hidden: nor can they perceive when they shall be raised up (for Judgment).

65.author: Transliteration
65.text: Qul la yaAAlamu man fee alssamawati waalardi alghayba illa Allahu wama yashAAuroona ayyana yubAAathoona

66.author: Ahmed Ali
66.text: Still less do they comprehend the life to come. In fact they are in doubt about it. Still more, they are blind to it."

66.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
66.text: Has their knowledge advanced so much as to know the Hereafter? On the contrary, they are in doubt concerning it; in fact they are blind towards it.

66.author: Arberry
66.text: nay, but their knowledge fails as to the Hereafter; nay, they are in doubt of it; nay, they are blind to it.

66.author: Daryabadi
66.text: Aye! their knowledge attaineth not to the Hereafter, Aye! they are in doubt thereof. Aye! thereunto they are blind.

66.author: Hilali & Khan
66.text: Nay, they have no knowledge of the Hereafter. Nay, they are in doubt about it. Nay, they are blind about it.

66.author: Itani
66.text: In fact, their knowledge of the Hereafter is confused. In fact, they are in doubt about it. In fact, they are blind to it.

66.author: Maududi
66.text: Nay, but they have lost their knowledge of the Hereafter. They are steeped in doubt and uncertainty about it: rather they are blind to it.

66.author: Mubarakpuri
66.text: Nay, their knowledge will perceive that in the Hereafter. Nay, they are in doubt about it. Nay, they are in complete blindness about it.

66.author: Pickthall
66.text: Nay, but doth their knowledge reach to the Hereafter? Nay, for they are in doubt concerning it. Nay, for they cannot see it.

66.author: Qarai
66.text: Do they comprehend the knowledge of the Hereafter? No, they are in doubt about it. Indeed, they are blind to it.

66.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
66.text: No, their knowledge fails them as to the Everlasting Life; no, they are in doubt about it, no, they are blind to it.

66.author: Saheeh International
66.text: Rather, their knowledge is arrested concerning the Hereafter. Rather, they are in doubt about it. Rather, they are, concerning it, blind.

66.author: Sarwar
66.text: Their knowledge of the next life is no more than doubts. In fact, they are blind about it.

66.author: Shakir
66.text: Nay, their knowledge respecting the hereafter is slight and hasty; nay, they are in doubt about it; nay, they are quite blind to it

66.author: Wahiduddin Khan
66.text: Indeed, their knowledge of the life to come stops short of the truth: they are [often] in doubt as to its reality: in fact, they are blind to it.

66.author: Yusuf Ali
66.text: Still less can their knowledge comprehend the Hereafter: Nay, they are in doubt and uncertainty thereanent; nay, they are blind thereunto!

66.author: Transliteration
66.text: Bali iddaraka AAilmuhum fee alakhirati bal hum fee shakkin minha bal hum minha AAamoona

67.author: Ahmed Ali
67.text: Those who do not believe, say: "When we and our fathers have turned to dust, how shall we be raised again?

67.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
67.text: And the disbelievers said, “Will we, when we and our forefathers have turned into dust, be removed again?”

67.author: Arberry
67.text: The unbelievers say, 'What, when we are dust, and our fathers, shall we indeed be brought forth?

67.author: Daryabadi
67.text: And those who disbelieve say: when we have become dust, we and our fathers, shall we, for sooth, be brought forth?

67.author: Hilali & Khan
67.text: And those who disbelieve say: "When we have become dust, we and our fathers, shall we really be brought forth (again)?

67.author: Itani
67.text: Those who disbelieve say, “When we have become dust, and our ancestors, shall we be brought out?

67.author: Maududi
67.text: The unbelievers say: "When we become dust, we and our forefathers, shall we really be brought out (from our graves)?

67.author: Mubarakpuri
67.text: And those who disbelieve say: "When we have become dust -- we and our fathers -- shall we really be brought forth"

67.author: Pickthall
67.text: Yet those who disbelieve say: When we have become dust like our fathers, shall we verily be brought forth (again)?

67.author: Qarai
67.text: The faithless say, ‘What! When we and our fathers have become dust shall we be raised [from the dead]?

67.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
67.text: The unbelievers say: 'When we and our fathers are turned to dust, shall we be brought forth?

67.author: Saheeh International
67.text: And those who disbelieve say, "When we have become dust as well as our forefathers, will we indeed be brought out [of the graves]?

67.author: Sarwar
67.text: The unbelievers have said, "Shall we and our fathers be brought out of the graves after we become dust?

67.author: Shakir
67.text: And those who disbelieve say: What! when we have become dust and our fathers (too), shall we certainly be brought forth?

67.author: Wahiduddin Khan
67.text: Those who deny the truth say, "When we have turned to dust like our fathers, shall we be brought back to life again?

67.author: Yusuf Ali
67.text: The Unbelievers say: "What! when we become dust,- we and our fathers,- shall we really be raised (from the dead)?

67.author: Transliteration
67.text: Waqala allatheena kafaroo aitha kunna turaban waabaona ainna lamukhrajoona

68.author: Ahmed Ali
68.text: Indeed we and our fathers had been promised this before. It is nothing but the earlier people's lore."

68.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
68.text: “Indeed this promise was given to us, and before us to our forefathers – this is nothing but stories of former people.”

68.author: Arberry
68.text: We have been promised this, and our fathers before; this is naught but the fairy-tales of the ancients.'

68.author: Daryabadi
68.text: Assuredly we have been promised this aforetime, we and our fathers. Naught is this but fables of the ancients.

68.author: Hilali & Khan
68.text: "Indeed we were promised this, we and our forefathers before, Verily, these are nothing but tales of ancients."

68.author: Itani
68.text: We were promised that before, we and our ancestors—these are nothing but legends of the ancients.”

68.author: Maududi
68.text: We were told about this and so were our forefathers before us. But these are no more than fairy tales that have been recounted from ancient times."

68.author: Mubarakpuri
68.text: "Indeed we were promised this -- we and our forefathers before, verily, these are nothing but tales of ancients."

68.author: Pickthall
68.text: We were promised this, forsooth, we and our fathers. (All) this is naught but fables of the men of old.

68.author: Qarai
68.text: We and our fathers were promised this before. [But] these are just myths of the ancients.’

68.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
68.text: We were promised this before, and so were our fathers. It is but the fictitious story of the ancients'

68.author: Saheeh International
68.text: We have been promised this, we and our forefathers, before. This is not but legends of the former peoples."

68.author: Sarwar
68.text: It was promised to us and to our fathers before us. It is only ancient legends."

68.author: Shakir
68.text: We have certainly been promised this, we and our fathers before; these are naught but stories of the ancients

68.author: Wahiduddin Khan
68.text: We and our fathers were promised this before; these are but old stories."

68.author: Yusuf Ali
68.text: "It is true we were promised this,- we and our fathers before (us): these are nothing but tales of the ancients."

68.author: Transliteration
68.text: Laqad wuAAidna hatha nahnu waabaona min qablu in hatha illa asateeru alawwaleena

69.author: Ahmed Ali
69.text: Say: "Travel in the land and see how (bad) was the end of sinners."

69.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
69.text: “Proclaim, (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him), “Travel in the land and see what sort of fate befell the guilty.”

69.author: Arberry
69.text: Say: 'Journey in the land, then behold how was the end of the sinners.'

69.author: Daryabadi
69.text: Say thou: travel in the land and behold what like hath been the end of the culprits.

69.author: Hilali & Khan
69.text: Say to them (O Muhammad SAW) "Travel in the land and see how has been the end of the criminals (those who denied Allah's Messengers and disobeyed Allah)."

69.author: Itani
69.text: Say, travel through the earth, and observe the fate of the guilty.”

69.author: Maududi
69.text: Say: "Go about through the earth and see what has been the end of the evil-doers."

69.author: Mubarakpuri
69.text: Say to them: "Travel in the land and see how has been the end of the criminals."

69.author: Pickthall
69.text: Say (unto them, O Muhammad): Travel in the land and see the nature of the sequel for the guilty!

69.author: Qarai
69.text: Say, ‘Travel over the land and observe how was the fate of the guilty.’

69.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
69.text: Say: 'Journey in the land and see what was the end of the sinners'

69.author: Saheeh International
69.text: Say, [O Muhammad], "Travel through the land and observe how was the end of the criminals."

69.author: Sarwar
69.text: Say, "Travel through the land and see how terrible was the end of the criminal ones".

69.author: Shakir
69.text: Say: Travel in the earth, then see how was the end of the guilty.

69.author: Wahiduddin Khan
69.text: Say, "Roam across the earth and observe what was the end of the sinful ones."

69.author: Yusuf Ali
69.text: Say: "Go ye through the earth and see what has been the end of those guilty (of sin)."

69.author: Transliteration
69.text: Qul seeroo fee alardi faonthuroo kayfa kana AAaqibatu almujrimeena

70.author: Ahmed Ali
70.text: Do not grieve over them, and do not be distressed by what they contrive.

70.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
70.text: And do not grieve upon them, nor be distressed because of their scheming.

70.author: Arberry
70.text: Do not sorrow for them, nor be thou straitened for what they devise.

70.author: Daryabadi
70.text: And grieve thou not over them nor be straitened because of that which they plot.

70.author: Hilali & Khan
70.text: And grieve you not for them, nor be straitened (in distress) because of what they plot.

70.author: Itani
70.text: But do not grieve over them, and do not be troubled by what they plot.

70.author: Maududi
70.text: (O Prophet), do not grieve over them, nor be distressed at their designs.

70.author: Mubarakpuri
70.text: And grieve you not over them, nor be straitened (in distress) because of what they plot.

70.author: Pickthall
70.text: And grieve thou not for them, nor be in distress because of what they plot (against thee).

70.author: Qarai
70.text: Do not grieve for them, and do not be upset by their guile.

70.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
70.text: Do not be sorry for them, nor be distressed for what they devise.

70.author: Saheeh International
70.text: And grieve not over them or be in distress from what they conspire.

70.author: Sarwar
70.text: (Muhammad), do not be grieved (about their disbelief) nor distressed about their evil plans against you.

70.author: Shakir
70.text: And grieve not for them and be not distressed because of what they plan.

70.author: Wahiduddin Khan
70.text: Do not grieve over them, nor feel distressed at their schemes.

70.author: Yusuf Ali
70.text: But grieve not over them, nor distress thyself because of their plots.

70.author: Transliteration
70.text: Wala tahzan AAalayhim wala takun fee dayqin mimma yamkuroona

71.author: Ahmed Ali
71.text: Yet they say: "When will this promise come to pass? (Tell Us) if you speak the truth."

71.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
71.text: And they say, “When will this promise come, if you are truthful?”

71.author: Arberry
71.text: They say, 'When shall this promise come to pass, if you speak the truth?'

71.author: Daryabadi
71.text: And they say: when will this Promise be fufilled if ye say sooth?

71.author: Hilali & Khan
71.text: And they (the disbelievers in the Oneness of Allah) say: "When (will) this promise (be fulfilled), if you are truthful?"

71.author: Itani
71.text: And they say, “When is this promise, if you are truthful?”

71.author: Maududi
71.text: They also say: "Tell us when this threat will come to pass, if you are truthful."

71.author: Mubarakpuri
71.text: And they say: "When this promise, if you are truthful"

71.author: Pickthall
71.text: And they say: When (will) this promise (be fulfilled), if ye are truthful?

71.author: Qarai
71.text: They say, ‘When will this promise be fulfilled, if you are truthful?’

71.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
71.text: And they ask: 'When will this promise come, if what you say is true'

71.author: Saheeh International
71.text: And they say, "When is [the fulfillment of] this promise, if you should be truthful?"

71.author: Sarwar
71.text: They ask, "When the Day of Judgment will come, if it is true at all?"

71.author: Shakir
71.text: And they say: When will this threat come to pass, if you are truthful?

71.author: Wahiduddin Khan
71.text: They ask, "When will this promise be fulfilled, if what you say be true?"

71.author: Yusuf Ali
71.text: They also say: "When will this promise (come to pass)? (Say) if ye are truthful."

71.author: Transliteration
71.text: Wayaqooloona mata hatha alwaAAdu in kuntum sadiqeena

72.author: Ahmed Ali
72.text: Say: "Perhaps some of what you wish to hasten is right behind you."

72.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
72.text: Say, “It could be that a part of what you are impatient for is (already) after you.”

72.author: Arberry
72.text: Say: 'It may be that riding behind you already is some part of that you seek to hasten on.

72.author: Daryabadi
72.text: Say thou: belike close behind you may be some of that which ye would hasten on.

72.author: Hilali & Khan
72.text: Say: "Perhaps that which you wish to hasten on, may be close behind you.

72.author: Itani
72.text: Say, “Perhaps some of what you are impatient for has drawn near.”

72.author: Maududi
72.text: Say: "The chastisement whose hastening you have been asking for, maybe a part of it has drawn quite near to you."

72.author: Mubarakpuri
72.text: Say: "Perhaps that which you wish to hasten on, may be close behind you."

72.author: Pickthall
72.text: Say: It may be that a part of that which ye would hasten on is close behind you.

72.author: Qarai
72.text: Say, ‘Perhaps there is right behind you some of what you seek to hasten.’

72.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
72.text: Say: 'It may be that part of what you seek to hasten on is already riding behind you'

72.author: Saheeh International
72.text: Say, "Perhaps it is close behind you - some of that for which you are impatient.

72.author: Sarwar
72.text: Say, "Perhaps some of the things which you wish to experience immediately are very close to you."

72.author: Shakir
72.text: Say: Maybe there may have drawn near to you somewhat of that which you seek to hasten on.

72.author: Wahiduddin Khan
72.text: Say, "It may be that a part of what you would hasten on is close behind you."

72.author: Yusuf Ali
72.text: Say: "It may be that some of the events which ye wish to hasten on may be (close) in your pursuit!"

72.author: Transliteration
72.text: Qul AAasa an yakoona radifa lakum baAAdu allathee tastaAAjiloona

73.author: Ahmed Ali
73.text: Verily your Lord is gracious to men, though most men are ungrateful.

73.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
73.text: Indeed your Lord is Most Munificent upon mankind, but most men do not give thanks.

73.author: Arberry
73.text: Surely thy Lord is bountiful to men; but most of them are not thankful.

73.author: Daryabadi
73.text: And verily thine Lord is full of grace for mankind, but most of them give not thanks.

73.author: Hilali & Khan
73.text: "Verily, your Lord is full of Grace for mankind, yet most of them do not give thanks."

73.author: Itani
73.text: Your Lord is gracious towards humanity, but most of them are not thankful.

73.author: Maududi
73.text: Indeed Your Lord is exceedingly bountiful to mankind. Yet most of them do not give thanks.

73.author: Mubarakpuri
73.text: Verily, your Lord is full of grace for mankind, yet most of them do not give thanks.

73.author: Pickthall
73.text: Lo! thy Lord is full of bounty for mankind, but most of them do not give thanks.

73.author: Qarai
73.text: Indeed your Lord is gracious to mankind, but most of them do not give thanks.

73.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
73.text: Surely, your Lord is bountiful to the people; yet most of them do not give thanks.

73.author: Saheeh International
73.text: And indeed, your Lord is full of bounty for the people, but most of them do not show gratitude."

73.author: Sarwar
73.text: Your Lord has many favors for mankind but most of them are ungrateful.

73.author: Shakir
73.text: And surely your Lord is the Lord of grace to men, but most of them are not grateful.

73.author: Wahiduddin Khan
73.text: Truly, your Lord is bountiful to mankind, but most of them are not grateful.

73.author: Yusuf Ali
73.text: But verily thy Lord is full of grace to mankind: Yet most of them are ungrateful.

73.author: Transliteration
73.text: Wainna rabbaka lathoo fadlin AAala alnnasi walakinna aktharahum la yashkuroona

74.author: Ahmed Ali
74.text: Verily your Lord knows what lies hidden in their breasts and what they disclose.

74.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
74.text: And indeed your Lord knows what is hidden in their bosoms, and what they disclose.

74.author: Arberry
74.text: Surely thy Lord knows what their hearts conceal, and what they publish.

74.author: Daryabadi
74.text: And verily thine Lord knoweth whatsoever their breasts conceal and whatsoever they make known.

74.author: Hilali & Khan
74.text: And verily, your Lord knows what their breasts conceal and what they reveal.

74.author: Itani
74.text: And your Lord knows what their hearts conceal, and what they reveal.

74.author: Maududi
74.text: Verily your Lord knows all that their hearts conceal as well as all that they reveal.

74.author: Mubarakpuri
74.text: And verily, your Lord knows what their breasts conceal and what they reveal.

74.author: Pickthall
74.text: Lo! thy Lord knoweth surely all that their bosoms hide, and all that they proclaim.

74.author: Qarai
74.text: Your Lord knows whatever their breasts conceal, and whatever they disclose.

74.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
74.text: Surely, your Lord knows what they hide in their hearts and what they reveal.

74.author: Saheeh International
74.text: And indeed, your Lord knows what their breasts conceal and what they declare.

74.author: Sarwar
74.text: Your Lord certainly knows whatever their hearts conceal or reveal.

74.author: Shakir
74.text: And most surely your Lord knows what their breasts conceal and what they manifest.

74.author: Wahiduddin Khan
74.text: The Lord knows full well what they conceal in their hearts and what they disclose:

74.author: Yusuf Ali
74.text: And verily thy Lord knoweth all that their hearts do hide. As well as all that they reveal.

74.author: Transliteration
74.text: Wainna rabbaka layaAAlamu ma tukinnu sudooruhum wama yuAAlinoona

75.author: Ahmed Ali
75.text: There is nothing of the hidden in the heavens and the earth that is not recorded in the luminous Book.

75.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
75.text: And all the hidden secrets of the heavens and the earth are (written) in a clear Book. (The Preserved Tablet)

75.author: Arberry
75.text: And not a thing is there hidden in heaven and earth but it is in a Manifest Book.

75.author: Daryabadi
75.text: And naught there is hidden in the heaven or the earth but it is in a Manifest Book.

75.author: Hilali & Khan
75.text: And there is nothing hidden in the heaven and the earth, but is in a Clear Book (i.e. Al-Lauh Al-Mahfuz).

75.author: Itani
75.text: There is no mystery in the heaven and the earth, but it is in a Clear Book.

75.author: Maududi
75.text: There is nothing that is hidden - be it in the heaven or the earth - but is recorded in a Clear Book.

75.author: Mubarakpuri
75.text: And there is nothing hidden in the heaven and the earth but it is in a Clear Book.

75.author: Pickthall
75.text: And there is nothing hidden in the heaven or the earth but it is in a clear Record.

75.author: Qarai
75.text: There is no invisible thing in the heaven and the earth but it is in a manifest Book.

75.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
75.text: There is not a thing hidden in the heaven and earth except that it is in a Clear Book.

75.author: Saheeh International
75.text: And there is nothing concealed within the heaven and the earth except that it is in a clear Register.

75.author: Sarwar
75.text: All the secrets in heavens and earth are recorded in the illustrious Book.

75.author: Shakir
75.text: And there is nothing concealed in the heaven and the earth but it is in a clear book.

75.author: Wahiduddin Khan
75.text: there is nothing hidden in heaven and on earth, but is recorded in a clear Book.

75.author: Yusuf Ali
75.text: Nor is there aught of the unseen, in heaven or earth, but is (recorded) in a clear record.

75.author: Transliteration
75.text: Wama min ghaibatin fee alssamai waalardi illa fee kitabin mubeenin

76.author: Ahmed Ali
76.text: Indeed this Qur'an explains to the children of Israel much of what they are at variance.

76.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
76.text: Indeed this Qur’an narrates to the Descendants of Israel most of the matters in which they differ.

76.author: Arberry
76.text: Surely this Koran relates to the Children of Israel most of that concerning which they are at variance;

76.author: Daryabadi
76.text: Verily this Qur'an recounteth with truth unto the Children of Isra'il much of that wherein they differ.

76.author: Hilali & Khan
76.text: Verily, this Quran narrates to the Children of Israel most of that about which they differ.

76.author: Itani
76.text: This Quran relates to the Children of Israel most of what they differ about.

76.author: Maududi
76.text: Surely this Qur'an explains to the Children of Israel most of the matters concerning which they have disagreements

76.author: Mubarakpuri
76.text: Verily, this Qur'an narrates to the Children of Israel most of that in which they differ.

76.author: Pickthall
76.text: Lo! this Qur'an narrateth unto the Children of Israel most of that concerning which they differ.

76.author: Qarai
76.text: This Quran recounts for the Children of Israel most of what they differ about,

76.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
76.text: Surely, this Koran relates to the Children of Israel most of that which they are at variance.

76.author: Saheeh International
76.text: Indeed, this Qur'an relates to the Children of Israel most of that over which they disagree.

76.author: Sarwar
76.text: This Quran tells the Israelites most of the matters about which they had disputes among themselves.

76.author: Shakir
76.text: Surely this Quran declares to the children of Israel most of what they differ in.

76.author: Wahiduddin Khan
76.text: This Quran explains to the children of Israel much of what they differ over,

76.author: Yusuf Ali
76.text: Verily this Qur'an doth explain to the Children of Israel most of the matters in which they disagree.

76.author: Transliteration
76.text: Inna hatha alqurana yaqussu AAala banee israeela akthara allathee hum feehi yakhtalifoona

77.author: Ahmed Ali
77.text: It is a guidance and grace for those who believe.

77.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
77.text: And indeed it is a guidance and a mercy for the Muslims.

77.author: Arberry
77.text: it is a guidance, and a mercy unto the believers.

77.author: Daryabadi
77.text: And verily it is a guidance and a mercy unto the believers.

77.author: Hilali & Khan
77.text: And truly, it (this Quran) is a guide and a mercy to the believers.

77.author: Itani
77.text: And it is guidance and mercy for the believers.

77.author: Maududi
77.text: and it is a guidance and mercy for the believers.

77.author: Mubarakpuri
77.text: And truly, it is a guide and a mercy for the believers.

77.author: Pickthall
77.text: And lo! it is a guidance and a mercy for believers.

77.author: Qarai
77.text: and it is indeed a guidance and mercy for the faithful.

77.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
77.text: It is a guidance and a mercy to believers.

77.author: Saheeh International
77.text: And indeed, it is guidance and mercy for the believers.

77.author: Sarwar
77.text: It is a guide and mercy for the believers.

77.author: Shakir
77.text: And most surely it is a guidance and a mercy for the believers.

77.author: Wahiduddin Khan
77.text: certainly it is guidance and a blessing for the believers.

77.author: Yusuf Ali
77.text: And it certainly is a Guide and a Mercy to those who believe.

78.author: Ahmed Ali
78.text: Surely your Lord in His wisdom will decide between them. He is all-mighty and all-knowing.

78.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
78.text: Indeed your Lord judges between them with His command; and He is the Almighty, the All Knowing.

78.author: Arberry
78.text: Surely thy Lord will decide between them by His Judgment; He is the All-mighty, the All-knowing.

78.author: Daryabadi
78.text: And verily thine Lord shall decide between them with His judgment, and He is the Mighty, the Knowing.

78.author: Hilali & Khan
78.text: Verily, your Lord will decide between them (various sects) by His Judgement. And He is the All-Mighty, the All-Knowing.

78.author: Itani
78.text: Your Lord will judge between them by His wisdom. He is the Almighty, the All-Knowing.

78.author: Maududi
78.text: Indeed your Lord will decide between them by His judgement. He is All-Mighty, All-Knowing.

78.author: Mubarakpuri
78.text: Verily, your Lord will decide between them by His judgement. And He is the All-Mighty, the All-Knowing.

78.author: Pickthall
78.text: Lo! thy Lord will judge between them of His wisdom, and He is the Mighty, the Wise.

78.author: Qarai
78.text: Your Lord will decide between them by His judgement, and He is the All-mighty, the All-knowing.

78.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
78.text: Surely, by His Judgement, your Lord will decide between them. He is the Mighty, the Knower.

78.author: Saheeh International
78.text: Indeed, your Lord will judge between them by His [wise] judgement. And He is the Exalted in Might, the Knowing.

78.author: Sarwar
78.text: Your Lord will judge among them according to His own decree. He is Majestic and All-Knowing.

78.author: Shakir
78.text: Surely your Lord will judge between them by his judgment, and He is the Mighty, the knowing.

78.author: Wahiduddin Khan
78.text: Certainly your Lord will decide between them in His wisdom, He is the Almighty, the All Knowing,

78.author: Yusuf Ali
78.text: Verily thy Lord will decide between them by His Decree: and He is Exalted in Might, All-Knowing.

78.author: Transliteration
78.text: Inna rabbaka yaqdee baynahum bihukmihi wahuwa alAAazeezu alAAaleemu

79.author: Ahmed Ali
79.text: So you place your trust in God. Certainly you stand on positive truth.

79.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
79.text: Therefore rely on Allah; you are indeed upon the clear Truth.

79.author: Arberry
79.text: So put thy trust in God; thou art upon the manifest truth.

79.author: Daryabadi
79.text: Wherefore put thy trust in Allah; verily thou art on manifest truth.

79.author: Hilali & Khan
79.text: So put your trust in Allah; surely, you (O Muhammad SAW) are on manifest truth.

79.author: Itani
79.text: So rely on God. You are upon the clear truth.

79.author: Maududi
79.text: So put your trust in Allah for you are on the manifest truth.

79.author: Mubarakpuri
79.text: So, put your trust in Allah; surely, you are on manifest truth.

79.author: Pickthall
79.text: Therefor (O Muhammad) put thy trust in Allah, for thou (standest) on the plain Truth.

79.author: Qarai
79.text: So put your trust in Allah, for you indeed stand on the manifest truth.

79.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
79.text: Therefore put your trust in Allah, for you are upon a clear truth.

79.author: Saheeh International
79.text: So rely upon Allah; indeed, you are upon the clear truth.

79.author: Sarwar
79.text: Trust in God for you follow the manifest truth.

79.author: Shakir
79.text: Therefore rely on Allah; surely you are on the clear truth.

79.author: Wahiduddin Khan
79.text: so put your trust in God. Surely you are on the path of manifest truth.

79.author: Yusuf Ali
79.text: So put thy trust in Allah: for thou art on (the path of) manifest Truth.

79.author: Transliteration
79.text: Fatawakkal AAala Allahi innaka AAala alhaqqi almubeeni

80.author: Ahmed Ali
80.text: You cannot make the dead to listen, or the deaf to hear the call, when they have turned their backs,

80.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
80.text: Indeed the dead* do not listen to your call nor do the deaf* listen to your call, when they flee turning back. (The dead and deaf implies the disbelievers.)

80.author: Arberry
80.text: Thou shalt not make the dead to hear, neither shalt thou make the deaf to hear the call when they turn about, retreating.

80.author: Daryabadi
80.text: Verily thou canst not make the dead hear, nor canst thou make the deaf hear the call when they flee turning their backs.

80.author: Hilali & Khan
80.text: Verily, you cannot make the dead to hear (i.e. benefit them and similarly the disbelievers), nor can you make the deaf to hear the call, when they flee, turning their backs.

80.author: Itani
80.text: You cannot make the dead hear, nor can you make the deaf hear the call if they turn their backs and flee.

80.author: Maududi
80.text: Surely you cannot make the dead hear you, nor can you make the deaf hear your call if they turn back in flight,

80.author: Mubarakpuri
80.text: Verily, you cannot make the dead to hear nor can you make the deaf to hear the call, when they flee, turning their backs.

80.author: Pickthall
80.text: Lo! thou canst not make the dead to hear, nor canst thou make the deaf to hear the call when they have turned to flee;

80.author: Qarai
80.text: You cannot make the dead hear, nor can you make the deaf listen to your call when they turn their backs,

80.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
80.text: You do not make the dead hear, nor do you make the deaf hear you call when they turn about, retreating.

80.author: Saheeh International
80.text: Indeed, you will not make the dead hear, nor will you make the deaf hear the call when they have turned their backs retreating.

80.author: Sarwar
80.text: You cannot make the dead listen and the deaf are unable to hear calls. Thus, they turn back on their heels.

80.author: Shakir
80.text: Surely you do not make the dead to hear, and you do not make the deaf to hear the call when they go back retreating.

80.author: Wahiduddin Khan
80.text: You cannot make the dead hear, nor can you make the deaf hear your call, when they turn their backs on it,

80.author: Yusuf Ali
80.text: Truly thou canst not cause the dead to listen, nor canst thou cause the deaf to hear the call, (especially) when they turn back in retreat.

80.author: Transliteration
80.text: Innaka la tusmiAAu almawta wala tusmiAAu alssumma aldduAAaa itha wallaw mudbireena

81.author: Ahmed Ali
81.text: Nor can you lead the blind when they have gone astray. You can make none hear except those who believe in Our signs and have come to peace and submission.

81.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
81.text: And you will not guide the blind out of their error; and none listen to you except those who accept faith in Our signs, and they are Muslims.

81.author: Arberry
81.text: Thou shalt not guide the blind out of their error neither shalt thou make any to hear, save such as believe in Our signs, and so surrender.

81.author: Daryabadi
81.text: Nor canst thou lead the blind out of their error. Thou canst make none hear save these who believe in Our signs and who have submitted themselves.

81.author: Hilali & Khan
81.text: Nor can you lead the blind out of their error, you can only make to hear those who believe in Our Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.), and who have submitted (themselves to Allah in Islam as Muslims).

81.author: Itani
81.text: Nor can you guide the blind out of their straying. You can make no one listen, except those who believe in Our verses; for they are Muslims.

81.author: Maududi
81.text: nor can you direct the blind to the Right Way, preventing them from falling into error. You can make only those who believe in Our verses to hear the call and then submit.

81.author: Mubarakpuri
81.text: Nor can you lead the blind out of their error. You can only make to hear those who believe in Our Ayat, so they submit (became Muslims).

81.author: Pickthall
81.text: Nor canst thou lead the blind out of their error. Thou canst make none to hear, save those who believe Our revelations and who have surrendered.

81.author: Qarai
81.text: nor can you lead the blind out of their error. You can make only those hear you who believe in Our signs and have submitted.

81.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
81.text: Nor do you guide the blind out of their error, nor do you make any to hear, except those who believe in Our verses and are Muslims.

81.author: Saheeh International
81.text: And you cannot guide the blind away from their error. You will only make hear those who believe in Our verses so they are Muslims [submitting to Allah].

81.author: Sarwar
81.text: You cannot guide the straying blind ones. You can only make hear those who believe in Our revelations submissively.

81.author: Shakir
81.text: Nor can you be a guide to the blind out of their error; you cannot make to bear (any one) except those who believe in Our communications, so they submit.

81.author: Wahiduddin Khan
81.text: nor can you guide the blind out of their error. You can make only those hear you who believe in Our revelations and surrender themselves to Us.

81.author: Yusuf Ali
81.text: Nor canst thou be a guide to the blind, (to prevent them) from straying: only those wilt thou get to listen who believe in Our Signs, and they will bow in Islam.

81.author: Transliteration
81.text: Wama anta bihadee alAAumyi AAan dalalatihim in tusmiAAu illa man yuminu biayatina fahum muslimoona

82.author: Ahmed Ali
82.text: When the sentence will have been passed against them, We shall bring forth beastly brutes from the earth who will torment them, for men certainly do not believe Our signs.

82.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
82.text: And when the Word (promise) appears upon them, We shall bring forth for them a beast from the earth to speak to them – because the people were not accepting faith in Our signs. (This beast will rise from the earth, when the Last Day draws near.)

82.author: Arberry
82.text: When the Word falls on them, We shall bring forth for them out of the earth a beast that shall speak unto them: 'Mankind had no faith in Our signs.'

82.author: Daryabadi
82.text: And when the word shall come to be fulfilled concerning them, We shall bring forth a beast of the earth speaking unto them, that the people have not of Our signs been convinced.

82.author: Hilali & Khan
82.text: And when the Word (of torment) is fulfilled against them, We shall bring out from the earth a beast to them, which will speak to them because mankind believed not with certainty in Our Ayat (Verses of the Quran and Prophet Muhammad SAW).

82.author: Itani
82.text: And when the Word has fallen on them, We will bring out for them from the earth a creature which will say to them that the people are uncertain of Our revelations.

82.author: Maududi
82.text: And when the time for the fulfilment of Our Word against them will come, We shall bring forth for them a beast from the earth who will speak to them because people did not believe in Our Signs.

82.author: Mubarakpuri
82.text: And when the Word is fulfilled against them, We shall bring out from the earth a beast for them, to speak to them because mankind believed not with certainty in Our Ayat.

82.author: Pickthall
82.text: And when the word is fulfilled concerning them, We shall bring forth a beast of the earth to speak unto them because mankind had not faith in Our revelations.

82.author: Qarai
82.text: When the word [of judgement] falls upon them, We shall bring out for them an Animal from the earth who shall tell them that the people had no faith in Our signs.

82.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
82.text: And when the Word falls on them, We will bring out from the earth a beast that shall speak to them: 'Indeed the people were not certain of Our verses.'

82.author: Saheeh International
82.text: And when the word befalls them, We will bring forth for them a creature from the earth speaking to them, [saying] that the people were, of Our verses, not certain [in faith].

82.author: Sarwar
82.text: When the word about them comes true We shall make a creature appear to them on earth who will tell them that people had no faith in Our revelations.

82.author: Shakir
82.text: And when the word shall come to pass against them, We shall bring forth for them a creature from the earth that shall wound them, because people did not believe in Our communications.

82.author: Wahiduddin Khan
82.text: When God's word is justly carried out against them, We will produce a dabbah from the earth which will tell them that mankind had no real faith in Our signs.

82.author: Yusuf Ali
82.text: And when the Word is fulfilled against them (the unjust), we shall produce from the earth a beast to (face) them: He will speak to them, for that mankind did not believe with assurance in Our Signs.

82.author: Transliteration
82.text: Waitha waqaAAa alqawlu AAalayhim akhrajna lahum dabbatan mina alardi tukallimuhum anna alnnasa kanoo biayatina la yooqinoona

83.author: Ahmed Ali
83.text: The day We shall gather from every community a section of those who denied Our signs, and they will be brought (in separate groups),

83.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
83.text: And the Day when We will raise from every nation an army that denies Our signs – so the former groups will be held back for the latter to join them.

83.author: Arberry
83.text: Upon the day when We shall muster out of every nation a troop of those that cried lies to Our signs, duly disposed,

83.author: Daryabadi
83.text: And remind them of the Day whereon We shall Rather from every community a troop of those who belied Our signs, and they shall be held in order.

83.author: Hilali & Khan
83.text: And (remember) the Day when We shall gather out of every nation a troop of those who denied Our Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.), and (then) they (all) shall be gathered (and driven to the place of reckoning),

83.author: Itani
83.text: On the Day when We gather from every community a group of those who rejected Our revelations; and they will be restrained.

83.author: Maududi
83.text: Just imagine the Day when We shall muster from every nation a large group of those who gave the lie to Our Signs, and they shall be duly arranged in ranks

83.author: Mubarakpuri
83.text: And (remember) the Day when We shall gather out of every nation a Fawj of those who denied Our Ayat, and they shall be driven,

83.author: Pickthall
83.text: And (remind them of) the Day when We shall gather out of every nation a host of those who denied Our revelations, and they will be set in array;

83.author: Qarai
83.text: On that day We shall resurrect from every nation a group of those who denied Our signs, and they will be held in check.

83.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
83.text: On that Day We shall gather a crowd from every nation of those who belied Our verses, so they are gathered.

83.author: Saheeh International
83.text: And [warn of] the Day when We will gather from every nation a company of those who deny Our signs, and they will be [driven] in rows

83.author: Sarwar
83.text: On the day when We resurrect from every nation a group from among those who had rejected Our revelations, they will be kept confined in ranks.

83.author: Shakir
83.text: And on the day when We will gather from every nation a party from among those who rejected Our communications, then they shall be formed into groups.

83.author: Wahiduddin Khan
83.text: On that Day We shall assemble together a host from every community of those who cried lies to Our revelations and they will be grouped

83.author: Yusuf Ali
83.text: One day We shall gather together from every people a troop of those who reject our Signs, and they shall be kept in ranks,-

83.author: Transliteration
83.text: Wayawma nahshuru min kulli ommatin fawjan mimman yukaththibu biayatina fahum yoozaAAoona

84.author: Ahmed Ali
84.text: So that when they come (before the Lord) He will say: "Did you deny My signs without having understood them? Or what was it that you were doing?"

84.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
84.text: Until when they all come together, He will say, “Did you deny My signs whereas your knowledge had not reached it, or what were the deeds you were doing?”

84.author: Arberry
84.text: till, when they are come, He shall say, 'Did you cry lies to My signs, not comprehending them in knowledge, or what have you been doing?'

84.author: Daryabadi
84.text: Until when they shall have come, He Shall say: belied ye My signs when ye encompassed them not in Your knowledge; nay, what else was it that ye have been working?

84.author: Hilali & Khan
84.text: Till, when they come (before their Lord at the place of reckoning), He will say: "Did you deny My Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.) when you comprehended them not in knowledge, or what (else) was it that you used to do?"

84.author: Itani
84.text: Until, when they arrive, He will say, “Did you reject My revelations without comprehending them? Or what is it you were doing?”

84.author: Maududi
84.text: until, when all of them have arrived, Allah will say: "Did you give the lie to My Signs even without encompassing them with your knowledge? If that is not so, what did you do?"

84.author: Mubarakpuri
84.text: Till, when they come, He will say: "Did you deny My Ayat whereas you comprehended them not by knowledge, or what was it that you used to do"

84.author: Pickthall
84.text: Till, when they come (before their Lord), He will say: Did ye deny My revelations when ye could not compass them in knowledge, or what was it that ye did?

84.author: Qarai
84.text: When they come, He will say, ‘Did you deny My signs without comprehending them in knowledge? What was it that you used to do?’

84.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
84.text: When they have come, He will say: 'Did you belie My verses, although you knew nothing, or what was it you were doing'

84.author: Saheeh International
84.text: Until, when they arrive [at the place of Judgement], He will say, "Did you deny My signs while you encompassed them not in knowledge, or what [was it that] you were doing?"

84.author: Sarwar
84.text: When they will be brought into the presence of God, He will ask them, "Did you reject My revelations without fully understanding them. What did you know about them if you had any knowledge at all?

84.author: Shakir
84.text: Until when they come, He will say: Did you reject My communications while you had no comprehensive knowledge of them? Or what was it that you did?

84.author: Wahiduddin Khan
84.text: then, when they have arrived, He will say, "Did you deny My revelations, even though you did not have proper knowledge of them? Or what was it that you were doing?"

84.author: Yusuf Ali
84.text: Until, when they come (before the Judgment-seat), (Allah) will say: "Did ye reject My Signs, though ye comprehended them not in knowledge, or what was it ye did?"

84.author: Transliteration
84.text: Hatta itha jaoo qala akaththabtum biayatee walam tuheetoo biha AAilman ammatha kuntum taAAmaloona

85.author: Ahmed Ali
85.text: When the sentence will have been passed upon them for their wickedness, they will not be able to say a word.

85.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
85.text: And the Word has appeared upon them because of their injustice, so now they do not speak.

85.author: Arberry
85.text: And the Word shall fall upon them because of the evil they committed, while they speak naught.

85.author: Daryabadi
85.text: And the Word shall be fulfilled concerning them, because they did wrong, and they shall not be able to speak.

85.author: Hilali & Khan
85.text: And the Word (of torment) will be fulfilled against them, because they have done wrong, and they will be unable to speak (in order to defend themselves).

85.author: Itani
85.text: The Word will come down upon them for their wrongdoing, and they will not speak.

85.author: Maududi
85.text: And the Word will come to pass against them because of their wrong-doing: they will then be able to utter nothing.

85.author: Mubarakpuri
85.text: And the Word will be fulfilled against them, because they have done wrong, and they will be unable to speak.

85.author: Pickthall
85.text: And the Word will be fulfilled concerning them because they have done wrong, and they will not speak.

85.author: Qarai
85.text: And the word [of judgement] shall fall upon them for their wrongdoing, and they will not speak.

85.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
85.text: And the Word shall fall upon them because of the evil they committed, and they shall be speechless.

85.author: Saheeh International
85.text: And the decree will befall them for the wrong they did, and they will not [be able to] speak.

85.author: Sarwar
85.text: They will become subject to punishment because of their injustice. Thus, they will not speak.

85.author: Shakir
85.text: And the word shall come to pass against them because they were unjust, so they shall not speak.

85.author: Wahiduddin Khan
85.text: The verdict will be given against them, because they did wrong, and they will be speechless.

85.author: Yusuf Ali
85.text: And the Word will be fulfilled against them, because of their wrong-doing, and they will be unable to speak (in plea).

85.author: Transliteration
85.text: WawaqaAAa alqawlu AAalayhim bima thalamoo fahum la yantiqoona

86.author: Ahmed Ali
86.text: Do they not see that We made the night for them to rest, the day to make things clear? Indeed there are signs in this for people who believe.

86.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
86.text: Did they not see that We created the night for them to rest in, and created the day providing sight? Indeed in this are signs for the people who have faith.

86.author: Arberry
86.text: Have they not seen how We made the night for them, to repose in it, and the day, to see? Surely in that is a sign for a people who are believers.

86.author: Daryabadi
86.text: Observe they not that We have appointed the night that they may repose therein, and the day sight-giving? Verily herein are signs unto a people who believe.

86.author: Hilali & Khan
86.text: See they not that We have made the night for them to rest therein, and the day sight-giving? Verily, in this are Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.) for the people who believe.

86.author: Itani
86.text: Do they not see that We made the night for them to rest therein, and the day for visibility? Surely in that are signs for people who believe.

86.author: Maududi
86.text: Did they not perceive that We had made the night so that they may repose in it and made the day clear and shining. Surely there are Signs in this for those who believe.

86.author: Mubarakpuri
86.text: See they not that We have made the night for them to rest therein, and the day sight-giving Verily, in this are Ayat for the people who believe.

86.author: Pickthall
86.text: Have they not seen how We have appointed the night that they may rest therein, and the day sight-giving? Lo! therein verily are portents for a people who believe.

86.author: Qarai
86.text: Do they not see that We made the night that they may rest in it, and the day to provide visibility. There are indeed signs in that for a people who have faith.

86.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
86.text: Have they not seen how We have made the night for them to rest in and the day to see? Surely, there are signs in this for the nation who believe.

86.author: Saheeh International
86.text: Do they not see that We made the night that they may rest therein and the day giving sight? Indeed in that are signs for a people who believe.

86.author: Sarwar
86.text: Have they not seen that We have created the night for them to rest and the day for them to see. In this there is evidence for the believing people.

86.author: Shakir
86.text: Do they not consider that We have made the night that. they may rest therein, and the day to give light? Most surely there are signs in this for a people who believe.

86.author: Wahiduddin Khan
86.text: Do they not see that We have made the night for them to rest in and the day to give them light? There are certainly signs in that for people who believe.

86.author: Yusuf Ali
86.text: See they not that We have made the Night for them to rest in and the Day to give them light? Verily in this are Signs for any people that believe!

86.author: Transliteration
86.text: Alam yaraw anna jaAAalna allayla liyaskunoo feehi waalnnahara mubsiran inna fee thalika laayatin liqawmin yuminoona

87.author: Ahmed Ali
87.text: The day the trumpet blast is sounded whoever is in the heavens and the earth will be terrified, save those whom God please, and all will appear before Him in abjectness.

87.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
87.text: And the Day when the Trumpet is blown – so all those who are in the heavens and the earth will be terrified, except whomever Allah wills; and everyone has come to Him, in submission.

87.author: Arberry
87.text: On the day the Trumpet is blown, and terrified is whosoever is in the heavens and earth, excepting whom God wills, and every one shall come to Him, all utterly abject;

87.author: Daryabadi
87.text: And remind them of the Day whereon the trumpet shall be blown, and affrighted will be those who are in the heavens and the earth, save him whom Allah willeth. And all shall come unto Him, lowly.

87.author: Hilali & Khan
87.text: And (remember) the Day on which the Trumpet will be blown and all who are in the heavens and all who are on the earth, will be terrified except him whom Allah will (exempt). And all shall come to Him humbled.

87.author: Itani
87.text: On the Day when the Trumpet is blown, everyone in the heavens and the earth will be horrified, except whomever God wills; and everyone will come before Him in humility.

87.author: Maududi
87.text: The Day when the Trumpet will be blown all those who are in the heavens and on the earth shall be terror stricken - all except those whom Allah wills - and everyone shall come to Him utterly abject.

87.author: Mubarakpuri
