87.text: And (remember) the Day on which the Trumpet (Sur) will be blown -- and all who are in the heavens and all who are on the earth, will be terrified except him whom Allah wills. And all shall come to Him, humbled.

87.author: Pickthall
87.text: And (remind them of) the Day when the Trumpet will be blown, and all who are in the heavens and the earth will start in fear, save him whom Allah willeth. And all come unto Him, humbled.

87.author: Qarai
87.text: The day when the trumpet is blown, whoever is in the heavens and whoever is on the earth will be terrified, except such as Allah wishes, and all will come to Him in utter humility.

87.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
87.text: On the Day the Horn shall be blown all who live in the heavens and earth will be terrified, except those whom Allah wills. All shall come to Him humbled.

87.author: Saheeh International
87.text: And [warn of] the Day the Horn will be blown, and whoever is in the heavens and whoever is on the earth will be terrified except whom Allah wills. And all will come to Him humbled.

87.author: Sarwar
87.text: Everyone in the heavens and earth will be terrified on the day when the trumpet will be sounded except those whom God will save. Everyone will humbly come into the presence of God.

87.author: Shakir
87.text: And on the day when the trumpet shall be blown, then those who are in the heavens and those who are in the earth shall be terrified except such as Allah please, and all shall come to him abased.

87.author: Wahiduddin Khan
87.text: On the Day when the trumpet is blown, whoever is in the heavens and whoever is on the earth will be struck with terror, except for those whom God wishes to spare. All shall come to Him in utter humility.

87.author: Yusuf Ali
87.text: And the Day that the Trumpet will be sounded - then will be smitten with terror those who are in the heavens, and those who are on earth, except such as Allah will please (to exempt): and all shall come to His (Presence) as beings conscious of their lowliness.

87.author: Transliteration
87.text: Wayawma yunfakhu fee alssoori fafaziAAa man fee alssamawati waman fee alardi illa man shaa Allahu wakullun atawhu dakhireena

88.author: Ahmed Ali
88.text: You will see the mountains and think they are firmly planted, but they will pass away like flying clouds: Artistry of God who perfected everything. He is indeed fully aware of what you do.

88.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
88.text: And you will see the mountains presuming them fixed whereas they will be moving like the clouds; this is Allah’s making, Who created all things with wisdom; indeed He is Informed of what you do.

88.author: Arberry
88.text: and thou shalt see the mountains, that thou supposest fixed, passing by like clouds -- God's handiwork, who has created everything very well. He is aware of the things you do.

88.author: Daryabadi
88.text: And thou shalt see the mountains thou deemest solid passing away as the passing away of the clouds: the handiwork of Allah Who hath perfected everything. Verily He is Aware of all that ye do.

88.author: Hilali & Khan
88.text: And you will see the mountains and think them solid, but they shall pass away as the passing away of the clouds. The Work of Allah, Who perfected all things, verily! He is Well-Acquainted with what you do.

88.author: Itani
88.text: And you see the mountains, and imagine them fixed, yet they pass, as the passing of the clouds—the making of God, who has perfected everything. He is fully Informed of what you do.

88.author: Maududi
88.text: You now see the mountains and consider them firmly fixed, but then they shall pass away even as clouds pass away. That will be the handiwork of Allah Who has created everything with perfect wisdom. He is well aware of what you do.

88.author: Mubarakpuri
88.text: And you will see the mountains and think them solid, but they shall pass away as the passing away of the clouds. The work of Allah, Who perfected all things, verily, He is well-acquainted with what you do.

88.author: Pickthall
88.text: And thou seest the hills thou deemest solid flying with the flight of clouds: the doing of Allah Who perfecteth all things. Lo! He is Informed of what ye do.

88.author: Qarai
88.text: You see the mountains, which you suppose, to be stationary, while they drift like passing clouds—the handiwork of Allah who has made everything faultless. He is indeed well aware of what you do.

88.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
88.text: And you will see the mountains which you think to be firm pass by like clouds. (Such is the) making of Allah, who has created everything well. He is Aware of the things you do.

88.author: Saheeh International
88.text: And you see the mountains, thinking them rigid, while they will pass as the passing of clouds. [It is] the work of Allah, who perfected all things. Indeed, He is Acquainted with that which you do.

88.author: Sarwar
88.text: You think the mountains are solid. In fact, they move like clouds. It is God's technique which has established everything perfectly. He is well Aware of what you do.

88.author: Shakir
88.text: And you see the mountains, you think them to be solid, and they shall pass away as the passing away of the cloud-- the handiwork of Allah Who has made every thing thoroughly; surely He is Aware of what you do.

88.author: Wahiduddin Khan
88.text: You see the mountains and think them firmly fixed. But they shall pass away as the clouds pass away. Such is the work of God, who has ordered all things to perfection: He is fully aware of what you do.

88.author: Yusuf Ali
88.text: Thou seest the mountains and thinkest them firmly fixed: but they shall pass away as the clouds pass away: (such is) the artistry of Allah, who disposes of all things in perfect order: for he is well acquainted with all that ye do.

88.author: Transliteration
88.text: Watara aljibala tahsabuha jamidatan wahiya tamurru marra alssahabi sunAAa Allahi allathee atqana kulla shayin innahu khabeerun bima tafAAaloona

89.author: Ahmed Ali
89.text: Whoever comes with good (deeds) will receive better than (what he had done), and be safe that day from terror.

89.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
89.text: Whoever brings a good deed – for him is a reward better than it; and they will be safe from the terror on that Day.

89.author: Arberry
89.text: Whosoever comes with a good deed, he shall have better than it; and they shall be secure from terror that day.

89.author: Daryabadi
89.text: Whosoever will bring good shall have better than the worth thereof; and they from the terror of that Day will be secure.

89.author: Hilali & Khan
89.text: Whoever brings a good deed (i.e. Belief in the Oneness of Allah along with every deed of righteousness), will have better than its worth, and they will be safe from the terror on that Day.

89.author: Itani
89.text: Whoever brings a virtue will receive better than it—and they will be safe from the horrors of that Day.

89.author: Maududi
89.text: Whosoever comes with good will receive a reward better than his deed, and they will be made secure from the terror of that Day.

89.author: Mubarakpuri
89.text: Whoever brings a good deed, will have better than its worth; and they will be safe from the terror on that Day.

89.author: Pickthall
89.text: Whoso bringeth a good deed will have better than its worth; and such are safe from fear that Day.

89.author: Qarai
89.text: Whoever brings virtue shall receive [a reward] better than it; and on that day they will be secure from terror.

89.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
89.text: Whosoever comes with a good deed shall have better than it, and shall be secure from the terrors of that Day.

89.author: Saheeh International
89.text: Whoever comes [at Judgement] with a good deed will have better than it, and they, from the terror of that Day, will be safe.

89.author: Sarwar
89.text: Whoever does a good deed will receive a better reward than what he has done. He will be secure from the terror of the Day of Judgment.

89.author: Shakir
89.text: Whoever brings good, he shall have better than it; and they shall be secure from terror on the day.

89.author: Wahiduddin Khan
89.text: Whoever does a good deed, shall be rewarded with what is better, and will be secure from fear of that Day,

89.author: Yusuf Ali
89.text: If any do good, good will (accrue) to them therefrom; and they will be secure from terror that Day.

89.author: Transliteration
89.text: Man jaa bialhasanati falahu khayrun minha wahum min fazaAAin yawmaithin aminoona

90.author: Ahmed Ali
90.text: But whosoever comes with evil will be flung face forward into the Fire. Can you expect reward for any thing but what you do?

90.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
90.text: And whoever brings evil – so their faces will be dipped into the fire; what reward will you get, except what you did?

90.author: Arberry
90.text: And whosoever comes with an evil deed, their faces shall be thrust into the Fire: 'Are you recompensed but for what you did?'

90.author: Daryabadi
90.text: And whosoever will bring evil --their faces shall be cast down into the Fire. Are ye being requited aught save that which ye have been working?

90.author: Hilali & Khan
90.text: And whoever brings an evil (deed) (i.e. Shirk polytheism, disbelief in the Oneness of Allah and every evil sinful deed), they will be cast down (prone) on their faces in the Fire. (And it will be said to them) "Are you being recompensed anything except what you used to do?"

90.author: Itani
90.text: But whoever brings evil—their faces will be tumbled into the Fire. Will you be rewarded except for what you used to do?

90.author: Maududi
90.text: And whosoever comes with evil, they will be flung upon their faces into the Fire. Will you be recompensed for aught other than what you do?

90.author: Mubarakpuri
90.text: And whoever brings an evil deed, they will be cast down on their faces in the Fire. (And it will be said to them) "Are you being recompensed anything except what you used to do"

90.author: Pickthall
90.text: And whoso bringeth an ill-deed, such will be flung down on their faces in the Fire. Are ye rewarded aught save what ye did?

90.author: Qarai
90.text: But whoever brings vice—they shall be cast on their faces into the Fire [and told:] ‘Shall you not be requited for what you used to do?’

90.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
90.text: But those who come with an evil deed shall have their faces thrust into the Fire: 'Are you recompensed except for your deeds'

90.author: Saheeh International
90.text: And whoever comes with an evil deed - their faces will be overturned into the Fire, [and it will be said], "Are you recompensed except for what you used to do?"

90.author: Sarwar
90.text: Those who commit evil will be thrown headlong into hell fire. (It will be said to them) can you expect any recompense other than what you deserve for your deeds?

90.author: Shakir
90.text: And whoever brings evil, these shall be thrown down on their faces into the fire; shall you be rewarded (for) aught except what you did?

90.author: Wahiduddin Khan
90.text: and those who do evil will be flung down on their faces in the Fire. Are you not rewarded according to your deeds?

90.author: Yusuf Ali
90.text: And if any do evil, their faces will be thrown headlong into the Fire: "Do ye receive a reward other than that which ye have earned by your deeds?"

90.author: Transliteration
90.text: Waman jaa bialssayyiati fakubbat wujoohuhum fee alnnari hal tujzawna illa ma kuntum taAAmaloona

91.author: Ahmed Ali
91.text: (Say): "I am commanded to worship the Lord of this land He has blessed, to whom all things belong; and I am commanded to be one of those who submit,

91.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
91.text: “I (Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him) have been commanded to worship the Lord of this city, Who has deemed it sacred, and everything belongs to Him; and I have been commanded to be among the obedient.”

91.author: Arberry
91.text: I have only been commanded to serve the Lord of this territory which He has made sacred; to Him belongs everything. And I have been commanded to be of those that surrender,

91.author: Daryabadi
91.text: I am commanded only to worship the Lord of this city which He hath sanctified, -and His is every thing - and I am commanded to be of the Muslims;

91.author: Hilali & Khan
91.text: I (Muhammad SAW) have been commanded only to worship the Lord of this city (Makkah), Him Who has sanctified it and His is everything. And I am commanded to be from among the Muslims (those who submit to Allah in Islam).

91.author: Itani
91.text: “I was commanded to worship the Lord of this town, who has sanctified it, and to Whom everything belongs; and I was commanded to be of those who submit.

91.author: Maududi
91.text: (Tell them, O Muhammad): "I have been commanded only to serve the Lord of this city that He has made inviolable, to serve Him to Whom all things belong. I have been commanded to be of those that submit to Allah,

91.author: Mubarakpuri
91.text: I have been commanded only to worship the Lord of this city, Who has sanctified it and to Whom belongs everything. And I am commanded to be from among the Muslims.

91.author: Pickthall
91.text: (Say): I (Muhammad) am commanded only to serve the Lord of this land which He hath hallowed, and unto Whom all things belong. And I am commanded to be of those who surrender (unto Him),

91.author: Qarai
91.text: [Say], ‘I have been commanded to worship the Lord of this city who has made it inviolable and to whom all things belong, and I have been commanded to be among those who submit [to Allah],

91.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
91.text: (Say Prophet Muhammad): 'I am only ordered to worship the Lord of this country, which He has made sacred, everything belongs to Him. And I am ordered to be of the Muslims,

91.author: Saheeh International
91.text: [Say, O Muhammad], "I have only been commanded to worship the Lord of this city, who made it sacred and to whom [belongs] all things. And I am commanded to be of the Muslims [those who submit to Allah]

91.author: Sarwar
91.text: I am commanded to worship the Lord of this town which He has made sacred. To Him belong all things. I am commanded to be a Muslim

91.author: Shakir
91.text: I am commanded only that I should serve the Lord of this city, Who has made it sacred, and His are all things; and I am commanded that I should be of these who submit;

91.author: Wahiduddin Khan
91.text: Say, "I am commanded to serve the Lord of this town, which He has made inviolable and to whom everything belongs; I am commanded to be one of those devoted to Him;

91.author: Yusuf Ali
91.text: For me, I have been commanded to serve the Lord of this city, Him Who has sanctified it and to Whom (belong) all things: and I am commanded to be of those who bow in Islam to Allah's Will,-

91.author: Transliteration
91.text: Innama omirtu an aAAbuda rabba hathihi albaldati allathee harramaha walahu kullu shayin waomirtu an akoona mina almuslimeena

92.author: Ahmed Ali
92.text: And to recite the Qur'an." Whoever comes to guidance does so for himself; as for him who stays astray, tell him: "I am only a warner."

92.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
92.text: “And to recite the Qur’an”; so whoever attained the right path, has attained it for his own good; and whoever goes astray – so say, “Indeed I am only a Herald of Warning.”

92.author: Arberry
92.text: and to recite the Koran. So whosoever is guided, is only guided to his own gain; and whosoever goes astray, say: 'I am naught but a warner.

92.author: Daryabadi
92.text: And that I should recite the Qur'an. And whosoever receiveth guidance, receiveth guidance for his own soul, and as for him who strayeth say thou: I am only of the warners.

92.author: Hilali & Khan
92.text: And to recite the Quran, so whosoever receives guidance, receives it for the good of his ownself, and whosoever goes astray, say (to him): "I am only one of the warners."

92.author: Itani
92.text: And to recite the Quran.” Whoever is guided—is guided to his own advantage. And whoever goes astray, then say, “I am one of the warners.”

92.author: Maududi
92.text: and to recite the Qur'an." So, whosoever is guided, his guidance will be to his own good. As for those who stray, tell them: "I am none but a warner."

92.author: Mubarakpuri
92.text: And that I should recite the Qur'an, then whosoever receives guidance, receives it for the good of himself; and whosoever goes astray, say (to him): "I am only one of the warners."

92.author: Pickthall
92.text: And to recite the Qur'an. And whoso goeth right, goeth right only for (the good of) his own soul; and as for him who goeth astray - (Unto him) say: Lo! I am only a warner.

92.author: Qarai
92.text: and to recite the Quran.’ Whoever is guided is guided only for his own good, and as for him who goes astray, say, ‘I am just one of the warners.’

92.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
92.text: and to recite the Koran. Whosoever is guided is only guided for himself, and to whosoever goes astray, say: 'I am only a warner'

92.author: Saheeh International
92.text: And to recite the Qur'an." And whoever is guided is only guided for [the benefit of] himself; and whoever strays - say, "I am only [one] of the warners."

92.author: Sarwar
92.text: and recite the Quran. Whoever seeks guidance will find it for his own soul. Say to whoever goes astray, "I am only a warner".

92.author: Shakir
92.text: And that I should recite the Quran. Therefore whoever goes aright, he goes aright for his own soul, and whoever goes ' astray, then say: I am only one of the warners.

92.author: Wahiduddin Khan
92.text: to recite the Quran." Whoever follows its guidance, follows it only for the good of his own soul; and as for him who goes astray, just say, "I am only a warner."

92.author: Yusuf Ali
92.text: And to rehearse the Qur'an: and if any accept guidance, they do it for the good of their own souls, and if any stray, say: "I am only a Warner".

92.author: Transliteration
92.text: Waan atluwa alqurana famani ihtada fainnama yahtadee linafsihi waman dalla faqul innama ana mina almunthireena

93.author: Ahmed Ali
93.text: Say: "All praise be to God. He will show you His signs, and you will recognise them. Your Lord is not heedless of what you do."

93.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
93.text: And proclaim, “All praise is to Allah – He will soon show you His signs so you will recognise them”; and (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him) your Lord is not unaware of what you, O people, do.”

93.author: Arberry
93.text: And say: 'Praise belongs to God. He shall show you His signs and you will recognise them. Thy Lord is not heedless of the things you do.'

93.author: Daryabadi
93.text: And say thou: All praise unto Allah! Anon He shall shew you His signs, so that ye will recognize them. And thy Lord is not negligent of that which ye Work.

93.author: Hilali & Khan
93.text: And say [(O Muhammad SAW) to these polytheists and pagans etc.]: "All the praises and thanks be to Allah. He will show you His Ayat (signs, in yourselves, and in the universe or punishments, etc.), and you shall recognise them. And your Lord is not unaware of what you do."

93.author: Itani
93.text: And say, “Praise belongs to God; He will show you His signs, and you will recognize them. Your Lord is not heedless of what you do.”

93.author: Maududi
93.text: And say: "All praise be to Allah, Who will soon show you His Signs that you will recognize." Your Lord is not unaware of what you do.

93.author: Mubarakpuri
93.text: And say: "All the praises and thanks be to Allah. He will show you His Ayat, and you shall recognize them. And your Lord is not unaware of what you do."

93.author: Pickthall
93.text: And say: Praise be to Allah Who will show you His portents so that ye shall know them. And thy Lord is not unaware of what ye (mortals) do.

93.author: Qarai
93.text: And say, ‘All praise belongs to Allah. Soon He will show you His signs, and you will recognize them.’ Your Lord is not oblivious of what you do.

93.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
93.text: And say: 'Praise belongs to Allah! He will show you His signs and you will recognize them. Your Lord is not inattentive of what you do'

93.author: Saheeh International
93.text: And say, "[All] praise is [due] to Allah. He will show you His signs, and you will recognize them. And your Lord is not unaware of what you do."

93.author: Sarwar
93.text: Say, "It is only He who deserves all praise. He will soon show you His signs and you will recognize them. Your Lord is not unaware of what you do.

93.author: Shakir
93.text: And say: Praise be to Allah, He will show you His signs so that you shall recognize them; nor is your Lord heedless of what you do.

93.author: Wahiduddin Khan
93.text: Then say, "Praise be to God! He will show you His signs and you will recognize them. Your Lord is not unaware of what you do."

93.author: Yusuf Ali
93.text: And say: "Praise be to Allah, Who will soon show you His Signs, so that ye shall know them"; and thy Lord is not unmindful of all that ye do.

93.author: Transliteration
93.text: Waquli alhamdu lillahi sayureekum ayatihi fataAArifoonaha wama rabbuka bighafilin AAamma taAAmaloona
